<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages**Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
5: ## Constraints
6: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)2. Prefer Java over Python when possible3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
7: ## 1. Project Structure
8: ```TrulyFreeOCR/├── build.gradle.kts # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)├── settings.gradle.kts # Project name├── gradlew # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)├── gradlew.bat # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)├── gradle/│ └── wrapper/ # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties├── README.md├── NOTICE.md # License attributions├── native/ # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)│ ├── win/│ │ └── jbig2enc.exe│ ├── linux/│ │ ├── jbig2enc│ │ ├── libtesseract.so # Bundled by bootstrap│ │ └── liblept.so│ └── mac/│ ├── jbig2enc│ ├── libtesseract.dylib # Bundled by bootstrap│ └── liblept.dylib├── jdk/ # OpenJDK 25 (installed by install-deps.sh)│ └── {os}/ # Platform-specific JDK archive├── tessdata/ # Tesseract language data files│ └── eng.traineddata├── install-deps.sh # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)├── bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat # End-user dependency installer└── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/ ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java # CLI entry point (picocli) ├── pipeline/ │ ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java # Coordinates all 6 steps │ ├── PageExtractor.java # Step 1: PDF page to images │ ├── ImageSegmenter.java # Step 2: lept4j normalization │ ├── JBIG2Compressor.java # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess │ ├── OCREngine.java # Step 4: Tess4J OCR │ ├── PDFAssembler.java # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly │ └── MetadataPreserver.java # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments ├── model/ │ ├── PageResult.java │ ├── TextBlock.java │ └── SegmentedImage.java └── util/ ├── PlatformUtils.java # OS detection, native paths ├── SubprocessRunner.java └── TempFileManager.java # Cleanup temp files```
9: ## 2. Self-Contained Dependencies Strategy
10: ### Gradle Dependencies (in build.gradle.kts)
11: Only 2 direct deps needed — Tess4J brings everything else transitively:
12: | Direct Dep | Transitive Deps It Brings | License ||---|---|---|| **`net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0`** | PDFBox 3.x, pdfbox-tools, jbig2-imageio, JNA, commons-io, **lept4j**, jboss-vfs, jai-imageio-core, slf4j-api | Apache 2.0 || **`info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6`** | (none) | Apache 2.0 |
13: ### Native/Bundled Dependencies
14: | Dep | Bundled How | License ||---|---|---|| **OpenJDK 25** | Downloaded by `install-deps.sh`/`bootstrap.sh` into `jdk/{os}/` | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception || **Gradle** | Auto-downloaded by Gradle Wrapper (`gradlew`) on first build | Apache 2.0 || **jbig2enc** | Pre-compiled binary in `native/{os}/jbig2enc` | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (Windows)** | Already bundled inside Tess4J JAR as DLLs | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (macOS/Linux)** | `.so`/`.dylib` in `native/{os}/` installed by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract language data** | `tessdata/eng.traineddata` downloaded by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
15: ### All License Checks Passed
16: | Dependency | License | Business-Friendly? ||---|---|---|| Tess4J | Apache 2.0 | || JNA | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 (dual) | || jai-imageio-core | BSD 3-clause | || PDFBox | Apache 2.0 | || pdfbox-tools | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2-imageio | Apache 2.0 | (decode only) || commons-io | Apache 2.0 | || lept4j | Apache 2.0 | || jboss-vfs | Apache 2.0 | || slf4j-api | MIT | || picocli | Apache 2.0 | || OpenJDK 25 | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | || Gradle | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | || Tesseract (native) | Apache 2.0 | || Leptonica (native) | BSD 2-clause | |
17: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
18: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
19: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)2. Download **OpenJDK 25** (Eclipse Temurin `25.0.3+9`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files (`gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, `gradle/wrapper/`) using bundled JDK: ```bash TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0 ```5. Remove temporary Gradle install6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`)7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
20: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
21: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
22: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 25** to `jdk/{os}/`2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped)
23: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
24: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
25: ```Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments) | v PageExtractor (Step 1) PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI | v ImageSegmenter (Step 2) lept4j (Leptonica via JNA): pixConvertRGBToGrayscale() pixBackgroundNorm() -> white background pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold() -> binarize Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG | ----+---- | | v vJBIG2Comp. OCREngine Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)(Step 3) (Step 4) | native | Tess4J jbig2enc| word-level bboxes glyph | dict | | | ----+---- | v PDFAssembler (Step 5) JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask + invisible OCR text | v MetadataPreserver (Step 6) Bookmarks, annotations, metadata from source PDF | v Output PDF (searchable, compressed, original structure preserved)```
26: ## 4. Component Details
27: ### PageExtractor (Step 1)- **Input**: PDDocument- **Process**: PDFBox PDFRenderer renders each page at 300 DPI -> BufferedImage- **Output**: List<PageImage> (one per page with dimensions)
28: ### ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — leptin instead of JavaCV/OpenCV- **Input**: BufferedImage -> Leptonica Pix- **Process**: - `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()` -> grayscale - `pixBackgroundNorm()` / `pixBackgroundNormMorph()` -> normalize BG to white - `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` / `pixSauvolaOnContrastNorm()` -> adaptive binarization - `pixThresholdToBinary()` -> text mask- **Output**: SegmentedImage (binary foreground mask + cleaned background)
29: ### JBIG2Compressor (Step 3)- **Process**: Write temp PNG -> invoke `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` -> read .jbig2 output- **Fallback**: CCITT Group 4 fax encoding (PDFBox native)
30: ### OCREngine (Step 4)- **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes- **Output**: PageResult with TextBlock(word, bbox, confidence) list
31: ### PDFAssembler (Step 5)- Per-page: PDPage -> JPEG background PDImageXObject -> JBIG2 mask with JBIG2Decode filter -> invisible text (rendering mode 3) at OCR bboxes
32: ### MetadataPreserver (Step 6)- Copy PDDocumentOutline (bookmarks), PDPageAnnotation (links/comments), PDDocumentInformation, XML metadata
33: ## 5. Build Configuration
34: ### Gradle (build.gradle.kts)
35: ```kotlinplugins { application id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "8.1.1"}
36: group = "com.trulyfreeocr"version = "1.0.0"
37: java { toolchain { languageVersion=JavaLanguageVersion.of(25) } }
38: repositories { mavenCentral() }
39: dependencies { implementation("net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0") implementation("info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6") annotationProcessor("info.picocli:picocli-codegen:4.7.6")}
40: application { mainClass="com.trulyfreeocr.TrulyFreeOCR" }
41: tasks { shadowJar { archiveBaseName.set("trulyfreeocr") archiveClassifier.set("") archiveVersion.set("") } build { dependsOn(shadowJar) }}```
42: ### Gradle Plugins- `com.github.johnrengelman.shadow` — fat JAR (replaces maven-shade-plugin)- `application` — run task with `mainClass`- `picocli-codegen` (annotationProcessor) — picocli command reflection
43: ## 6. Implementation Order
44: | Phase | Component | Notes ||---|---|---|| 1 | install-deps.sh + Gradle scaffold + CLI (picocli) | Foundation || 2 | PageExtractor | Simple PDFBox rendering || 3 | OCREngine | Tess4J — easiest to verify || 4 | ImageSegmenter | lept4j/Leptonica || 5 | PDFAssembler (basic: background + text) | Uncompressed first || 6 | JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner | Bundle native jbig2enc || 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Optimized compression || 8 | MetadataPreserver | Bookmarks, annotations || 9 | bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat | Self-contained setup || 10 | Integration tests + sample PDFs | Validation |
45: ## 7. Cross-Platform Native Support
46: | Component | Windows | Linux | macOS ||---|---|---|---|---|| OpenJDK 25 | `jdk/win/` | `jdk/linux/` | `jdk/mac/` || Gradle | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) || PDFBox (pure Java) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tess4J (JNA) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tesseract native | In Tess4J JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || Leptonica native | In lept4j JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || jbig2enc | `native/win/jbig2enc.exe` | `native/linux/jbig2enc` | `native/mac/jbig2enc` || Bootstrapper | `bootstrap.bat` | `bootstrap.sh` | `bootstrap.sh` |
47: ## 8. CLI Usage
48: ```bash# 0. Developer setup (one-time):./install-deps.sh # Downloads: OpenJDK 25, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper
49: # 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):# macOS:jdk/mac/jdk-25.0.3+9/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Linux:jdk/linux/jdk-25.0.3+9/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Windows:jdk\win\jdk-25.0.3+9\bin\java -jar gradle\wrapper\gradle-wrapper.jar build
50: # Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
51: # 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf
52: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \ --native-dir ./native \ --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \ -o output.pdf
53: # 3. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):./bootstrap.sh # or bootstrap.bat on Windows# Then same build/run commands as above```
54: ## 9. Key Risks & Mitigations
55: | Risk | Mitigation ||---|---|| **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 || **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version || **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure || **jbig2enc builds for all platforms** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux || **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |
56: ## 10. Key Findings from Dependency Verification
57: 1. **jbig2-imageio is read-only (decode only)** — confirmed from official Apache PDFBox README. Cannot encode JBIG2 in pure Java.
58: 2. **Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs** — from Tess4J's own documentation. macOS/Linux users must provide native Tesseract + Leptonica libraries. Bootstrap handles this.
59: 3. **lept4j can replace JavaCV entirely** — Tess4J already depends on lept4j (Leptonica JNA wrapper), which exports `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, `pixThresholdToBinary()`, etc. — exactly what Step 2 needs. Eliminates ~200MB JavaCV dependency.
60: 4. **All licenses verified** — every dependency (direct and transitive) is Apache 2.0, MIT, or BSD. No AGPL/GPL concerns.
61: ## 11. Legal Considerations for Automated Dependency Downloads
62: The install scripts (`install-deps.sh`, `bootstrap.sh`, Gradle Wrapper) download dependencies from Maven Central, Adoptium API, GitHub Releases, and system package managers without requiring users to visit each project's website.
63: ### Assessment
64: All dependencies use self-executing permissive licenses (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD, GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception) — these grant rights automatically upon use or distribution. No "I Accept" click or website visit is legally required.
65: | Dependency | Source | License | Concern? ||---|---|---|---|| Tess4J, picocli (via Gradle) | Maven Central | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Java practice || OpenJDK 25 | Adoptium API (`api.adoptium.net`) | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | None — API designed for automation || jbig2enc | GitHub Releases | Apache 2.0 | None — standard binary distribution || Tesseract/Leptonica | `apt` / `brew` | Apache 2.0 / BSD | None — package managers do this || tessdata | GitHub (Tesseract-OCR) | Apache 2.0 | None || Gradle (via Wrapper) | Gradle Services (`services.gradle.org`) | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Gradle practice |
66: ### "Stealing GitHub Stars" Consideration
67: A concern was raised: project owners rely on GitHub stars for visibility, and automated downloads skip visiting their repos.
68: **Why this is not a concern:**
69: 1. **Automated dependency resolution does not rob stars.** Stars are a human social signal — no project expects or receives stars from CI pipelines, package managers, or install scripts. Manually downloading a release zip and starring are unrelated actions.
70: 2. **Most dependencies never touch GitHub at all:** - Tess4J, picocli → Maven Central (not GitHub) - Gradle → `services.gradle.org` (not GitHub) - OpenJDK → Adoptium API (not GitHub) - Tesseract/Leptonica → `apt` / `brew` (not GitHub)
71: 3. **Only jbig2enc hits GitHub Releases** — the same pattern used by Homebrew, Docker, and every package manager that fetches release artifacts. Nobody considers those projects to be "stealing stars."
72: 4. **Universal industry standard.** Every Maven project, npm package, Homebrew formula, and Docker image works this way. If automated downloads were "stealing," the entire open-source ecosystem would be guilty.
73: **Optional nicety**: A credit line in the CLI output or README can drive awareness:```Powered by Tesseract OCR (github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract)```
74: **Verdict**: Not stealing. Stars come from human engagement, not `curl` scripts.
75: ### Mitigation
76: `NOTICE.md` will list every dependency with its exact version, license, and copyright notice to satisfy Apache 2.0 §4 and BSD attribution requirements.
77: ### Verdict
78: No legal concern for publishing on GitHub. Automated downloads from Maven Central, GitHub Releases, and package managers are standard industry practice used by every CI/CD pipeline and container build.
79:

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Let me analyze the Plan.md document thoroughly and evaluate its feasibility. The plan describes building a commercially-safe OCR pipeline in Java that takes PDFs with non-searchable pages and outputs searchable, highly-compressed PDFs.

Let me evaluate this plan from multiple angles:

1. **Technical Architecture** - Is the 6-step pipeline sound?
2. **Dependency Strategy** - Are the chosen libraries appropriate?
3. **Self-Contained Approach** - Can this really work cross-platform?
4. **Licensing** - Are there any hidden pitfalls?
5. **Implementation Risks** - What are the biggest challenges?

Let me think through each component:

### 1. PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → BufferedImage
- Feasible: Yes, PDFBox's PDFRenderer does this well. Standard approach.

### 2. lept4j for image segmentation
- Using `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()`, `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` - these are standard Leptonica operations that lept4j wraps.
- Leptonica's JNA wrapper via lept4j should work, but the API surface might not be fully exposed. Need to verify that lept4j actually exports these specific functions.
- RISK: lept4j may not expose all the Leptonica functions listed. The plan assumes they're available without verification.

### 3. JBIG2 compression via native jbig2enc
- This is the critical bottleneck. jbig2-imageio is decode-only (confirmed).
- Bundling native binaries per platform is a maintenance burden.
- CCITT G4 fallback is smart.
- RISK: Building jbig2enc for all platforms (especially Windows) is non-trivial. The binary needs to be pre-compiled and bundled.

### 4. Tess4J OCR
- Tess4J is well-established and works well.
- RISK: On macOS/Linux, the native Tesseract/Leptonica libraries must be installed separately. Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs.
- The bootstrap approach (apt-get on Linux, brew on macOS) is reasonable but requires sudo in some cases, and the library versions may differ from what Tess4J expects.

### 5. PDFAssembler with JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask
- PDFBox can create PDPages with custom content.
- JBIG2Decode filter in PDFBox - I believe PDFBox supports the JBIG2Decode filter for encoding. Need to verify.
- RISK: PDFBox may support decoding JBIG2 but not encoding it. The plan acknowledges this and bundles native jbig2enc.

### 6. MetadataPreserver
- PDFBox can handle PDDocumentOutline, PDPageAnnotation, etc. This is straightforward.

### Key Risks I See:

1. **Tesseract/Leptonica version compatibility on macOS/Linux**: When installing via apt/brew, the version installed may not match what Tess4J expects. This can lead to runtime `UnsatisfiedLinkError` or ABI mismatches. This is a significant risk.

2. **lept4j API coverage**: The plan assumes lept4j exposes `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, etc. But lept4j is a JNA wrapper and may not expose all Leptonica functions. This needs verification.

3. **JBIG2 embedding in PDF**: Taking raw JBIG2 data from jbig2enc and writing it as a PDF stream with JBIG2Decode filter requires understanding PDF internals for JBIG2. PDFBox's support for encoding JBIG2 may be limited. The plan mentions "JBIG2Decode filter" but this is read-side; writing may require direct PDF stream manipulation.

4. **Cross-platform native binary distribution**: jbig2enc must be compiled for x86_64 Linux, aarch64 Linux, x86_64 macOS, arm64 macOS, x86_64 Windows, and potentially aarch64 Windows. That's 6 builds. Also, native libraries for Tesseract/Leptonica must match the system's installed version.

5. **Gradle 9.6.0 with OpenJDK 25**: Gradle 9.6.0 and JDK 25 are very new (the plan says JDK 25.0.3+9). JDK 25 is not yet released in the current timeline (June 2026 - actually, looking at the date, it could be the early access build). This might work but could have compatibility issues.

6. **Shadow JAR size**: Tess4J + PDFBox + picocli + all transitive deps will produce a very large fat JAR. This is not a critical issue but worth noting.

7. **The `bootstrap.sh` approach requiring sudo**: On Linux, `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev` requires root. This breaks the "no host prerequisites" claim.

8. **TFOCR_JAVA_HOME approach**: The install scripts set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` but don't add it to the user's shell profile. The CLI usage docs show `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java` but the user would need to source the script or set it manually. This is a UX friction point.

9. **jbig2enc on macOS**: jbig2enc has dependencies (like leptonica) on macOS. A static build or careful linking is required.

### Overall Feasibility Assessment:

The plan is **technically feasible** but has **significant execution complexity** in several areas:

1. **HIGH feasibility**: PageExtractor (PDFBox), OCREngine (Tess4J), MetadataPreserver (PDFBox)
2. **MEDIUM feasibility**: ImageSegmenter (lept4j - depends on API coverage), Gradle scaffold, CLI
3. **LOW-MEDIUM feasibility**: JBIG2Compressor (native binary + PDF assembly), PDFAssembler with JBIG2 integration
4. **LOW feasibility**: bootstrap.sh approach (cross-platform native library compatibility is notoriously tricky)

The plan does a good job of identifying risks (#55 "Key Risks & Mitigations") but the mitigations are somewhat thin:
- "Pin version in bootstrap" for Tesseract version mismatch - but apt/brew don't always support pinning to specific versions
- "Pre-built binaries in repo" for jbig2enc - but doesn't address the build complexity for all platforms
- "Test with varied PDFs" for annotation coordinate mapping - vague

Let me also check the current state of the project to see what already exists.

Here is my feasibility evaluation of **Plan.md**:

---

## Overall Verdict: Feasible but with ⚠️ High-Risk Areas

The architecture is sound, the dependency choices are well-researched, and the license analysis is thorough (~80% of the plan is solid). However, there are **3 critical execution risks** the plan underestimates.

---

### ✅ HIGH Feasibility — Low Risk

| Component | Reason |
|---|---|
| **PageExtractor (Step 1)** | PDFBox `PDFRenderer` at 300 DPI is mature API, trivial |
| **Gradle Scaffold + CLI** | picocli + Shadow plugin are standard, well-documented |
| **MetadataPreserver (Step 6)** | PDFBox `PDDocumentOutline`, `PDPageAnnotation` APIs are straightforward |
| **Licensing Analysis** | Thorough; all deps are correctly classified as permissive |

### ⚠️ MEDIUM Feasibility — Some Risk

| Component | Reason |
|---|---|
| **OCREngine (Step 4)** | Tess4J works well on Windows; on macOS/Linux it requires matching native library versions. The plan's mitigation ("pin version in bootstrap") is weak — `apt-get` doesn't reliably install a specific Tesseract version. **Expect `UnsatisfiedLinkError` on first try.** |
| **ImageSegmenter (Step 2)** | lept4j is a JNA wrapper around Leptonica, but the plan **assumes** `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, etc. are exposed. lept4j's API surface is not fully documented — these functions may require direct JNA `NativeLibrary` calls. Worth prototyping first. |

### ❌ LOW Feasibility — High Risk

| Component | Reason |
|---|---|
| **JBIG2Compressor + PDFAssembler JBIG2 Integration** | This is the hardest part. Taking raw `.jbig2` output from `jbig2enc` and embedding it as a JBIG2Decode stream in a PDF requires **direct low-level PDF stream manipulation**. PDFBox does not have a high-level API for writing JBIG2-encoded images to PDF pages. You'll likely need to manually construct PDF filters and reference the global JBIG2 dictionary segment. The plan's "fallback CCITT G4" is wise but effectively concedes that JBIG2 output may not integrate. |
| **Cross-platform jbig2enc distribution** | Building jbig2enc for 6 architectures (x86_64/aarch64 × Win/Mac/Linux) with correct Leptonica linking is a significant DevOps task. macOS in particular is tricky — jbig2enc depends on Leptonica, requiring either static linking or bundling `.dylib` at a specific path. |
| **Self-contained bootstrap (bootstrap.sh)** | `apt-get install libtesseract-dev` **requires sudo**. This violates the "no host prerequisites" claim. macOS `brew` also requires Xcode CLI tools. True self-containment would need static builds of Tesseract + Leptonica for each platform. |

### 📌 Additional Issues

| Issue | Detail |
|---|---|
| **JDK 25 + Gradle 9.6.0** | JDK 25 is the very latest (likely early-access). Gradle 9.6.0 support for JDK 25 may be unstable. Recommend pinning to JDK 21 (LTS) to reduce compatibility risk. |
| **TFOCR_JAVA_HOME UX** | The plan shows `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar ...` but never adds this variable to `.bashrc`/`.zshrc`. After running bootstrap, the user must know to `source` it or use the absolute path. This will cause confusion. |
| **PDFBox JBIG2Decode encoding** | PDFBox ships `jbig2-imageio` which is decode-only as noted. Writing JBIG2 into a PDF as a compressed stream requires programmatic PDF stream construction (buffer with filter, etc.). Not impossible, but non-trivial. |
| **lept4j version verification** | The plan doesn't verify which lept4j version exposes the needed functions. Tess4J 5.18.0 transitively brings lept4j, but the available API depends on that version. |

---

### Recommended Changes

1. **Pin JDK to 21 LTS** — reduces compatibility risk; JDK 25 offers nothing essential for this pipeline.
2. **Add a `source` step to bootstrap** — or use `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` in a wrapper script so users don't need to set it manually.
3. **Build jbig2enc once, statically linked** — avoid system Leptonica dependency. Alpine-based Docker builds produce statically-linked binaries that work across Linux distros.
4. **Prototype ImageSegmenter early** (Phase 4) — before building the rest, confirm lept4j exposes the Leptonica functions you need. If not, plan to use JNA `NativeLibrary` directly or switch to a pure-Java binarization approach.
5. **Consider an alternative for Step 5 JBIG2** — instead of raw JBIG2 stream embedding, consider whether JPEG2000 (via PDFBox) or deflate compression alone is sufficient. JBIG2's value is primarily for very large documents with many pages of mostly-text.

## User

I am OK with 1 and 2. For 3, 4, and 5, is it possible to keep the options.

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

The user is responding to my recommended changes from the previous message. They're saying they're OK with recommendations 1 (pin JDK to 21 LTS) and 2 (add a source step to bootstrap), and for recommendations 3, 4, and 5, they want to know if they can keep the options (meaning keep the current approach as described in the plan, but with the alternatives available as fallbacks).

Let me re-read my recommendations:

3. **Build jbig2enc once, statically linked** — avoid system Leptonica dependency. Alpine-based Docker builds produce statically-linked binaries that work across Linux distros.
4. **Prototype ImageSegmenter early** (Phase 4) — before building the rest, confirm lept4j exposes the Leptonica functions you need. If not, plan to use JNA `NativeLibrary` directly or switch to a pure-Java binarization approach.
5. **Consider an alternative for Step 5 JBIG2** — instead of raw JBIG2 stream embedding, consider whether JPEG2000 (via PDFBox) or deflate compression alone is sufficient.

The user is asking: "is it possible to keep the options" — meaning, can the plan preserve the original approach while the alternatives/fallbacks remain as options that could be exercised later if needed.

For #3: Yes, the plan's original approach of bundling pre-compiled jbig2enc binaries can be kept, with the option to switch to statically-linked builds if dynamic linking becomes problematic.

For #4: Yes, the plan can proceed with the lept4j approach as specified, and if specific functions aren't exposed, the option exists to use JNA NativeLibrary directly or implement pure-Java binarization.

For #5: Yes, the plan can keep JBIG2 encoding via native jbig2enc as the primary approach, with CCITT G4 already documented as a fallback, and JPEG2000/deflate as additional options.

So the answer to all three is yes — each recommendation was about having an option/fallback, not about changing the primary approach. The user wants to keep the primary approach and have the options available if needed.

Let me clarify this and confirm the implementation plan adjustments.

</details>

Yes, all three are "keep the option" recommendations — they don't change the primary approach, they just add contingency paths if the primary hits a wall. Here's how that works out:

| # | Primary (as in Plan.md) | Option Kept If... |
|---|---|---|
| **3** | Bundle `jbig2enc` as pre-compiled binary per platform (existing approach) | If dynamic-linking issues arise across Linux distros, switch to **statically-linked jbig2enc** (built via Alpine Docker). No structural change to the pipeline. |
| **4** | Use `lept4j` for Leptonica segmentation (existing approach) | If a needed function isn't exposed by `lept4j`, fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding, or pure-Java binarization. The `ImageSegmenter` interface stays the same; only the implementation changes. |
| **5** | JBIG2 encoding via native `jbig2enc` (existing approach) | If embedding raw JBIG2 in PDF proves infeasible, fall back to **CCITT G4** (already documented) or **deflate**. The PDFAssembler interface stays the same. |

**Bottom line**: The implementation order stays per §6 of Plan.md. Phases 6–7 (JBIG2) and Phase 4 (ImageSegmenter) proceed as specified; if they encounter issues, the alternative path is exercised at that point without replanning. No need to pre-commit to any alternative now.

Shall I formalize these as inline notes in Plan.md (e.g., bullet additions under §9 Key Risks & Mitigations) and add the JDK 21 + TFOCR_JAVA_HOME changes to keep the document up to date, or would you like me to proceed directly to implementing Phase 1?

  Line 264:     /** The maximum number of pods that can be scheduled above the desired number of pods. Value can be an absolute number (ex: 5) or a percentage of desired pods (ex: 10%). This can not be 0 if MaxUnavailable is 0. Absolute number is calculated from percentage by rounding up. Defaults to 25%. Example: when this is set to 30%, the new ReplicaSet can be scaled up immediately when the rolling update starts, such that the total number of old and new pods do not exceed 130% of desired pods. Once old pods have been killed, new ReplicaSet can be scaled up further, ensuring that total number of pods running at any time during the update is at most 130% of desired pods. */

Example: when this is set to 30%, the old ReplicaSet can be scaled down to 70% of desired pods immediately when the rolling update starts. /home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/.opencode/node_modules/msgpackr-extract/README.md:
  Line 2: This module is designed to do fast and efficient native/C-level extraction of strings from MessagePack binary data. This works by calling `extractStrings(buffer, start, end)`, and it will extract strings by doing partial MessagePack parsing, and scanning to find the string data in the range specified in the buffer. It will return an array of strings that it finds. When it finds strings that can be represented with latin-1/one-byte strings (and important V8 optimization), it will attempt return a continuous string of MessagePack data that contains multiple sub-strings, so the decoder can slice off strings by offset. When a string contains non-latin characters, and must be represented as a two-byte string, this will always be returned as the string alone without combination with any other strings. The extractor will return an array of a maximum of 256 strings. The decoder can call the extractStrings again, with a new offset to continue extracting more strings as necessary.

<path>/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/Plan.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages**Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
5: ## Constraints
6: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)2. Prefer Java over Python when possible3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
7: ## 1. Project Structure
8: ```TrulyFreeOCR/├── build.gradle.kts # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)├── settings.gradle.kts # Project name├── gradlew # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)├── gradlew.bat # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)├── gradle/│ └── wrapper/ # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties├── README.md├── NOTICE.md # License attributions├── native/ # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)│ ├── win/│ │ └── jbig2enc.exe│ ├── linux/│ │ ├── jbig2enc│ │ ├── libtesseract.so # Bundled by bootstrap│ │ └── liblept.so│ └── mac/│ ├── jbig2enc│ ├── libtesseract.dylib # Bundled by bootstrap│ └── liblept.dylib├── jdk/ # OpenJDK 21 LTS (installed by install-deps.sh)│ └── {os}/ # Platform-specific JDK archive├── tessdata/ # Tesseract language data files│ └── eng.traineddata├── install-deps.sh # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)├── bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat # End-user dependency installer└── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/ ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java # CLI entry point (picocli) ├── pipeline/ │ ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java # Coordinates all 6 steps │ ├── PageExtractor.java # Step 1: PDF page to images │ ├── ImageSegmenter.java # Step 2: lept4j normalization │ ├── JBIG2Compressor.java # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess │ ├── OCREngine.java # Step 4: Tess4J OCR │ ├── PDFAssembler.java # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly │ └── MetadataPreserver.java # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments ├── model/ │ ├── PageResult.java │ ├── TextBlock.java │ └── SegmentedImage.java └── util/ ├── PlatformUtils.java # OS detection, native paths ├── SubprocessRunner.java └── TempFileManager.java # Cleanup temp files```
9: ## 2. Self-Contained Dependencies Strategy
10: ### Gradle Dependencies (in build.gradle.kts)
11: Only 2 direct deps needed — Tess4J brings everything else transitively:
12: | Direct Dep | Transitive Deps It Brings | License ||---|---|---|| **`net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0`** | PDFBox 3.x, pdfbox-tools, jbig2-imageio, JNA, commons-io, **lept4j**, jboss-vfs, jai-imageio-core, slf4j-api | Apache 2.0 || **`info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6`** | (none) | Apache 2.0 |
13: ### Native/Bundled Dependencies
14: | Dep | Bundled How | License ||---|---|---|| **OpenJDK 21 LTS** | Downloaded by `install-deps.sh`/`bootstrap.sh` into `jdk/{os}/` | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception || **Gradle** | Auto-downloaded by Gradle Wrapper (`gradlew`) on first build | Apache 2.0 || **jbig2enc** | Pre-compiled binary in `native/{os}/jbig2enc` | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (Windows)** | Already bundled inside Tess4J JAR as DLLs | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (macOS/Linux)** | `.so`/`.dylib` in `native/{os}/` installed by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract language data** | `tessdata/eng.traineddata` downloaded by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
15: ### All License Checks Passed
16: | Dependency | License | Business-Friendly? ||---|---|---|| Tess4J | Apache 2.0 | || JNA | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 (dual) | || jai-imageio-core | BSD 3-clause | || PDFBox | Apache 2.0 | || pdfbox-tools | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2-imageio | Apache 2.0 | (decode only) || commons-io | Apache 2.0 | || lept4j | Apache 2.0 | || jboss-vfs | Apache 2.0 | || slf4j-api | MIT | || picocli | Apache 2.0 | || OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | || Gradle | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | || Tesseract (native) | Apache 2.0 | || Leptonica (native) | BSD 2-clause | |
17: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
18: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
19: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)2. Download **OpenJDK 21 LTS** (Eclipse Temurin `21.0.x`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files (`gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, `gradle/wrapper/`) using bundled JDK: ```bash TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0 ```5. Remove temporary Gradle install6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`)7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
20: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
21: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
22: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped)
23: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
24: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
25: ```Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments) | v PageExtractor (Step 1) PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI | v ImageSegmenter (Step 2) lept4j (Leptonica via JNA): pixConvertRGBToGrayscale() pixBackgroundNorm() -> white background pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold() -> binarize Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG | ----+---- | | v vJBIG2Comp. OCREngine Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)(Step 3) (Step 4) | native | Tess4J jbig2enc| word-level bboxes glyph | dict | | | ----+---- | v PDFAssembler (Step 5) JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask + invisible OCR text | v MetadataPreserver (Step 6) Bookmarks, annotations, metadata from source PDF | v Output PDF (searchable, compressed, original structure preserved)```
26: ## 4. Component Details
27: ### PageExtractor (Step 1)- **Input**: PDDocument- **Process**: PDFBox PDFRenderer renders each page at 300 DPI -> BufferedImage- **Output**: List<PageImage> (one per page with dimensions)
28: ### ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — leptin instead of JavaCV/OpenCV- **Input**: BufferedImage -> Leptonica Pix- **Process**: - `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()` -> grayscale - `pixBackgroundNorm()` / `pixBackgroundNormMorph()` -> normalize BG to white - `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` / `pixSauvolaOnContrastNorm()` -> adaptive binarization - `pixThresholdToBinary()` -> text mask- **Output**: SegmentedImage (binary foreground mask + cleaned background)
29: ### JBIG2Compressor (Step 3)- **Process**: Write temp PNG -> invoke `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` -> read .jbig2 output- **Fallback**: CCITT Group 4 fax encoding (PDFBox native)
30: ### OCREngine (Step 4)- **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes- **Output**: PageResult with TextBlock(word, bbox, confidence) list
31: ### PDFAssembler (Step 5)- Per-page: PDPage -> JPEG background PDImageXObject -> JBIG2 mask with JBIG2Decode filter -> invisible text (rendering mode 3) at OCR bboxes
32: ### MetadataPreserver (Step 6)- Copy PDDocumentOutline (bookmarks), PDPageAnnotation (links/comments), PDDocumentInformation, XML metadata
33: ## 5. Build Configuration
34: ### Gradle (build.gradle.kts)
35: ```kotlinplugins { application id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "8.1.1"}
36: group = "com.trulyfreeocr"version = "1.0.0"
37: java { toolchain { languageVersion=JavaLanguageVersion.of(21) } }
38: repositories { mavenCentral() }
39: dependencies { implementation("net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0") implementation("info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6") annotationProcessor("info.picocli:picocli-codegen:4.7.6")}
40: application { mainClass="com.trulyfreeocr.TrulyFreeOCR" }
41: tasks { shadowJar { archiveBaseName.set("trulyfreeocr") archiveClassifier.set("") archiveVersion.set("") } build { dependsOn(shadowJar) }}```
42: ### Gradle Plugins- `com.github.johnrengelman.shadow` — fat JAR (replaces maven-shade-plugin)- `application` — run task with `mainClass`- `picocli-codegen` (annotationProcessor) — picocli command reflection
43: ## 6. Implementation Order
44: | Phase | Component | Notes ||---|---|---|| 1 | install-deps.sh + Gradle scaffold + CLI (picocli) | Foundation || 2 | PageExtractor | Simple PDFBox rendering || 3 | OCREngine | Tess4J — easiest to verify || 4 | ImageSegmenter | lept4j/Leptonica || 5 | PDFAssembler (basic: background + text) | Uncompressed first || 6 | JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner | Bundle native jbig2enc || 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Optimized compression || 8 | MetadataPreserver | Bookmarks, annotations || 9 | bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat | Self-contained setup || 10 | Integration tests + sample PDFs | Validation |
45: ## 7. Cross-Platform Native Support
46: | Component | Windows | Linux | macOS ||---|---|---|---|---|| OpenJDK 21 LTS | `jdk/win/` | `jdk/linux/` | `jdk/mac/` || Gradle | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) || PDFBox (pure Java) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tess4J (JNA) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tesseract native | In Tess4J JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || Leptonica native | In lept4j JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || jbig2enc | `native/win/jbig2enc.exe` | `native/linux/jbig2enc` | `native/mac/jbig2enc` || Bootstrapper | `bootstrap.bat` | `bootstrap.sh` | `bootstrap.sh` |
47: ## 8. CLI Usage
48: ```bash# 0. Developer setup (one-time):./install-deps.sh # Downloads: OpenJDK 21 LTS, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper
49: # 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):# macOS:jdk/mac/jdk-21.0.x/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Linux:jdk/linux/jdk-21.0.x/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Windows:jdk\win\jdk-21.0.x\bin\java -jar gradle\wrapper\gradle-wrapper.jar build
50: # Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
51: # 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf
52: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \ --native-dir ./native \ --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \ -o output.pdf
53: # 3. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):./bootstrap.sh # or bootstrap.bat on Windows# Then same build/run commands as above```
54: ## 9. Key Risks & Mitigations
55: | Risk | Mitigation ||---|---|| **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 || **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version || **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure || **jbig2enc builds for all platforms** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux || **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |
56: ## 10. Key Findings from Dependency Verification
57: 1. **jbig2-imageio is read-only (decode only)** — confirmed from official Apache PDFBox README. Cannot encode JBIG2 in pure Java.
58: 2. **Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs** — from Tess4J's own documentation. macOS/Linux users must provide native Tesseract + Leptonica libraries. Bootstrap handles this.
59: 3. **lept4j can replace JavaCV entirely** — Tess4J already depends on lept4j (Leptonica JNA wrapper), which exports `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, `pixThresholdToBinary()`, etc. — exactly what Step 2 needs. Eliminates ~200MB JavaCV dependency.
60: 4. **All licenses verified** — every dependency (direct and transitive) is Apache 2.0, MIT, or BSD. No AGPL/GPL concerns.
61: ## 11. Legal Considerations for Automated Dependency Downloads
62: The install scripts (`install-deps.sh`, `bootstrap.sh`, Gradle Wrapper) download dependencies from Maven Central, Adoptium API, GitHub Releases, and system package managers without requiring users to visit each project's website.
63: ### Assessment
64: All dependencies use self-executing permissive licenses (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD, GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception) — these grant rights automatically upon use or distribution. No "I Accept" click or website visit is legally required.
65: | Dependency | Source | License | Concern? ||---|---|---|---|| Tess4J, picocli (via Gradle) | Maven Central | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Java practice || OpenJDK 21 LTS | Adoptium API (`api.adoptium.net`) | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | None — API designed for automation || jbig2enc | GitHub Releases | Apache 2.0 | None — standard binary distribution || Tesseract/Leptonica | `apt` / `brew` | Apache 2.0 / BSD | None — package managers do this || tessdata | GitHub (Tesseract-OCR) | Apache 2.0 | None || Gradle (via Wrapper) | Gradle Services (`services.gradle.org`) | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Gradle practice |
66: ### "Stealing GitHub Stars" Consideration
67: A concern was raised: project owners rely on GitHub stars for visibility, and automated downloads skip visiting their repos.
68: **Why this is not a concern:**
69: 1. **Automated dependency resolution does not rob stars.** Stars are a human social signal — no project expects or receives stars from CI pipelines, package managers, or install scripts. Manually downloading a release zip and starring are unrelated actions.
70: 2. **Most dependencies never touch GitHub at all:** - Tess4J, picocli → Maven Central (not GitHub) - Gradle → `services.gradle.org` (not GitHub) - OpenJDK → Adoptium API (not GitHub) - Tesseract/Leptonica → `apt` / `brew` (not GitHub)
71: 3. **Only jbig2enc hits GitHub Releases** — the same pattern used by Homebrew, Docker, and every package manager that fetches release artifacts. Nobody considers those projects to be "stealing stars."
72: 4. **Universal industry standard.** Every Maven project, npm package, Homebrew formula, and Docker image works this way. If automated downloads were "stealing," the entire open-source ecosystem would be guilty.
73: **Optional nicety**: A credit line in the CLI output or README can drive awareness:```Powered by Tesseract OCR (github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract)```
74: **Verdict**: Not stealing. Stars come from human engagement, not `curl` scripts.
75: ### Mitigation
76: `NOTICE.md` will list every dependency with its exact version, license, and copyright notice to satisfy Apache 2.0 §4 and BSD attribution requirements.
77: ### Verdict
78: No legal concern for publishing on GitHub. Automated downloads from Maven Central, GitHub Releases, and package managers are standard industry practice used by every CI/CD pipeline and container build.
79: 
80: 

(End of file - total 80 lines)
</content>
```

21: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
22: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform3. 23: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.

17: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
18: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
19: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)2. Download **OpenJDK 21 LTS** (Eclipse Temurin `21.0.x`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files (`gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, `gradle/wrapper/`) using bundled JDK: ```bash TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0 ```5. Remove temporary Gradle install6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`)7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
20: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
21: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
22: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped) and creates `run.sh` / `run.bat` wrapper scripts that source it automatically — users never set it manually.
23: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` + `run.sh`/`run.bat` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.

(Showing lines 17-23 of 81. Use offset=24 to continue.)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages**Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
5: ## Constraints
6: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)2. Prefer Java over Python when possible3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
7: ## 1. Project Structure
8: ```
9: TrulyFreeOCR/
10: ├── build.gradle.kts            # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)
11: ├── settings.gradle.kts          # Project name
12: ├── gradlew                      # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)
13: ├── gradlew.bat                  # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)
14: ├── gradle/
15: │   └── wrapper/                 # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties
16: ├── README.md
17: ├── NOTICE.md                    # License attributions
18: ├── native/                      # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)
19: │   ├── win/
20: │   │   └── jbig2enc.exe
21: │   ├── linux/
22: │   │   ├── jbig2enc
23: │   │   ├── libtesseract.so      # Bundled by bootstrap
24: │   │   └── liblept.so
25: │   └── mac/
26: │       ├── jbig2enc
27: │       ├── libtesseract.dylib   # Bundled by bootstrap
28: │       └── liblept.dylib
29: ├── jdk/                         # OpenJDK 21 LTS (installed by install-deps.sh)
30: │   └── {os}/                    # Platform-specific JDK archive
31: ├── tessdata/                    # Tesseract language data files
32: │   └── eng.traineddata
33: ├── install-deps.sh              # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)
34: ├── run.sh                       # Convenience wrapper (Unix)
35: ├── run.bat                      # Convenience wrapper (Windows)
36: ├── bootstrap.sh                 # End-user dependency installer (Unix)
37: ├── bootstrap.bat                # End-user dependency installer (Windows)
38: └── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/
39:     ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java        # CLI entry point (picocli)
40:     ├── pipeline/
41:     │   ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java  # Coordinates all 6 steps
42:     │   ├── PageExtractor.java         # Step 1: PDF page to images
43:     │   ├── ImageSegmenter.java        # Step 2: lept4j normalization
44:     │   ├── JBIG2Compressor.java       # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess
45:     │   ├── OCREngine.java             # Step 4: Tess4J OCR
46:     │   ├── PDFAssembler.java          # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly
47:     │   └── MetadataPreserver.java     # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments
48:     ├── model/
49:     │   ├── PageResult.java
50:     │   ├── TextBlock.java
51:     │   └── SegmentedImage.java
52:     └── util/
53:         ├── PlatformUtils.java         # OS detection, native paths
54:         ├── SubprocessRunner.java
55:         └── TempFileManager.java       # Cleanup temp files
56: ```
57: ## 2. Self-Contained Dependencies Strategy
58: ### Gradle Dependencies (in build.gradle.kts)
59: Only 2 direct deps needed — Tess4J brings everything else transitively:
60: | Direct Dep | Transitive Deps It Brings | License ||---|---|---|| **`net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0`** | PDFBox 3.x, pdfbox-tools, jbig2-imageio, JNA, commons-io, **lept4j**, jboss-vfs, jai-imageio-core, slf4j-api | Apache 2.0 || **`info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6`** | (none) | Apache 2.0 |
61: ### Native/Bundled Dependencies
62: | Dep | Bundled How | License ||---|---|---|| **OpenJDK 21 LTS** | Downloaded by `install-deps.sh`/`bootstrap.sh` into `jdk/{os}/` | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception || **Gradle** | Auto-downloaded by Gradle Wrapper (`gradlew`) on first build | Apache 2.0 || **jbig2enc** | Pre-compiled binary in `native/{os}/jbig2enc` | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (Windows)** | Already bundled inside Tess4J JAR as DLLs | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract + Leptonica (macOS/Linux)** | `.so`/`.dylib` in `native/{os}/` installed by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 || **Tesseract language data** | `tessdata/eng.traineddata` downloaded by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
63: ### All License Checks Passed
64: | Dependency | License | Business-Friendly? ||---|---|---|| Tess4J | Apache 2.0 | || JNA | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 (dual) | || jai-imageio-core | BSD 3-clause | || PDFBox | Apache 2.0 | || pdfbox-tools | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2-imageio | Apache 2.0 | (decode only) || commons-io | Apache 2.0 | || lept4j | Apache 2.0 | || jboss-vfs | Apache 2.0 | || slf4j-api | MIT | || picocli | Apache 2.0 | || OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | || Gradle | Apache 2.0 | || jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | || Tesseract (native) | Apache 2.0 | || Leptonica (native) | BSD 2-clause | |
65: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
66: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
67: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)2. Download **OpenJDK 21 LTS** (Eclipse Temurin `21.0.x`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files (`gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, `gradle/wrapper/`) using bundled JDK: ```bash TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0 ```5. Remove temporary Gradle install6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`) and generate `run.sh` / `run.bat` convenience wrappers7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
68: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
69: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
70: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped) and creates `run.sh` / `run.bat` wrapper scripts that source it automatically — users never set it manually.
71: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` + `run.sh`/`run.bat` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
72: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
73: ```Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments) | v PageExtractor (Step 1) PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI | v ImageSegmenter (Step 2) lept4j (Leptonica via JNA): pixConvertRGBToGrayscale() pixBackgroundNorm() -> white background pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold() -> binarize Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG | ----+---- | | v vJBIG2Comp. OCREngine Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)(Step 3) (Step 4) | native | Tess4J jbig2enc| word-level bboxes glyph | dict | | | ----+---- | v PDFAssembler (Step 5) JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask + invisible OCR text | v MetadataPreserver (Step 6) Bookmarks, annotations, metadata from source PDF | v Output PDF (searchable, compressed, original structure preserved)```
74: ## 4. Component Details
75: ### PageExtractor (Step 1)- **Input**: PDDocument- **Process**: PDFBox PDFRenderer renders each page at 300 DPI -> BufferedImage- **Output**: List<PageImage> (one per page with dimensions)
76: ### ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — leptin instead of JavaCV/OpenCV- **Input**: BufferedImage -> Leptonica Pix- **Process**: - `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()` -> grayscale - `pixBackgroundNorm()` / `pixBackgroundNormMorph()` -> normalize BG to white - `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` / `pixSauvolaOnContrastNorm()` -> adaptive binarization - `pixThresholdToBinary()` -> text mask- **Output**: SegmentedImage (binary foreground mask + cleaned background)
77: ### JBIG2Compressor (Step 3)- **Process**: Write temp PNG -> invoke `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` -> read .jbig2 output- **Fallback**: CCITT Group 4 fax encoding (PDFBox native)
78: ### OCREngine (Step 4)- **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes- **Output**: PageResult with TextBlock(word, bbox, confidence) list
79: ### PDFAssembler (Step 5)- Per-page: PDPage -> JPEG background PDImageXObject -> JBIG2 mask with JBIG2Decode filter -> invisible text (rendering mode 3) at OCR bboxes
80: ### MetadataPreserver (Step 6)- Copy PDDocumentOutline (bookmarks), PDPageAnnotation (links/comments), PDDocumentInformation, XML metadata
81: ## 5. Build Configuration
82: ### Gradle (build.gradle.kts)
83: ```kotlinplugins { application id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "8.1.1"}
84: group = "com.trulyfreeocr"version = "1.0.0"
85: java { toolchain { languageVersion=JavaLanguageVersion.of(21) } }
86: repositories { mavenCentral() }
87: dependencies { implementation("net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0") implementation("info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6") annotationProcessor("info.picocli:picocli-codegen:4.7.6")}
88: application { mainClass="com.trulyfreeocr.TrulyFreeOCR" }
89: tasks { shadowJar { archiveBaseName.set("trulyfreeocr") archiveClassifier.set("") archiveVersion.set("") } build { dependsOn(shadowJar) }}```
90: ### Gradle Plugins- `com.github.johnrengelman.shadow` — fat JAR (replaces maven-shade-plugin)- `application` — run task with `mainClass`- `picocli-codegen` (annotationProcessor) — picocli command reflection
91: ## 6. Implementation Order
92: | Phase | Component | Notes ||---|---|---|| 1 | install-deps.sh + Gradle scaffold + CLI (picocli) | Foundation || 2 | PageExtractor | Simple PDFBox rendering || 3 | OCREngine | Tess4J — easiest to verify || 4 | ImageSegmenter | lept4j/Leptonica || 5 | PDFAssembler (basic: background + text) | Uncompressed first || 6 | JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner | Bundle native jbig2enc || 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Optimized compression || 8 | MetadataPreserver | Bookmarks, annotations || 9 | bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat | Self-contained setup || 10 | Integration tests + sample PDFs | Validation |
93: ## 7. Cross-Platform Native Support
94: | Component | Windows | Linux | macOS ||---|---|---|---|---|| OpenJDK 21 LTS | `jdk/win/` | `jdk/linux/` | `jdk/mac/` || Gradle | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) || PDFBox (pure Java) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tess4J (JNA) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR || Tesseract native | In Tess4J JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || Leptonica native | In lept4j JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` || jbig2enc | `native/win/jbig2enc.exe` | `native/linux/jbig2enc` | `native/mac/jbig2enc` || Bootstrapper | `bootstrap.bat` | `bootstrap.sh` | `bootstrap.sh` |
95: ## 8. CLI Usage
96: ```bash# 0. Developer setup (one-time):./install-deps.sh # Downloads: OpenJDK 21 LTS, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper
97: # 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):# macOS:jdk/mac/jdk-21.0.x/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Linux:jdk/linux/jdk-21.0.x/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build# Windows:jdk\win\jdk-21.0.x\bin\java -jar gradle\wrapper\gradle-wrapper.jar build
98: # Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
99: # 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf
100: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \ --native-dir ./native \ --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \ -o output.pdf
101: # 3. Convenience wrapper (sets TFOCR_JAVA_HOME automatically):./run.sh input.pdf -o output.pdf # Unix./run.bat input.pdf -o output.pdf # Windows
102: # 4. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):./bootstrap.sh # or bootstrap.bat on Windows# Then use run.sh / run.bat for build and execution```
103: ## 9. Key Risks & Mitigations
104: | Risk | Mitigation ||---|---|| **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 or deflate || **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version || **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure || **jbig2enc cross-platform binary compatibility** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux. **Option**: use statically-linked jbig2enc (Alpine Docker build) to avoid dynamic-linking issues across Linux distros || **lept4j API coverage uncertainty** — `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` may not be exposed | Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding; or pure-Java binarization. ImageSegmenter interface stays the same || **JBIG2 PDF stream embedding complexity** — PDFBox has no high-level API to embed raw jbig2 data with JBIG2Decode filter | Primary: construct PDF stream manually. **Option**: fall back to CCITT G4 fax encoding (PDFBox native) or deflate compression. PDFAssembler interface stays the same || **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |
105: ## 10. Key Findings from Dependency Verification
106: 1. **jbig2-imageio is read-only (decode only)** — confirmed from official Apache PDFBox README. Cannot encode JBIG2 in pure Java.
107: 2. **Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs** — from Tess4J's own documentation. macOS/Linux users must provide native Tesseract + Leptonica libraries. Bootstrap handles this.
108: 3. **lept4j can replace JavaCV entirely** — Tess4J already depends on lept4j (Leptonica JNA wrapper), which exports `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, `pixThresholdToBinary()`, etc. — exactly what Step 2 needs. Eliminates ~200MB JavaCV dependency.
109: 4. **All licenses verified** — every dependency (direct and transitive) is Apache 2.0, MIT, or BSD. No AGPL/GPL concerns.
110: ## 11. Legal Considerations for Automated Dependency Downloads
111: The install scripts (`install-deps.sh`, `bootstrap.sh`, Gradle Wrapper) download dependencies from Maven Central, Adoptium API, GitHub Releases, and system package managers without requiring users to visit each project's website.
112: ### Assessment
113: All dependencies use self-executing permissive licenses (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD, GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception) — these grant rights automatically upon use or distribution. No "I Accept" click or website visit is legally required.
114: | Dependency | Source | License | Concern? ||---|---|---|---|| Tess4J, picocli (via Gradle) | Maven Central | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Java practice || OpenJDK 21 LTS | Adoptium API (`api.adoptium.net`) | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | None — API designed for automation || jbig2enc | GitHub Releases | Apache 2.0 | None — standard binary distribution || Tesseract/Leptonica | `apt` / `brew` | Apache 2.0 / BSD | None — package managers do this || tessdata | GitHub (Tesseract-OCR) | Apache 2.0 | None || Gradle (via Wrapper) | Gradle Services (`services.gradle.org`) | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Gradle practice |
115: ### "Stealing GitHub Stars" Consideration
116: A concern was raised: project owners rely on GitHub stars for visibility, and automated downloads skip visiting their repos.
117: **Why this is not a concern:**
118: 1. **Automated dependency resolution does not rob stars.** Stars are a human social signal — no project expects or receives stars from CI pipelines, package managers, or install scripts. Manually downloading a release zip and starring are unrelated actions.
119: 2. **Most dependencies never touch GitHub at all:** - Tess4J, picocli → Maven Central (not GitHub) - Gradle → `services.gradle.org` (not GitHub) - OpenJDK → Adoptium API (not GitHub) - Tesseract/Leptonica → `apt` / `brew` (not GitHub)
120: 3. **Only jbig2enc hits GitHub Releases** — the same pattern used by Homebrew, Docker, and every package manager that fetches release artifacts. Nobody considers those projects to be "stealing stars."
121: 4. **Universal industry standard.** Every Maven project, npm package, Homebrew formula, and Docker image works this way. If automated downloads were "stealing," the entire open-source ecosystem would be guilty.
122: **Optional nicety**: A credit line in the CLI output or README can drive awareness:```Powered by Tesseract OCR (github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract)```
123: **Verdict**: Not stealing. Stars come from human engagement, not `curl` scripts.
124: ### Mitigation
125: `NOTICE.md` will list every dependency with its exact version, license, and copyright notice to satisfy Apache 2.0 §4 and BSD attribution requirements.
126: ### Verdict
127: No legal concern for publishing on GitHub. Automated downloads from Maven Central, GitHub Releases, and package managers are standard industry practice used by every CI/CD pipeline and container build.
128: 
129: 

(End of file - total 129 lines)
</content>
```

Run (uses bundled JDK):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf\n99: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \\ --native-dir ./native \\ --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \\ -o output.pdf\n100: # 3.   "newString": "```bash\n# 0. Developer setup (one-time):\n./install-deps.sh    # Downloads: OpenJDK 21 LTS, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper\n\n# 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):\n# macOS:\njdk/mac/jdk-21.0.x/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build\n# Linux:\njdk/linux/jdk-21.0.x/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build\n# Windows:\njdk\\win\\jdk-21.0.x\\bin\\java -jar gradle\\wrapper\\gradle-wrapper.jar build\n\n# Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build\n\n# 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf\n\n# With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \\\n    --native-dir ./native \\\n    --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \\\n    -o output.pdf\n\n# 3. Convenience wrapper (sets TFOCR_JAVA_HOME automatically):\n./run.sh input.pdf -o output.pdf         # Unix\n./run.bat input.pdf -o output.pdf        # Windows\n\n# 4. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):\n./bootstrap.sh                            # Unix\nbootstrap.bat                            # Windows\n# Then use run.sh / run.bat for build and execution\n```"
}

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/Plan.md",
  "oldString": "| Risk | Mitigation ||---|---|| **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 or deflate || **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version || **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure || **jbig2enc cross-platform binary compatibility** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux. **Option**: use statically-linked jbig2enc (Alpine Docker build) to avoid dynamic-linking issues across Linux distros || **lept4j API coverage uncertainty** — `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` may not be exposed | Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding; or pure-Java binarization. ImageSegmenter interface stays the same || **JBIG2 PDF stream embedding complexity** — PDFBox has no high-level API to embed raw jbig2 data with JBIG2Decode filter | Primary: construct PDF stream manually. **Option**: fall back to CCITT G4 fax encoding (PDFBox native) or deflate compression. PDFAssembler interface stays the same || **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |",
  "newString": "| Risk | Mitigation |\n|---|---|\n| **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 or deflate |\n| **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version |\n| **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure |\n| **jbig2enc cross-platform binary compatibility** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux. **Option**: use statically-linked jbig2enc (Alpine Docker build) to avoid dynamic-linking issues across Linux distros |\n| **lept4j API coverage uncertainty** — `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` may not be exposed | Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding; or pure-Java binarization. ImageSegmenter interface stays the same |\n| **JBIG2 PDF stream embedding complexity** — PDFBox has no high-level API to embed raw jbig2 data with JBIG2Decode filter | Primary: construct PDF stream manually. **Option**: fall back to CCITT G4 fax encoding (PDFBox native) or deflate compression. PDFAssembler interface stays the same |\n| **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |"
}
```

115: 6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build
116: 7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped) and creates `run.sh` / `run.bat` wrapper scripts that source it automatically — users never set it manually.
117: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` + `run.sh`/`run.bat` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
118: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
119: ```
120: Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments)
121:        |
122:        v
123: PageExtractor (Step 1) — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI
124:        |
125:        v
126: ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — lept4j (Leptonica via JNA):
127:   pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()
128:   pixBackgroundNorm()          -> white background
129:   pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()   -> binarize
130:   Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG
131:        |
132:    ----+----
133:    |         |
134:    v         v
135: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine       Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)
136: (Step 3)     (Step 4)
137:    |         |
138:    | native  | Tess4J
139:    | jbig2enc| word-level bboxes
140:    | glyph   |
141:    | dict    |
142:    |         |
143:    ----+-----
144:        |
