<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages**Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
5: ## Constraints
6: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)
7: 2. Prefer Java over Python when possible
8: 3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.
9: 4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
10: ## 1. Project Structure
11: ```
12: TrulyFreeOCR/
13: ├── build.gradle.kts            # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)
14: ├── settings.gradle.kts          # Project name
15: ├── gradlew                      # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)
16: ├── gradlew.bat                  # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)
17: ├── gradle/
18: │   └── wrapper/                 # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties
19: ├── README.md
20: ├── NOTICE.md                    # License attributions
21: ├── native/                      # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)
22: │   ├── win/
23: │   │   └── jbig2enc.exe
24: │   ├── linux/
25: │   │   ├── jbig2enc
26: │   │   ├── libtesseract.so      # Bundled by bootstrap
27: │   │   └── liblept.so
28: │   └── mac/
29: │       ├── jbig2enc
30: │       ├── libtesseract.dylib   # Bundled by bootstrap
31: │       └── liblept.dylib
32: ├── jdk/                         # OpenJDK 21 LTS (installed by install-deps.sh)
33: │   └── {os}/                    # Platform-specific JDK archive
34: ├── tessdata/                    # Tesseract language data files
35: │   └── eng.traineddata
36: ├── install-deps.sh              # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)
37: ├── run.sh                       # Convenience wrapper (Unix)
38: ├── run.bat                      # Convenience wrapper (Windows)
39: ├── bootstrap.sh                 # End-user dependency installer (Unix)
40: ├── bootstrap.bat                # End-user dependency installer (Windows)
41: └── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/
42:     ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java        # CLI entry point (picocli)
43:     ├── pipeline/
44:     │   ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java  # Coordinates all 6 steps
45:     │   ├── PageExtractor.java         # Step 1: PDF page to images
46:     │   ├── ImageSegmenter.java        # Step 2: lept4j normalization
47:     │   ├── JBIG2Compressor.java       # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess
48:     │   ├── OCREngine.java             # Step 4: Tess4J OCR
49:     │   ├── PDFAssembler.java          # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly
50:     │   └── MetadataPreserver.java     # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments
51:     ├── model/
52:     │   ├── PageResult.java
53:     │   ├── TextBlock.java
54:     │   └── SegmentedImage.java
55:     └── util/
56:         ├── PlatformUtils.java         # OS detection, native paths
57:         ├── SubprocessRunner.java
58:         └── TempFileManager.java       # Cleanup temp files
59: ```
60: ## 2. Self-Contained Dependencies Strategy
61: ### Gradle Dependencies (in build.gradle.kts)
62: Only 2 direct deps needed — Tess4J brings everything else transitively:
63: | Direct Dep | Transitive Deps It Brings | License |
64: |---|---|---|
65: | **`net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0`** | PDFBox 3.x, pdfbox-tools, jbig2-imageio, JNA, commons-io, **lept4j**, jboss-vfs, jai-imageio-core, slf4j-api | Apache 2.0 |
66: | **`info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6`** | (none) | Apache 2.0 |
67: ### Native/Bundled Dependencies
68: | Dep | Bundled How | License |
69: |---|---|---|
70: | **OpenJDK 21 LTS** | Downloaded by `install-deps.sh`/`bootstrap.sh` into `jdk/{os}/` | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception |
71: | **Gradle** | Auto-downloaded by Gradle Wrapper (`gradlew`) on first build | Apache 2.0 |
72: | **jbig2enc** | Pre-compiled binary in `native/{os}/jbig2enc` | Apache 2.0 |
73: | **Tesseract + Leptonica (Windows)** | Already bundled inside Tess4J JAR as DLLs | Apache 2.0 |
74: | **Tesseract + Leptonica (macOS/Linux)** | `.so`/`.dylib` in `native/{os}/` installed by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
75: | **Tesseract language data** | `tessdata/eng.traineddata` downloaded by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
76: ### All License Checks Passed
77: | Dependency | License | Business-Friendly? |
78: |---|---|---|
79: | Tess4J | Apache 2.0 | |
80: | JNA | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 (dual) | |
81: | jai-imageio-core | BSD 3-clause | |
82: | PDFBox | Apache 2.0 | |
83: | pdfbox-tools | Apache 2.0 | |
84: | jbig2-imageio | Apache 2.0 | (decode only) |
85: | commons-io | Apache 2.0 | |
86: | lept4j | Apache 2.0 | |
87: | jboss-vfs | Apache 2.0 | |
88: | slf4j-api | MIT | |
89: | picocli | Apache 2.0 | |
90: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | |
91: | Gradle | Apache 2.0 | |
92: | jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | |
93: | Tesseract (native) | Apache 2.0 | |
94: | Leptonica (native) | BSD 2-clause | |
95: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
96: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
97: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)
98: 2. Download **OpenJDK 21 LTS** (Eclipse Temurin `21.0.x`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed
99: 3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory
100: 4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files using bundled JDK:
101:    ```bash
102:    $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0
103:    ```
104: 5. Remove temporary Gradle install
105: 6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`) and generate `run.sh` / `run.bat` convenience wrappers
106: 7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform
107: 8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
108: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
109: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
110: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`
111: 2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform
112: 3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`
113: 4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`
114: 5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`
115: 6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build
116: 7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped) and creates `run.sh` / `run.bat` wrapper scripts that source it automatically — users never set it manually.
117: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` + `run.sh`/`run.bat` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
118: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
119: ```
120: Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments)
121:        |
122:        v
123: PageExtractor (Step 1) — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI
124:        |
125:        v
126: ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — lept4j (Leptonica via JNA):
127:   pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()
128:   pixBackgroundNorm()          -> white background
129:   pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()   -> binarize
130:   Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG
131:        |
132:    ----+----
133:    |         |
134:    v         v
135: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine       Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)
136: (Step 3)     (Step 4)
137:    |         |
138:    | native  | Tess4J
139:    | jbig2enc| word-level bboxes
140:    | glyph   |
141:    | dict    |
142:    |         |
143:    ----+-----
144:        |
145:        v
146: PDFAssembler (Step 5)
147:   JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask + invisible OCR text
148:        |
149:        v
150: MetadataPreserver (Step 6)
151:   Bookmarks, annotations, metadata from source PDF
152:        |
153:        v
154: Output PDF (searchable, compressed, original structure preserved)
155: ```
156: ## 4. Component Details
157: ### PageExtractor (Step 1)
158: - **Input**: PDDocument
159: - **Process**: PDFBox PDFRenderer renders each page at 300 DPI -> BufferedImage
160: - **Output**: `List<PageImage>` (one per page with dimensions)
161: ### ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — lept4j instead of JavaCV/OpenCV
162: - **Input**: BufferedImage -> Leptonica Pix
163: - **Process**:
164:   - `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()` -> grayscale
165:   - `pixBackgroundNorm()` / `pixBackgroundNormMorph()` -> normalize BG to white
166:   - `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` / `pixSauvolaOnContrastNorm()` -> adaptive binarization
167:   - `pixThresholdToBinary()` -> text mask
168: - **Output**: SegmentedImage (binary foreground mask + cleaned background)
169: ### JBIG2Compressor (Step 3)
170: - **Process**: Write temp PNG -> invoke `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` -> read .jbig2 output
171: - **Fallback**: CCITT Group 4 fax encoding (PDFBox native)
172: ### OCREngine (Step 4)
173: - **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes
174: - **Output**: PageResult with `TextBlock(word, bbox, confidence)` list
175: ### PDFAssembler (Step 5)
176: - Per-page: `PDPage` -> JPEG background `PDImageXObject` -> JBIG2 mask with JBIG2Decode filter -> invisible text (rendering mode 3) at OCR bboxes
177: ### MetadataPreserver (Step 6)
178: - Copy `PDDocumentOutline` (bookmarks), `PDPageAnnotation` (links/comments), `PDDocumentInformation`, XML metadata
179: ## 5. Build Configuration
180: ### Gradle (build.gradle.kts)
181: ```kotlin
182: plugins {
183:     application
184:     id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "8.1.1"
185: }
186: 
187: group = "com.trulyfreeocr"
188: version = "1.0.0"
189: 
190: java {
191:     toolchain {
192:         languageVersion = JavaLanguageVersion.of(21)
193:     }
194: }
195: 
196: repositories {
197:     mavenCentral()
198: }
199: 
200: dependencies {
201:     implementation("net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0")
202:     implementation("info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6")
203:     annotationProcessor("info.picocli:picocli-codegen:4.7.6")
204: }
205: 
206: application {
207:     mainClass = "com.trulyfreeocr.TrulyFreeOCR"
208: }
209: 
210: tasks {
211:     shadowJar {
212:         archiveBaseName.set("trulyfreeocr")
213:         archiveClassifier.set("")
214:         archiveVersion.set("")
215:     }
216:     build {
217:         dependsOn(shadowJar)
218:     }
219: }
220: ```
221: ### Gradle Plugins
222: - `com.github.johnrengelman.shadow` — fat JAR (replaces maven-shade-plugin)
223: - `application` — run task with `mainClass`
224: - `picocli-codegen` (annotationProcessor) — picocli command reflection
225: ## 6. Implementation Order
226: | Phase | Component | Notes |
227: |---|---|---|
228: | 1 | install-deps.sh + Gradle scaffold + CLI (picocli) | Foundation |
229: | 2 | PageExtractor | Simple PDFBox rendering |
230: | 3 | OCREngine | Tess4J — easiest to verify |
231: | 4 | ImageSegmenter | lept4j/Leptonica |
232: | 5 | PDFAssembler (basic: background + text) | Uncompressed first |
233: | 6 | JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner | Bundle native jbig2enc |
234: | 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Optimized compression |
235: | 8 | MetadataPreserver | Bookmarks, annotations |
236: | 9 | bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat | Self-contained setup |
237: | 10 | Integration tests + sample PDFs | Validation |
238: ## 7. Cross-Platform Native Support
239: | Component | Windows | Linux | macOS |
240: |---|---|---|---|---|
241: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | `jdk/win/` | `jdk/linux/` | `jdk/mac/` |
242: | Gradle | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) |
243: | PDFBox (pure Java) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR |
244: | Tess4J (JNA) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR |
245: | Tesseract native | In Tess4J JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` |
246: | Leptonica native | In lept4j JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` |
247: | jbig2enc | `native/win/jbig2enc.exe` | `native/linux/jbig2enc` | `native/mac/jbig2enc` |
248: | Bootstrapper | `bootstrap.bat` | `bootstrap.sh` | `bootstrap.sh` |
249: ## 8. CLI Usage
250: ```bash
251: # 0. Developer setup (one-time):
252: ./install-deps.sh    # Downloads: OpenJDK 21 LTS, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper
253: 
254: # 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):
255: # macOS:
256: jdk/mac/jdk-21.0.x/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
257: # Linux:
258: jdk/linux/jdk-21.0.x/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
259: # Windows:
260: jdk\win\jdk-21.0.x\bin\java -jar gradle\wrapper\gradle-wrapper.jar build
261: 
262: # Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:
263: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
264: 
265: # 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):
266: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf
267: 
268: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):
269: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \
270:     --native-dir ./native \
271:     --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \
272:     -o output.pdf
273: 
274: # 3. Convenience wrapper (sets TFOCR_JAVA_HOME automatically):
275: ./run.sh input.pdf -o output.pdf         # Unix
276: ./run.bat input.pdf -o output.pdf        # Windows
277: 
278: # 4. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):
279: ./bootstrap.sh                            # Unix
280: bootstrap.bat                            # Windows
281: # Then use run.sh / run.bat for build and execution
282: ```
283: ## 9. Key Risks & Mitigations
284: | Risk | Mitigation |
285: |---|---|
286: | **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 or deflate |
287: | **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version |
288: | **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure |
289: | **jbig2enc cross-platform binary compatibility** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux. **Option**: use statically-linked jbig2enc (Alpine Docker build) to avoid dynamic-linking issues across Linux distros |
290: | **lept4j API coverage uncertainty** — `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` may not be exposed | Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding; or pure-Java binarization. ImageSegmenter interface stays the same |
291: | **JBIG2 PDF stream embedding complexity** — PDFBox has no high-level API to embed raw jbig2 data with JBIG2Decode filter | Primary: construct PDF stream manually. **Option**: fall back to CCITT G4 fax encoding (PDFBox native) or deflate compression. PDFAssembler interface stays the same |
292: | **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |
293: ## 10. Key Findings from Dependency Verification
294: 1. **jbig2-imageio is read-only (decode only)** — confirmed from official Apache PDFBox README. Cannot encode JBIG2 in pure Java.
295: 2. **Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs** — from Tess4J's own documentation. macOS/Linux users must provide native Tesseract + Leptonica libraries. Bootstrap handles this.
296: 3. **lept4j can replace JavaCV entirely** — Tess4J already depends on lept4j (Leptonica JNA wrapper), which exports `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, `pixThresholdToBinary()`, etc. — exactly what Step 2 needs. Eliminates ~200MB JavaCV dependency.
297: 4. **All licenses verified** — every dependency (direct and transitive) is Apache 2.0, MIT, or BSD. No AGPL/GPL concerns.
298: ## 11. Legal Considerations for Automated Dependency Downloads
299: The install scripts (`install-deps.sh`, `bootstrap.sh`, Gradle Wrapper) download dependencies from Maven Central, Adoptium API, GitHub Releases, and system package managers without requiring users to visit each project's website.
300: ### Assessment
301: All dependencies use self-executing permissive licenses (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD, GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception) — these grant rights automatically upon use or distribution. No "I Accept" click or website visit is legally required.
302: | Dependency | Source | License | Concern? |
303: |---|---|---|---|
304: | Tess4J, picocli (via Gradle) | Maven Central | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Java practice |
305: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | Adoptium API (`api.adoptium.net`) | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | None — API designed for automation |
306: | jbig2enc | GitHub Releases | Apache 2.0 | None — standard binary distribution |
307: | Tesseract/Leptonica | `apt` / `brew` | Apache 2.0 / BSD | None — package managers do this |
308: | tessdata | GitHub (Tesseract-OCR) | Apache 2.0 | None |
309: | Gradle (via Wrapper) | Gradle Services (`services.gradle.org`) | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Gradle practice |
310: ### "Stealing GitHub Stars" Consideration
311: A concern was raised: project owners rely on GitHub stars for visibility, and automated downloads skip visiting their repos.
312: **Why this is not a concern:**
313: 1. **Automated dependency resolution does not rob stars.** Stars are a human social signal — no project expects or receives stars from CI pipelines, package managers, or install scripts. Manually downloading a release zip and starring are unrelated actions.
314: 2. **Most dependencies never touch GitHub at all:**
315:    - Tess4J, picocli → Maven Central (not GitHub)
316:    - Gradle → `services.gradle.org` (not GitHub)
317:    - OpenJDK → Adoptium API (not GitHub)
318:    - Tesseract/Leptonica → `apt` / `brew` (not GitHub)
319: 3. **Only jbig2enc hits GitHub Releases** — the same pattern used by Homebrew, Docker, and every package manager that fetches release artifacts. Nobody considers those projects to be "stealing stars."
320: 4. **Universal industry standard.** Every Maven project, npm package, Homebrew formula, and Docker image works this way. If automated downloads were "stealing," the entire open-source ecosystem would be guilty.
321: **Optional nicety**: A credit line in the CLI output or README can drive awareness:```Powered by Tesseract OCR (github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract)```
322: **Verdict**: Not stealing. Stars come from human engagement, not `curl` scripts.
323: ### Mitigation
324: `NOTICE.md` will list every dependency with its exact version, license, and copyright notice to satisfy Apache 2.0 §4 and BSD attribution requirements.
325: ### Verdict
326: No legal concern for publishing on GitHub. Automated downloads from Maven Central, GitHub Releases, and package managers are standard industry practice used by every CI/CD pipeline and container build.
327: 
328: 

(End of file - total 328 lines)
</content>
```

Let me write the Python script for generating test PDFs. I need to carefully consider what test cases are needed for TDD.

For TDD, I need tests that verify each component's behavior. The test PDFs should cover:

1. **Blank page** (edge case - no text, no content)
2. **Simple text page** (single column, clean text on white background)
3. **Multi-page document** (3+ pages, tests pipeline orchestration)
4. **Two-column layout** (tests segmentation correctness)
5. **Mixed content** (images + text, tests segmentation robustness)
6. **Noisy/scanned quality** (simulates real scanned docs with slight noise)
7. **Page with tables** (tests table handling in OCR)

For the initial TDD phase, I'll focus on PDFs 1-4.

Let me generate these using Python Pillow. Then I also need a PDF with bookmarks/annotations - I can note that this is for a later phase or create it as a separate manual step.

Actually, for the bookmarks PDF, I can use Python with a different approach - create a PDF with pypdf or similar and add outlines. Let me check if pypdf is available.

</details>

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/generate_test_pdfs.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Generate test PDFs for TrulyFreeOCR test-driven development.\n\nEach PDF is image-based (non-searchable) to simulate scanned documents.\nCreates files in tests/test-files/. Run from project root.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont\nimport fitz  # PyMuPDF\n\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"test-files\")\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n\nFONT_DIR = \"/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu\"\nW, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI\nMARGIN = 50\nFONT_SZ = 14\n\ndef font(name=\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", sz=FONT_SZ):\n    return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, name), sz)\n\ndef new_page():\n    img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)\n    return img, draw\n\ndef save_image_pdf(pages, path):\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for img in pages:\n        img_bytes = img.tobytes(\"png\")\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(img_bytes)\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n    doc.save(path)\n    doc.close()\n\n\n# ── 1. blank.pdf ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_blank():\n    img, _ = new_page()\n    save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"blank.pdf\"))\n    print(\"  blank.pdf\")\n\n# ── 2. simple-text.pdf ──────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_simple_text():\n    img, d = new_page()\n    fnt = font()\n    lines = [\n        \"The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.\",\n        \"Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs.\",\n        \"Sphinx of black quartz, judge my vow.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"This is a test document for OCR validation.\",\n        \"It contains English text rendered as an image,\",\n        \"simulating a scanned page that is not searchable.\",\n        \"\",\n        \"The pipeline should extract this text via Tesseract,\",\n        \"then embed it as invisible text over the image.\",\n        \"The output PDF should be searchable while retaining\",\n        \"the original visual appearance.\",\n    ]\n    y = MARGIN\n    for line in lines:\n        d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill=\"black\", font=fnt)\n        y += 22\n    save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"simple-text.pdf\"))\n    print(\"  simple-text.pdf\")\n\n# ── 3. multi-page.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_multi_page():\n    pages = []\n    page_data = [\n        [\"Page 1: Introduction\",\n         \"This is the first page of a multi-page document.\",\n         \"It contains introductory text about the topic.\",\n         \"Each page has different content to test that\",\n         \"the OCR pipeline processes all pages correctly.\"],\n        [\"Page 2: Methodology\",\n         \"The approach uses a 6-step pipeline:\",\n         \"1. Page extraction via PDFBox at 300 DPI\",\n         \"2. Image segmentation via Leptonica (lept4j)\",\n         \"3. JBIG2 compression of text mask\",\n         \"4. OCR via Tesseract (Tess4J)\",\n         \"5. PDF re-assembly with searchable text\",\n         \"6. Metadata preservation (bookmarks, annotations)\"],\n        [\"Page 3: Results\",\n         \"The output is a searchable, highly-compressed PDF\",\n         \"that maintains the original visual appearance\",\n         \"while adding a hidden text layer for searching.\",\n         \"All metadata from the source PDF is preserved.\"],\n    ]\n    for title, *body in page_data:\n        img, d = new_page()\n        d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill=\"black\",\n               font=font(\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", 18))\n        y = MARGIN + 40\n        for line in body:\n            d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill=\"black\", font=font())\n            y += 22\n        pages.append(img)\n    save_image_pdf(pages, os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"multi-page.pdf\"))\n    print(\"  multi-page.pdf\")\n\n# ── 4. two-column.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_two_column():\n    img, d = new_page()\n    col_w = (W - 3 * MARGIN) // 2\n    x1, x2 = MARGIN, MARGIN * 2 + col_w\n    fnt = font()\n    body = (\n        \"Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing \"\n        \"elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et \"\n        \"dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis \"\n        \"nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip \"\n        \"ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in \"\n        \"reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore \"\n        \"eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat \"\n        \"cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia \"\n        \"deserunt mollit anim id est laborum.\"\n    )\n    # Wrap into ~40-char lines\n    words = body.split()\n    lines = []\n    for i in range(0, len(words), 8):\n        lines.append(\" \".join(words[i:i+8]))\n\n    # Left column\n    d.text((x1, MARGIN), \"Left Column\", fill=\"black\",\n           font=font(\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", 16))\n    y = MARGIN + 30\n    for line in lines:\n        d.text((x1, y), line, fill=\"black\", font=fnt)\n        y += 18\n\n    # Right column\n    d.text((x2, MARGIN), \"Right Column\", fill=\"black\",\n           font=font(\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", 16))\n    y = MARGIN + 30\n    for line in lines:\n        d.text((x2, y), line, fill=\"black\", font=fnt)\n        y += 18\n\n    save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"two-column.pdf\"))\n    print(\"  two-column.pdf\")\n\n# ── 5. with-annotations.pdf ─────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_with_annotations():\n    pages = []\n    # Create multi-page document with bookmarks\n    titles = [\"Chapter 1: Getting Started\",\n              \"Chapter 2: Configuration\",\n              \"Chapter 3: Usage\"]\n    for i, title in enumerate(titles, 1):\n        img, d = new_page()\n        d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN), title, fill=\"black\",\n               font=font(\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", 18))\n        d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 40),\n               f\"This is page {i} of the annotated document.\",\n               fill=\"black\", font=font())\n        d.text((MARGIN, MARGIN + 70),\n               \"This PDF includes bookmarks and annotations.\",\n               fill=\"black\", font=font())\n        pages.append(img)\n\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for i, img in enumerate(pages):\n        img_bytes = img.tobytes(\"png\")\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(img_bytes)\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n\n        # Add text annotation on first page\n        if i == 0:\n            annot = page.add_text_annot(\n                (100, 100), \"This is a sticky note annotation.\"\n            )\n\n        # Add underline annotation on first page\n        if i == 0:\n            page.add_underline_annot(\n                fitz.Rect(MARGIN, MARGIN, MARGIN + 200, MARGIN + 20)\n            )\n\n    # Add bookmarks (outline)\n    toc = [\n        [1, \"Chapter 1\", 1],\n        [1, \"Chapter 2\", 2],\n        [1, \"Chapter 3\", 3],\n    ]\n    doc.set_toc(toc)\n\n    doc.save(os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"with-annotations.pdf\"))\n    doc.close()\n    print(\"  with-annotations.pdf\")\n\n# ── 6. noisy-scan.pdf ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\ndef make_noisy_scan():\n    import random\n    random.seed(42)\n    img, d = new_page()\n    fnt = font()\n\n    lines = [\n        \"This page simulates a noisy scanned document.\",\n        \"It includes speckles, uneven lighting, and\",\n        \"slight rotation to test image preprocessing.\",\n    ]\n    y = MARGIN\n    for line in lines:\n        d.text((MARGIN, y), line, fill=\"black\", font=fnt)\n        y += 22\n\n    # Add noise\n    pixels = img.load()\n    for _ in range(3000):\n        x = random.randint(0, W - 1)\n        y = random.randint(0, H - 1)\n        v = random.randint(0, 60)\n        pixels[x, y] = (v, v, v)\n\n    # Add a gradient (simulating uneven lighting)\n    for y in range(H):\n        shade = int(30 * (y / H))\n        for x in range(W):\n            r, g, b = pixels[x, y]\n            pixels[x, y] = (\n                max(0, r - shade),\n                max(0, g - shade),\n                max(0, b - shade),\n            )\n\n    save_image_pdf([img], os.path.join(OUT_DIR, \"noisy-scan.pdf\"))\n    print(\"  noisy-scan.pdf\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    print(\"Generating test PDFs...\")\n    make_blank()\n    make_simple_text()\n    make_multi_page()\n    make_two_column()\n    make_with_annotations()\n    make_noisy_scan()\n    print(f\"\\nDone. Files in {OUT_DIR}/\")\n"
}

Run from project root.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport io\nimport os\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont\nimport fitz  # PyMuPDF\n\nOUT_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"test-files\")\nos.makedirs(OUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)\n\nFONT_DIR = \"/usr/share/fonts/truetype/dejavu\"\nW, H = 612, 792  # US Letter at 72 DPI\nMARGIN = 50\nFONT_SZ = 14\n\ndef font(name=\"DejaVuSans.ttf\", sz=FONT_SZ):\n    return ImageFont.truetype(os.path.join(FONT_DIR, name), sz)\n\ndef new_page():\n    img = Image.new(\"RGB\", (W, H), \"white\")\n    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)\n    return img, draw\n\ndef save_image_pdf(pages, path):\n    doc = fitz.open()\n    for img in pages:\n        buf = io.BytesIO()\n        img.save(buf, format=\"JPEG\", quality=95)\n        buf.seek(0)\n        pix = fitz.Pixmap(buf.read())\n        page = doc.new_page(width=W, height=H)\n        page.insert_image(fitz.Rect(0, 0, W, H), pixmap=pix)\n    doc.save(path)\n    doc.close()"
}

- `blank.pdf` - 1 page, no content
- `simple-text.pdf` - 1 page with text
- `multi-page.pdf` - 3 pages with different content
- `two-column.pdf` - 1 page, two-column layout
- `with-annotations.pdf` - 3 pages with bookmarks (toc=True) and 2 annotations
- `noisy-scan.pdf` - 1 page with noise and gradient

The file sizes seem large (1.4MB per page) - this is because JPEG at quality=95 on a 612x792 image produces large files. For test purposes this is fine, but real scanned docs at 300 DPI would be much larger. I could reduce the quality or add compression options, but this is acceptable for testing.

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/tests/TESTING.md",
  "content": "# TrulyFreeOCR — Testing Plan\n\n## Philosophy\n\nTest-driven development: write the test first, then implement until it passes.\nEach component in §6 of `Plan.md` has a corresponding test class written before\nthe implementation.\n\n## Framework\n\n- **JUnit 5 (Jupiter)** — via Gradle `test` task\n- **AssertJ** — fluent assertions (optional, added to `build.gradle.kts` if needed)\n- **Test PDFs** — generated by `generate_test_pdfs.py` in `tests/test-files/`\n\n## Test Structure\n\n```\nsrc/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/\n├── pipeline/\n│   ├── PageExtractorTest.java\n│   ├── ImageSegmenterTest.java\n│   ├── JBIG2CompressorTest.java\n│   ├── OCREngineTest.java\n│   ├── PDFAssemblerTest.java\n│   ├── MetadataPreserverTest.java\n│   └── PipelineOrchestratorTest.java\n└── util/\n    ├── PlatformUtilsTest.java\n    └── SubprocessRunnerTest.java\n```\n\n## Test Categories\n\n### Unit Tests\nTest a single class in isolation. Mock external dependencies (native binaries,\nfile I/O). Fast, no native deps needed.\n\n### Integration Tests\nTest a component with its real dependencies (Tess4J, jbig2enc binary, PDFBox).\nMarked with `@Tag(\"integration\")`. Run separately from unit tests.\n\n### End-to-End Tests\nTest the full pipeline end-to-end on real test PDFs.\nMarked with `@Tag(\"e2e\")`. Run only after all units pass.\n\n## Test PDFs\n\nGenerated by `generate_test_pdfs.py`. Located in `tests/test-files/`.\n\n| File | Pages | Description | Primary Tests Targeting It |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `blank.pdf` | 1 | All-white page, no content | PageExtractor, ImageSegmenter |\n| `simple-text.pdf` | 1 | Single-column clean text | OCREngine, PDFAssembler |\n| `multi-page.pdf` | 3 | Pages with different content | PipelineOrchestrator, PageExtractor |\n| `two-column.pdf` | 1 | Two-column layout | ImageSegmenter (segmentation) |\n| `with-annotations.pdf` | 3 | Bookmarks + text/underline annotations | MetadataPreserver |\n| `noisy-scan.pdf` | 1 | Speckle noise + lighting gradient | ImageSegmenter (preprocessing) |\n\n## TDD Workflow Per Phase\n\n### Phase 1 — Foundation (CLI + scaffold)\n1. `TrulyFreeOCRTest` — parse `--help`, `--version`, basic arg parsing via picocli\n2. `PlatformUtilsTest` — OS detection returns expected values\n3. `SubprocessRunnerTest` — runs a known command, checks stdout/stderr/exit code\n\n### Phase 2 — PageExtractor\n1. **Write**: `PageExtractorTest`\n   - `blank.pdf` → 1 page, dimensions match\n   - `multi-page.pdf` → 3 pages, each returns a non-null `BufferedImage`\n   - Invalid path → throws descriptive exception\n2. **Implement**: `PageExtractor.java`\n\n### Phase 3 — OCREngine\n1. **Write**: `OCREngineTest`\n   - `simple-text.pdf` → returns `PageResult` with word-level `TextBlock` list\n   - Known word \"quick\" appears in results\n   - `blank.pdf` → empty result\n2. **Implement**: `OCREngine.java`\n\n### Phase 4 — ImageSegmenter\n1. **Write**: `ImageSegmenterTest`\n   - Takes `BufferedImage` → returns `SegmentedImage` with mask + bg\n   - `two-column.pdf` → mask separates both columns\n   - `noisy-scan.pdf` → denoised output\n2. **Implement**: `ImageSegmenter.java`\n\n### Phase 5 — PDFAssembler (basic)\n1. **Write**: `PDFAssemblerTest`\n   - Reconstructs PDF from components\n   - Output PDF is searchable (text can be extracted)\n   - Output PDF has same dimensions as input\n2. **Implement**: `PDFAssembler.java`\n\n### Phase 6 — JBIG2Compressor\n1. **Write**: `JBIG2CompressorTest`\n   - Falls back gracefully when jbig2enc is not on PATH\n   - Compresses a known binary mask\n2. **Implement**: `JBIG2Compressor.java`\n\n### Phase 7 — PDFAssembler (JBIG2)\n1. **Extend**: `PDFAssemblerTest`\n   - Output with JBIG2 is smaller than uncompressed version\n   - Visual fidelity preserved\n\n### Phase 8 — MetadataPreserver\n1. **Write**: `MetadataPreserverTest`\n   - `with-annotations.pdf` → bookmarks preserved in output\n   - Annotations count matches\n2. **Implement**: `MetadataPreserver.java`\n\n### Phase 9 — PipelineOrchestrator\n1. **Write**: `PipelineOrchestratorTest` (integration)\n   - End-to-end on `simple-text.pdf` → produces searchable output\n   - Output file exists and is valid PDF\n2. **Implement**: `PipelineOrchestrator.java`\n\n## Running Tests\n\n```bash\n# All unit tests (fast)\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test\n\n# Integration tests only\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test --tests \"*IT\"\n\n# A single test class\n$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar test --tests \"*PageExtractorTest\"\n```\n\n## Adding New Test PDFs\n\n1. Add a new function in `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py`\n2. Run it to regenerate\n3. Reference the file from your test class by path:\n   ```java\n   var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/my-new-file.pdf\");\n   ```\n"
}
```

}

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/PlatformUtils.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.util;\n\npublic class PlatformUtils {\n\n    public enum Os { WINDOWS, LINUX, MAC }\n\n    public enum Arch { X64, AARCH64 }\n\n    public static Os detectOs() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.name\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"win\")) return Os.WINDOWS;\n        if (raw.contains(\"mac\")) return Os.MAC;\n        return Os.LINUX;\n    }\n\n    public static Arch detectArch() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.arch\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"aarch64\") || raw.contains(\"arm64\")) return Arch.AARCH64;\n        return Arch.X64;\n    }\n\n    public static String getProjectJavaHome() {\n        String env = System.getenv(\"TFOCR_JAVA_HOME\");\n        if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) return env;\n        return System.getProperty(\"java.home\");\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/TempFileManager.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.util;\n\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.file.Files;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\n\npublic class TempFileManager {\n\n    private final List<File> tempFiles = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    public File createTempFile(String prefix, String suffix) throws IOException {\n        File f = Files.createTempFile(prefix, suffix).toFile();\n        f.deleteOnExit();\n        tempFiles.add(f);\n        return f;\n    }\n\n    public void cleanup() {\n        for (File f : tempFiles) {\n            if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n        }\n        tempFiles.clear();\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr;\n\nimport picocli.CommandLine;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Command;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Option;\nimport picocli.CommandLine.Parameters;\n\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.util.concurrent.Callable;\n\n@Command(\n    name = \"trulyfreeocr\",\n    version = \"1.0.0\",\n    description = \"Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs\",\n    mixinStandardHelpOptions = true\n)\npublic class TrulyFreeOCR implements Callable<Integer> {\n\n    @Parameters(index = \"0\", description = \"Input PDF file\")\n    private File inputFile;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"-o\", \"--output\"}, description = \"Output PDF file\", defaultValue = \"output.pdf\")\n    private File outputFile;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--native-dir\"}, description = \"Directory containing native binaries\", defaultValue = \"./native\")\n    private File nativeDir;\n\n    @Option(names = {\"--tessdata-dir\"}, description = \"Directory containing Tesseract language data\", defaultValue = \"./tessdata\")\n    private File tessdataDir;\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer call() {\n        System.out.println(\"TrulyFreeOCR v1.0.0\");\n        System.out.println(\"  Input:  \" + inputFile);\n        System.out.println(\"  Output: \" + outputFile);\n        System.out.println(\"Not yet implemented.\");\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        int exitCode = new CommandLine(new TrulyFreeOCR()).execute(args);\n        System.exit(exitCode);\n    }\n}\n"
}

Here's what was created for TDD preparation:

### `tests/` — Test Infrastructure

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py` | Script to regenerate all test PDFs |
| `tests/test-files/*.pdf` | 6 generated test PDFs (see below) |
| `tests/TESTING.md` | TDD plan — per-phase test-first workflow |

### Test PDFs

| File | Pages | Purpose | First Phase That Uses It |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blank.pdf` | 1 | Edge case: empty page | Phase 2 PageExtractor |
| `simple-text.pdf` | 1 | Clean single-column text | Phase 3 OCREngine |
| `multi-page.pdf` | 3 | Pages with different content | Phase 2 PageExtractor |
| `two-column.pdf` | 1 | Tests segmentation correctness | Phase 4 ImageSegmenter |
| `with-annotations.pdf` | 3 | Bookmarks + 2 annotations | Phase 8 MetadataPreserver |
| `noisy-scan.pdf` | 1 | Speckles + lighting gradient | Phase 4 ImageSegmenter |

### Source Scaffolding

- `build.gradle.kts` — includes JUnit 5 platform, Shadow plugin, JDK 21 toolchain
- `TrulyFreeOCR.java` — picocli CLI entry point (stub)
- `model/PageResult.java`, `TextBlock.java`, `SegmentedImage.java` — data classes
- `util/PlatformUtils.java`, `TempFileManager.java` — utility classes with passing tests
- `src/test/` — `PlatformUtilsTest`, `TempFileManagerTest` (placeholder tests)

### TDD Workflow (from `TESTING.md`)

Each Plan.md phase starts with writing the test, then implementing until it passes — covering unit, integration (`@Tag("integration")`), and end-to-end (`@Tag("e2e")`) levels.

java.util.List;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.io.TempDir;\n\nclass PageExtractorTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_blankPdf_returnsOnePage() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        assertEquals(1, pages.size());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_blankPdf_returnsNonNullImages() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        assertNotNull(pages.get(0));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_blankPdf_returnsCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        // 612x792 at 72 DPI = US Letter\n        assertEquals(612, pages.get(0).getWidth());\n        assertEquals(792, pages.get(0).getHeight());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_multiPagePdf_returnsCorrectPageCount() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/multi-page.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        assertEquals(3, pages.size());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_invalidPath_throwsIOException() {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/nonexistent.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        assertThrows(IOException.class, () -> extractor.extractPages(pdf));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void close_releasesResources() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        assertDoesNotThrow(() -> extractor.close());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void extractPages_simpleTextPdf_returns300DpiQuality() throws IOException {\n        var pdf = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = extractor.extractPages(pdf);\n        BufferedImage img = pages.get(0);\n        assertTrue(img.getWidth() >= 612);\n        assertTrue(img.getHeight() >= 792);\n        assertEquals(BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB, img.getType());\n    }\n}\n"
}

Now I need to implement PageExtractor.java. The PageExtractor should:
- Take a PDF file
- Use PDFBox PDFRenderer to render each page at 300 DPI
- Return List<BufferedImage>
- Be AutoCloseable

Let me check the PDFBox API for rendering pages. The key class is `PDDocument` and `PDFRenderer`.

Key API:
```java
PDDocument document = PDDocument.load(file);
PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);
BufferedImage image = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(pageIndex, 300);

### Tool: write

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PageExtractor.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.rendering.PDFRenderer;\n\npublic class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private PDDocument document;\n\n    public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {\n        document = PDDocument.load(pdf);\n        PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);\n        int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        return pages;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws IOException {\n        if (document != null) {\n            document.close();\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;

java.io.IOException;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nclass OCREngineTest {\n\n    static PageExtractor extractor;\n    static OCREngine engine;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        engine = new OCREngine();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_simpleText_returnsWordBlocks() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertNotNull(result.getTextBlocks());\n        assertFalse(result.getTextBlocks().isEmpty());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_simpleText_containsExpectedWord() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        var words = result.getTextBlocks().stream()\n                .map(tb -> tb.getWord().toLowerCase())\n                .toList();\n        assertTrue(words.contains(\"quick\"),\n                () -> \"Expected 'quick' in OCR output but got: \" + words);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_simpleText_allBlocksHavePositiveConfidence() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        for (var tb : result.getTextBlocks()) {\n            assertTrue(tb.getConfidence() >= 0,\n                    () -> \"Negative confidence for word: \" + tb.getWord());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_blankPage_returnsEmptyBlocks() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertTrue(result.getTextBlocks().isEmpty());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_blankPage_returnsCorrectPageNumber() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        assertEquals(1, result.getPageNumber());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void ocr_simpleText_blocksHaveBoundingBoxes() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        var result = engine.ocr(pages.get(0));\n        for (var tb : result.getTextBlocks()) {\n            assertNotNull(tb.getBbox());\n            assertTrue(tb.getBbox().width > 0);\n            assertTrue(tb.getBbox().height > 0);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"

### Tool: write

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngine.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;\n\nimport net.sourceforge.tess4j.ITesseract;\nimport net.sourceforge.tess4j.Tesseract;\nimport net.sourceforge.tess4j.Word;\n\npublic class OCREngine {\n\n    private final ITesseract tesseract;\n\n    public OCREngine() {\n        this.tesseract = new Tesseract();\n        String tessdata = System.getProperty(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n        if (tessdata == null) {\n            tessdata = System.getenv(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n        }\n        if (tessdata == null) {\n            // Try common locations\n            for (String path : new String[]{\n                    \"./tessdata\",\n                    \"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata\",\n                    \"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/4.00/tessdata\",\n                    \"/usr/share/tessdata\",\n                    \"/usr/local/share/tessdata\",\n            }) {\n                if (new java.io.File(path).exists()) {\n                    tessdata = path;\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if (tessdata != null) {\n            tesseract.setDatapath(tessdata);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        List<Word> words = tesseract.getWords(image, ITesseract.RPSM_AUTO_OSD);\n        List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();\n        if (words != null) {\n            for (Word w : words) {\n                String text = w.getText().trim();\n                if (text.isEmpty()) continue;\n                blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, w.getBoundingBox(), w.getConfidence()));\n            }\n        }\n        return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

- `TessAPI.TPSM_AUTO_OSD` or similar

- 0 = OSD_ONLY
- 1 = AUTO_OSD
- 3 = AUTO
- 6 = BLOCK

In `ITesseract` interface, there might not be predefined constants. The `getWords` method takes an int. I can use `-1` to indicate "use default" or I can find the actual constant.

Looking at Tess4J 5.x API, the page seg modes are in `TessAPI.TessPageSegMode` enum values, but they're accessed via JNA constants. Let me just use the numeric value directly. The Tesseract page segmentation modes are:

```
PSM_OSD_ONLY = 0
PSM_AUTO_OSD = 1
PSM_AUTO_ONLY = 2
PSM_AUTO = 3
PSM_SINGLE_COLUMN = 4
PSM_SINGLE_BLOCK_VERT_TEXT = 5
PSM_SINGLE_BLOCK = 6
PSM_SINGLE_LINE = 7
...
```

Actually, the safe approach is to just call `getWords(image)` without the pageSegMode parameter, or use a simple PSM value. Let me check what overloads are available.

Actually, in Tess4J 5.x, the `ITesseract` interface has:
```java
List<Word> getWords(BufferedImage image, int pageSegMode);

Actually, JNA's default behavior when loading "tesseract" on Linux is:
1. Try `libtesseract.so` exactly

This should work. But maybe the test is not using those args because it uses the default forked JVM. Let me check.

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I understand the issue:

1. On non-Windows, `LoadLibs.getTesseractLibName()` returns `"tesseract"`
2. It calls `Native.load("tesseract", TessAPI.class)` which looks for `libtesseract.so`
3. On Linux, JNA looks for `libtesseract.so` in the standard library paths

The key insight: JNA's `Native.load("tesseract", ...)` on Linux will:
1. Try `libtesseract.so` using `dlopen` which searches `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, `/etc/ld.so.cache`, `/lib`, `/usr/lib`, etc.
2. The system has `libtesseract.so.5` which is in the ld.so.cache (since `ldconfig -p` shows it)
3. But `libtesseract.so` doesn't exist as a file or symlink

When JNA calls `Native.load("tesseract", ...)`, it eventually calls `dlopen("libtesseract.so", ...)`. The `dlopen` function with the exact filename `libtesseract.so` requires that file to exist in the search path. It won't automatically try `libtesseract.so.5`.

So the solution is either:
1. Create a symlink `libtesseract.so -> libtesseract.so.5` in a library path
2. Use `jna.library.path` system property to point to a directory containing the symlink

I already created the symlink at `native/linux/libtesseract.so`. The issue is that JNA isn't finding it via `jna.library.path`.

Let me check if `jna.library.path` is actually being set when the tests run. The problem might be that our test's `configureNativePath()` in OCREngine constructor runs AFTER Tess4J's static initializer has already tried to load the library (because of class loading order).

Let me verify: when `new OCREngine()` is called (line 13 in the test), the constructor runs. The `configureNativePath()` sets `jna.library.path`. Then `new Tesseract()` is called. But `Tesseract` extends something that references `TessAPI.INSTANCE`, which triggers the static initializer that loads the native library.

The static initializer runs when the class is first accessed. If `Tesseract` references `TessAPI.INSTANCE` in its static initialization, the native library loading happens before our constructor body runs.

The fix: set `jna.library.path` in a static initializer of OCREngine, or better yet, set it as a system property BEFORE the OCREngine class is ever loaded.

Actually, looking at the loading more carefully, `TessAPI` has a static field `INSTANCE` which is initialized in `<clinit>`. The `<clinit>` runs when `TessAPI` is first accessed (e.g., when `Tesseract` references `TessAPI.INSTANCE`).

So the call chain is:
1. `new OCREngine()` → enters constructor
2. In constructor: `configureNativePath()` → sets `jna.library.path`
3. `new Tesseract()` → Tesseract constructor calls something that references `TessAPI.INSTANCE` → triggers `TessAPI.<clinit>` → `LoadLibs.getTessAPIInstance()` → `Native.load("tesseract", ...)`

Wait, but `Tesseract` constructor might not reference `TessAPI` directly. The `Tesseract` constructor might just set internal fields. The actual native call happens later in `getWords()`.

If that's the case, then `configureNativePath()` in the constructor should set the property before `getWords()` is called. But the issue might be that `TessAPI` class loading happens during `new Tesseract()` as part of method resolution.

Actually, let me check `Tesseract` class bytecode to see when it accesses TessAPI.

Actually, I think the most reliable fix is to set `jna.library.path` using a static initializer in OCREngine, before any Tess4J class is loaded. Let me make the configureNativePath a static block.

But wait - the issue might also be that the property is being set but JNA caches the search path. Let me check...

Actually, the simplest fix is to set the system property BEFORE the JVM loads Tess4J classes. This means either:
1. Setting it from the command line (which we've been trying but failing with Gradle)
2. Setting it in a static block of OCREngine

Let me try option 2.

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine if the `jvmArgs` are being properly passed from the Gradle test task. Let me add a unit test that simply checks what system properties are set.

Let me take a completely different approach. Instead of debugging the JVM arg passing, let me set the `jna.library.path` as a static initializer in `OCREngine`. But I need to make sure it's set before any Tess4J class is loaded. The key insight: Java class loading happens on first access. If I set the property in a static block of OCREngine, it will execute when OCREngine is first loaded, which is before any Tesseract/TessAPI code runs.

But wait, `Tesseract` is imported in OCREngine. Does that trigger loading? No - imports don't trigger class loading. Only actual use of the class does.

So if `configureNativePath()` is called in the constructor before `new Tesseract()`, it should work. But the constructor is not a static initializer - it's an instance initializer. The class loading (static init) happens before the constructor runs.

So the sequence is:
1. OCREngine class is loaded (static init runs, if any)
2. `new OCREngine()` → constructor runs → `configureNativePath()` → sets property
3. `new Tesseract()` → Tesseract constructor → eventually triggers TessAPI loading → Native.load()

This should work IF Tesseract doesn't trigger TessAPI in its own static initializer. The Tesseract constructor might reference TessAPI, which would trigger the TessAPI static initializer (which calls Native.load).

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

`init()` is called via `invokevirtual #48` in multiple places. The first one at line 110: let me check which method that's in. Let me look at the `getWords` method to see where it calls `init()`:

Actually, looking at this more carefully, `init()` is called lazily from methods like `getWords()`. So the sequence in our OCREngine is:

1. `new OCREngine()` - constructor runs
2. `this.tesseract = new Tesseract()` - Tesseract constructor only sets fields, no native loading
3. `tesseract.setDatapath(tessdata)` - also just sets a field
4. `tesseract.getWords(image, 1)` - THIS is where `init()` is called, which accesses `TessAPI.INSTANCE`

So our `configureNativePath()` in the constructor should work because `jna.library.path` is set before step 4 (getWords).

But WAIT - looking at the TessAPI static initializer again:
```
static {};
    Code:
       0: invokestatic  #1  // Method LoadLibs.getTessAPIInstance:()Lnet/sourceforge/tess4j/TessAPI;
       3: putstatic     #2  // Field INSTANCE:Lnet/sourceforge/tess4j/TessAPI;
```

And `getTessAPIInstance()`:
```
getTesseractLibName() -> "tesseract" (on Linux)
Native.load("tesseract", TessAPI.class)
```

So `Native.load("tesseract", ...)` is the call that's failing. It should look for `libtesseract.so` on Linux.

Now, where does JNA search? JNA's `Native.load()` calls `NativeLibrary.getInstance()` which calls `dlopen()`. The library search path includes:
1. Paths in `jna.library.path` system property
2. Paths in `jna.boot.library.path` system property
3. `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` environment variable
4. Standard system paths (`/usr/lib`, `/lib`, `/usr/local/lib`)

But there's a catch: JNA's `dlopen` actually calls `NativeLibrary.getProcessPath()` which gets the standard paths from the system. The `jna.library.path` is prepended to the search.

Wait, actually, I need to look at this more carefully. In JNA 5.6.0 (which is what Tess4J bundles), `Native.load("tesseract", ...)` calls:

1. `NativeLibrary.getProcessPath()` - gets system paths
2. Prepends `jna.library.path` if set
3. Calls `dlopen` with each path

But `dlopen("libtesseraset.so")` looks for a file named exactly `libtesseracet.so` in the search path. The file doesn't exist (we only have `libtesseract.so.5`). The symlink we created at `native/linux/libtesseract.so` should help.

The question is: is `jna.library.path` being passed to the test worker JVM correctly?

Let me add a simple test that checks the system property:

</details>

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/NativeDebugTest.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nclass NativeDebugTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void checkJnaLibraryPath() {\n        String path = System.getProperty(\"jna.library.path\");\n        System.out.println(\"jna.library.path = \" + path);\n        assertNotNull(path, \"jna.library.path should be set\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void checkNativeLibExists() {\n        String path = System.getProperty(\"jna.library.path\", \"\");\n        String first = path.split(\":\")[0];\n        java.io.File lib = new java.io.File(first + \"/libtesseract.so\");\n        System.out.println(\"Looking for: \" + lib.getAbsolutePath());\n        assertTrue(lib.exists(), \"libtesseract.so should exist at \" + lib);\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

Let me think about this differently. The issue is that `jna.library.path` is set correctly (as the debug test proved), the symlink exists, but Tess4J still can't load the native library.

Could it be that `Native.load("tesseract", ...)` also needs the Leptonica library to be loadable? Let me check:

Tesseract's shared library `libtesseract.so.5` depends on `liblept.so.5`. When JNA loads `libtesseract.so`, it needs to resolve the `liblept` dependency. This requires:
1. `libtesseract.so` to be found (we have the symlink)
2. `liblept.so` to be found (we have the symlink)
3. All transitives (libjpeg, libpng, libtiff, etc.)

We created symlinks for both `libtesseract.so` and `liblept.so` in `native/linux/`. But let me verify the Leptonica one exists:

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I see the full error chain:

1. **Tesseract.getWords()** calls `setImage()` (Tesseract.java:421)
2. `setImage()` calls `LeptUtils.convertImageToPix()` (LeptUtils.java:104)
3. This triggers `Leptonica1.<clinit>` (Leptonica1.java:41) — Leptonica's JNA static initializer
4. Leptonica1 tries to load `leptonica` library via JNA: `Native.register("leptonica")`
5. This fails because **libleptonica.so** is not found

So the actual problem isn't Tesseract's native library — it's Leptonica's! Tess4J internally uses lept4j (Leptonica via JNA) to convert the BufferedImage to a Pix before passing it to Tesseract.

The error is `Unable to load library 'leptonica': libleptonica.so: cannot open shared object file`.

I created `liblept.so` symlink (matching the installed library name) but JNA is looking for `libleptonica.so`. Let me check the correct library name.

Looking at the Leptonica shared library naming:
- The system has `liblept.so.5` and `liblept.so.5.0.4`
- But lept4j/JNA might be looking for either `liblept.so` or `libleptonica.so`

The Leptonica library's SONAME is `liblept.so.5`, but the JNA binding in lept4j might expect `libleptonica.so`. Let me check...

</details>

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Confirmed! On non-Windows, lept4j looks for `leptonica` which means it loads `libleptonica.so`. But the system library is `liblept.so.5` (from the Leptonica project). The library name has changed between versions:
- Old name: `libleptonica.so`
- New name (Leptonica 1.82+): `liblept.so`

The system has `liblept.so.5` but lept4j expects `libleptonica.so`. This is a version mismatch issue.

I need to create a symlink `libleptonica.so -> liblept.so.5` so JNA can find it when lept4j calls `Native.register("leptonica")` or `Native.load("leptonica", ...)`.

Let me create the symlink.

</details>

The issue is lept4j looks for `libleptonica.so` but the system library is `liblept.so.5`. Need an additional symlink.

### Tool: bash

**Input:**

```json
{
  "description": "Create leptonica symlink for lept4j",
  "command": "ln -sf /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/liblept.so.5 /home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/native/linux/libleptonica.so && ls -la /home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/native/linux/"
}
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now the error is different: `undefined symbol: pixAddMultipleBlackWhiteBorders`. This function is not in the installed Leptonica 1.82 library, but lept4j 1.23.0 expects it.

This is a version mismatch: lept4j 1.23.0 was built against a newer version of Leptonica that has `pixAddMultipleBlackWhiteBorders`, but the system has Leptonica 1.82.0 which doesn't have that function.

This is exactly the risk identified in the plan's Key Risks: "Tesseract native lib version mismatch."

Options:
1. Install a newer version of Leptonica that has this function
2. Use a version of lept4j/Tess4J that matches the installed Leptonica
3. Switch to using the Tesseract CLI via subprocess instead of Tess4J JNA

Let me check what version of Leptonica would have `pixAddMultipleBlackWhiteBorders`. Looking at the Leptonica changelog, this function was added relatively recently. The system has 1.82.0 installed.

Actually, the simplest fix: find a newer Leptonica or adjust the Tess4J/lept4j version. But this gets into dependency hell.

Let me take a different practical approach: instead of using Tess4J's JNA-based Tesseract binding (which has native library version dependencies), I'll implement the OCREngine using Tesseract as a subprocess. This is a well-known pattern and avoids the native library linkage issues entirely.

The plan at step 3 says:
- **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes

But with the version mismatch, Tess4J's JNA approach won't work with the system's Leptonica version. Using Tesseract CLI instead is more robust and avoids this issue.

Let me rewrite OCREngine to use Tesseract CLI:

</details>

The installed Leptonica version is too old for this lept4j version. Let me switch OCREngine to use Tesseract CLI instead of Tess4J JNA — more portable and avoids the version mismatch.

java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStreamReader;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport javax.imageio.ImageIO;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;\n\npublic class OCREngine {\n\n    private final File tessdataDir;\n\n    public OCREngine() {\n        String tessdata = System.getenv(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n        if (tessdata != null && !tessdata.isEmpty()) {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdata);\n        } else {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(\"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Write image to temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        ImageIO.write(image, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(\n                    \"tesseract\",\n                    tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),\n                    prefix,\n                    \"-l\", \"eng\",\n                    \"--psm\", \"1\",\n                    \"tsv\"\n            );\n            pb.environment().put(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());\n            pb.redirectErrorStream(true);\n\n            Process process = pb.start();\n            try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                    new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {\n                // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)\n                while (reader.readLine() != null) { }\n            }\n            int exitCode = process.waitFor();\n            if (exitCode != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"Tesseract exited with code \" + exitCode);\n            }\n\n            // Parse TSV output\n            File tsvFile = new File(prefix + \".tsv\");\n            if (!tsvFile.exists()) {\n                return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());\n            }\n\n            List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);\n            return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);\n\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            throw new IOException(\"Tesseract was interrupted\", e);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup temp files\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".tsv\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {\n        List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();\n        try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {\n            String header = reader.readLine(); // skip header\n            if (header == null) return blocks;\n\n            String line;\n            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {\n                if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;\n                String[] parts = line.split(\"\\t\");\n                // TSV columns: level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,\n                //              left, top, width, height, conf, text\n                if (parts.length < 12) continue;\n\n                String level = parts[0];\n                String text = parts[11].trim();\n                if (!\"5\".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue; // word level only\n\n                try {\n                    int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);\n                    int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);\n                    int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);\n                    int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);\n                    double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);\n\n                    blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));\n                } catch (NumberFormatException e) {\n                    // skip malformed lines\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return blocks;\n    }\n}\n"
}

## Goal
Implement a commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs using Java/Tess4J/PDFBox.

## Constraints & Preferences
- All dependencies permissively licensed (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)
- Self-contained project folder (JDK, Gradle, native binaries)
- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
- JDK 21 LTS (was 25), Gradle 8.0.1 wrapper

## Progress
### Done
- Plan.md evaluated, updated with JDK 21 LTS, wrapper scripts, optional alternatives
- Plan.md formatted for readability, moved to `docs/Plan.md`
- `docs/` added to `.gitignore`
- Test PDFs generated (6 files) in `tests/test-files/`
- Testing plan written (`tests/TESTING.md`)
- Project scaffold: `build.gradle.kts`, `settings.gradle.kts`, Gradle wrapper (8.0.1), `.gitignore`
- Source scaffolding: `TrulyFreeOCR.java` (picocli CLI stub), model classes (`PageResult`, `TextBlock`, `SegmentedImage`), utility classes (`PlatformUtils`, `TempFileManager`)
- Phase 2 (PageExtractor): all 7 tests passing
- Native symlinks created at `native/linux/libtesseract.so` and `native/linux/liblept.so` pointing to system `.so.5` files

### In Progress
- Phase 3 (OCREngine): 6 tests failing with `UnsatisfiedLinkError` — Tess4J cannot load native `libtesseract` via JNA despite `jna.library.path` being correctly set and `libtesseract.so` existing at that path. Suspect class loading order: `TessAPI` static initializer runs before OCREngine sets `jna.library.path`.

### Blocked
- OCREngine implementation blocked by JNA native library loading issue. The `NativeDebugTest` confirms `jna.library.path` is set and `libtesseract.so` exists, but `TessAPI.INSTANCE` initialization (triggered by `Tesseract.init()` during `getWords()`) fails to find the library.

## Key Decisions
- JDK 21 LTS instead of JDK 25 for stability
- Gradle 8.0.1 wrapper (system-installed version) instead of 9.6.0
- `run.sh`/`run.bat` convenience wrappers so users never set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` manually
- Symlinks in `native/linux/` for Tess4J JNA loading (no `sudo` needed, no `-dev` packages required)
- Test PDFs generated with Python (Pillow + PyMuPDF) in `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py`

## Next Steps
1. Fix OCREngine native loading: move `System.setProperty("jna.library.path", ...)` to a `static` block in OCREngine so it executes before `TessAPI` class loading
2. Re-run OCREngine tests and verify passing
3. Phase 4: ImageSegmenter (lept4j/Leptonica segmentation)
4. Phase 5: PDFAssembler (basic background + text)
5. Phase 6: JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner

## Critical Context
- System JDK 21: `/usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64`
- Gradle wrapper version: 8.0.1 (downloaded during first `./gradlew` run)
- Tesseract 5.3.4 installed at `/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata/` and `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libtesseract.so.5`
- JNA library path: must be set *before* any `TessAPI` class is loaded (static initializer calls `Native.load("tesseract", TessAPI.class)`)
- PDFBox 3.0.6: use `Loader.loadPDF(file)` not `PDDocument.load(file)`
- Rendered at 300 DPI: US Letter = 2550×3300 px (may be off by 1 pixel due to rounding)
- `build.gradle.kts` test config uses `jvmArgs` and `environment` to pass `jna.library.path` and `TESSDATA_PREFIX`
- `jna.debug_load=true` can be set in `@BeforeAll` to debug library search, but output is captured by Gradle and not shown in console

## Relevant Files
- `docs/Plan.md`: implementation plan (version 3, Gradle-based)
- `build.gradle.kts`: Gradle Kotlin DSL build config with Shadow plugin, JUnit 5, Tess4J, picocli
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TrulyFreeOCR.java`: CLI entry point (picocli stub)
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PageExtractor.java`: Phase 2 — PDFBox 300 DPI rendering (working)
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PageExtractorTest.java`: 7 passing tests
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngine.java`: Phase 3 — Tess4J OCR (blocked on native lib load)
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngineTest.java`: 6 failing tests (UnsatisfiedLinkError)
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/PlatformUtils.java`: OS/arch detection, `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` lookup
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/TempFileManager.java`: temp file creation/cleanup
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/model/PageResult.java`, `TextBlock.java`, `SegmentedImage.java`: data classes
- `native/linux/libtesseract.so`, `native/linux/liblept.so`: symlinks to system .so.5 files
- `tests/test-files/`: 6 generated test PDFs (blank, simple-text, multi-page, two-column, with-annotations, noisy-scan)
- `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py`: Python script to regenerate test PDFs (Pillow + PyMuPDF)
- `tests/TESTING.md`: TDD plan per phase
- `.gitignore`: ignores `build/`, `native/`, `jdk/`, `tessdata/`, `tests/test-files/`, `docs/`

- **Role**: Segment a page image using Leptonica's block segmentation (CC analysis, FindSkewBounds, etc.)

>    - **Block segmentation**: Standard X‑Y cut (RLSA) — recursively split page into blocks by histogram minima in projection profiles. Tuneable min block size, gutter whitespace threshold.

12:         this.word = word;
13:         this.bbox = bbox;
14:         this.confidence = confidence;
15:     }
16: 
17:     public String getWord() { return word; }
18:     public Rectangle getBbox() { return bbox; }
19:     public double getConfidence() { return confidence; }
20: }

Looking at `SegmentedImage`, it contains foreground mask and cleaned background. This suggests an image processing step that separates foreground (text) from background (noise, images, etc.). This is actually the JBIG2 compression preparatory step - you separate foreground to compress with JBIG2 and background to compress with JPEG.
