<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages
5: **Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
6: ## Constraints
7: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)
8: 2. Prefer Java over Python when possible
9: 3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.
10: 4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
11: ## 1. Project Structure
12: ```
13: TrulyFreeOCR/
14: ├── build.gradle.kts            # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)
15: ├── settings.gradle.kts          # Project name
16: ├── gradlew                      # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)
17: ├── gradlew.bat                  # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)
18: ├── gradle/
19: │   └── wrapper/                 # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties
20: ├── README.md
21: ├── NOTICE.md                    # License attributions
22: ├── native/                      # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)
23: │   ├── win/
24: │   │   └── jbig2enc.exe
25: │   ├── linux/
26: │   │   ├── jbig2enc
27: │   │   ├── libtesseract.so      # Bundled by bootstrap
28: │   │   └── liblept.so
29: │   └── mac/
30: │       ├── jbig2enc
31: │       ├── libtesseract.dylib   # Bundled by bootstrap
32: │       └── liblept.dylib
33: ├── jdk/                         # OpenJDK 21 LTS (installed by install-deps.sh)
34: │   └── {os}/                    # Platform-specific JDK archive
35: ├── tessdata/                    # Tesseract language data files
36: │   └── eng.traineddata
37: ├── install-deps.sh              # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)
38: ├── run.sh                       # Convenience wrapper (Unix)
39: ├── run.bat                      # Convenience wrapper (Windows)
40: ├── bootstrap.sh                 # End-user dependency installer (Unix)
41: ├── bootstrap.bat                # End-user dependency installer (Windows)
42: └── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/
43:     ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java        # CLI entry point (picocli)
44:     ├── pipeline/
45:     │   ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java  # Coordinates all 6 steps
46:     │   ├── PageExtractor.java         # Step 1: PDF page to images
47:     │   ├── ImageSegmenter.java        # Step 2: lept4j normalization
48:     │   ├── JBIG2Compressor.java       # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess
49:     │   ├── OCREngine.java             # Step 4: Tess4J OCR
50:     │   ├── PDFAssembler.java          # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly
51:     │   └── MetadataPreserver.java     # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments
52:     ├── model/
53:     │   ├── PageResult.java
54:     │   ├── TextBlock.java
55:     │   └── SegmentedImage.java
56:     └── util/
57:         ├── PlatformUtils.java         # OS detection, native paths
58:         ├── SubprocessRunner.java
59:         └── TempFileManager.java       # Cleanup temp files
60: ```
61: ## 2. Self-Contained Dependencies Strategy
62: ### Gradle Dependencies (in build.gradle.kts)
63: Only 2 direct deps needed — Tess4J brings everything else transitively:
64: | Direct Dep | Transitive Deps It Brings | License |
65: |---|---|---|
66: | **`net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0`** | PDFBox 3.x, pdfbox-tools, jbig2-imageio, JNA, commons-io, **lept4j**, jboss-vfs, jai-imageio-core, slf4j-api | Apache 2.0 |
67: | **`info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6`** | (none) | Apache 2.0 |
68: ### Native/Bundled Dependencies
69: | Dep | Bundled How | License |
70: |---|---|---|
71: | **OpenJDK 21 LTS** | Downloaded by `install-deps.sh`/`bootstrap.sh` into `jdk/{os}/` | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception |
72: | **Gradle** | Auto-downloaded by Gradle Wrapper (`gradlew`) on first build | Apache 2.0 |
73: | **jbig2enc** | Pre-compiled binary in `native/{os}/jbig2enc` | Apache 2.0 |
74: | **Tesseract + Leptonica (Windows)** | Already bundled inside Tess4J JAR as DLLs | Apache 2.0 |
75: | **Tesseract + Leptonica (macOS/Linux)** | `.so`/`.dylib` in `native/{os}/` installed by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
76: | **Tesseract language data** | `tessdata/eng.traineddata` downloaded by bootstrap | Apache 2.0 |
77: ### All License Checks Passed
78: | Dependency | License | Business-Friendly? |
79: |---|---|---|
80: | Tess4J | Apache 2.0 | |
81: | JNA | Apache 2.0 / LGPL 2.1 (dual) | |
82: | jai-imageio-core | BSD 3-clause | |
83: | PDFBox | Apache 2.0 | |
84: | pdfbox-tools | Apache 2.0 | |
85: | jbig2-imageio | Apache 2.0 | (decode only) |
86: | commons-io | Apache 2.0 | |
87: | lept4j | Apache 2.0 | |
88: | jboss-vfs | Apache 2.0 | |
89: | slf4j-api | MIT | |
90: | picocli | Apache 2.0 | |
91: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | |
92: | Gradle | Apache 2.0 | |
93: | jbig2enc | Apache 2.0 | |
94: | Tesseract (native) | Apache 2.0 | |
95: | Leptonica (native) | BSD 2-clause | |
96: ### install-deps.sh (Developer Setup)
97: `install-deps.sh` is for developers who want to build the project locally:
98: 1. Detect OS (`mac`/`linux`/`win`) and arch (`x64`/`aarch64`)
99: 2. Download **OpenJDK 21 LTS** (Eclipse Temurin `21.0.x`) to `jdk/{os}/` — unpack, no install needed
100: 3. Download **Gradle 9.6.0** binary zip to a temp directory
101: 4. Generate Gradle Wrapper files using bundled JDK:
102:    ```bash
103:    $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle-9.6.0/lib/gradle-launcher-9.6.0.jar wrapper --gradle-version 9.6.0
104:    ```
105: 5. Remove temporary Gradle install
106: 6. Set `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped — does not pollute global `JAVA_HOME`) and generate `run.sh` / `run.bat` convenience wrappers
107: 7. Download pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform
108: 8. Verify with `$TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -version`
109: ### bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat (End-User Setup)
110: `bootstrap.sh` / `bootstrap.bat` handles everything for end users:
111: 1. Detects OS and downloads **OpenJDK 21 LTS** to `jdk/{os}/`
112: 2. Downloads pre-built `jbig2enc` binary for current platform
113: 3. On macOS: `brew install tesseract`, copy `.dylib` files to `native/mac/`
114: 4. On Linux: `apt-get install libtesseract-dev libleptonica-dev`, copy `.so` files to `native/linux/`
115: 5. Downloads `eng.traineddata` to `tessdata/`
116: 6. **Gradle is NOT needed** — `gradlew` is checked into the repo and auto-downloads Gradle on first build
117: 7. Sets `TFOCR_JAVA_HOME` (project-scoped) and creates `run.sh` / `run.bat` wrapper scripts that source it automatically — users never set it manually.
118: **Deliverable**: Shaded fat-JAR via Gradle Shadow Plugin + `jdk/` + `native/` + `tessdata/` + `gradlew` + `run.sh`/`run.bat` — single directory, fully portable. No host prerequisites beyond a shell and curl/wget.
119: ## 3. 6-Step Pipeline
120: ```
121: Input PDF (may have bookmarks/links/comments)
122:        |
123:        v
124: PageExtractor (Step 1) — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI
125:        |
126:        v
127: ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — lept4j (Leptonica via JNA):
128:   pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()
129:   pixBackgroundNorm()          -> white background
130:   pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()   -> binarize
131:   Extract: foreground mask + cleaned BG
132:        |
133:    ----+----
134:    |         |
135:    v         v
136: JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine       Steps 3 & 4 (parallel)
137: (Step 3)     (Step 4)
138:    |         |
139:    | native  | Tess4J
140:    | jbig2enc| word-level bboxes
141:    | glyph   |
142:    | dict    |
143:    |         |
144:    ----+-----
145:        |
146:        v
147: PDFAssembler (Step 5)
148:   JPEG background + JBIG2 text mask + invisible OCR text
149:        |
150:        v
151: MetadataPreserver (Step 6)
152:   Bookmarks, annotations, metadata from source PDF
153:        |
154:        v
155: Output PDF (searchable, compressed, original structure preserved)
156: ```
157: ## 4. Component Details
158: ### PageExtractor (Step 1)
159: - **Input**: PDDocument
160: - **Process**: PDFBox PDFRenderer renders each page at 300 DPI -> BufferedImage
161: - **Output**: `List<PageImage>` (one per page with dimensions)
162: ### ImageSegmenter (Step 2) — lept4j instead of JavaCV/OpenCV
163: - **Input**: BufferedImage -> Leptonica Pix
164: - **Process**:
165:   - `pixConvertRGBToGrayscale()` -> grayscale
166:   - `pixBackgroundNorm()` / `pixBackgroundNormMorph()` -> normalize BG to white
167:   - `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` / `pixSauvolaOnContrastNorm()` -> adaptive binarization
168:   - `pixThresholdToBinary()` -> text mask
169: - **Output**: SegmentedImage (binary foreground mask + cleaned background)
170: ### JBIG2Compressor (Step 3)
171: - **Process**: Write temp PNG -> invoke `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` -> read .jbig2 output
172: - **Fallback**: CCITT Group 4 fax encoding (PDFBox native)
173: ### OCREngine (Step 4)
174: - **Process**: Tess4J ITesseract with page iterator for word-level bounding boxes
175: - **Output**: PageResult with `TextBlock(word, bbox, confidence)` list
176: ### PDFAssembler (Step 5)
177: - Per-page: `PDPage` -> JPEG background `PDImageXObject` -> JBIG2 mask with JBIG2Decode filter -> invisible text (rendering mode 3) at OCR bboxes
178: ### MetadataPreserver (Step 6)
179: - Copy `PDDocumentOutline` (bookmarks), `PDPageAnnotation` (links/comments), `PDDocumentInformation`, XML metadata
180: ## 5. Build Configuration
181: ### Gradle (build.gradle.kts)
182: ```kotlin
183: plugins {
184:     application
185:     id("com.github.johnrengelman.shadow") version "8.1.1"
186: }
187: 
188: group = "com.trulyfreeocr"
189: version = "1.0.0"
190: 
191: java {
192:     toolchain {
193:         languageVersion = JavaLanguageVersion.of(21)
194:     }
195: }
196: 
197: repositories {
198:     mavenCentral()
199: }
200: 
201: dependencies {
202:     implementation("net.sourceforge.tess4j:tess4j:5.18.0")
203:     implementation("info.picocli:picocli:4.7.6")
204:     annotationProcessor("info.picocli:picocli-codegen:4.7.6")
205: }
206: 
207: application {
208:     mainClass = "com.trulyfreeocr.TrulyFreeOCR"
209: }
210: 
211: tasks {
212:     shadowJar {
213:         archiveBaseName.set("trulyfreeocr")
214:         archiveClassifier.set("")
215:         archiveVersion.set("")
216:     }
217:     build {
218:         dependsOn(shadowJar)
219:     }
220: }
221: ```
222: ### Gradle Plugins
223: - `com.github.johnrengelman.shadow` — fat JAR (replaces maven-shade-plugin)
224: - `application` — run task with `mainClass`
225: - `picocli-codegen` (annotationProcessor) — picocli command reflection
226: ## 6. Implementation Order
227: | Phase | Component | Notes |
228: |---|---|---|
229: | 1 | install-deps.sh + Gradle scaffold + CLI (picocli) | Foundation |
230: | 2 | PageExtractor | Simple PDFBox rendering |
231: | 3 | OCREngine | Tess4J — easiest to verify |
232: | 4 | ImageSegmenter | lept4j/Leptonica |
233: | 5 | PDFAssembler (basic: background + text) | Uncompressed first |
234: | 6 | JBIG2Compressor + PlatformUtils + SubprocessRunner | Bundle native jbig2enc |
235: | 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Optimized compression |
236: | 8 | MetadataPreserver | Bookmarks, annotations |
237: | 9 | bootstrap.sh / bootstrap.bat | Self-contained setup |
238: | 10 | Integration tests + sample PDFs | Validation |
239: ## 7. Cross-Platform Native Support
240: | Component | Windows | Linux | macOS |
241: |---|---|---|---|---|
242: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | `jdk/win/` | `jdk/linux/` | `jdk/mac/` |
243: | Gradle | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) | `gradlew` (auto-downloads) |
244: | PDFBox (pure Java) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR |
245: | Tess4J (JNA) | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR | Fat-JAR |
246: | Tesseract native | In Tess4J JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` |
247: | Leptonica native | In lept4j JAR | `native/linux/` | `native/mac/` |
248: | jbig2enc | `native/win/jbig2enc.exe` | `native/linux/jbig2enc` | `native/mac/jbig2enc` |
249: | Bootstrapper | `bootstrap.bat` | `bootstrap.sh` | `bootstrap.sh` |
250: ## 8. CLI Usage
251: ```bash
252: # 0. Developer setup (one-time):
253: ./install-deps.sh    # Downloads: OpenJDK 21 LTS, jbig2enc, generates Gradle wrapper
254: 
255: # 1. Build (uses bundled JDK + Gradle wrapper):
256: # macOS:
257: jdk/mac/jdk-21.0.x/Contents/Home/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
258: # Linux:
259: jdk/linux/jdk-21.0.x/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
260: # Windows:
261: jdk\win\jdk-21.0.x\bin\java -jar gradle\wrapper\gradle-wrapper.jar build
262: 
263: # Or with TFOCR_JAVA_HOME set:
264: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar build
265: 
266: # 2. Run (uses bundled JDK):
267: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf -o output.pdf
268: 
269: # With explicit paths (auto-detected by default):
270: $TFOCR_JAVA_HOME/bin/java -jar build/libs/trulyfreeocr.jar input.pdf \
271:     --native-dir ./native \
272:     --tessdata-dir ./tessdata \
273:     -o output.pdf
274: 
275: # 3. Convenience wrapper (sets TFOCR_JAVA_HOME automatically):
276: ./run.sh input.pdf -o output.pdf         # Unix
277: ./run.bat input.pdf -o output.pdf        # Windows
278: 
279: # 4. End-user bootstrap (no host prerequisites beyond shell + curl/wget):
280: ./bootstrap.sh                            # Unix
281: bootstrap.bat                            # Windows
282: # Then use run.sh / run.bat for build and execution
283: ```
284: ## 9. Key Risks & Mitigations
285: | Risk | Mitigation |
286: |---|---|
287: | **No pure Java JBIG2 encoder** — jbig2-imageio is decode-only | Bundle native jbig2enc binary per platform; fallback CCITT G4 or deflate |
288: | **Tess4J bundles Windows DLLs only** — macOS/Linux need manual native libs | Bootstrap installs & copies .so/.dylib; pin exact Tesseract version |
289: | **Tesseract native lib version mismatch** | Pin version in bootstrap; document build procedure |
290: | **jbig2enc cross-platform binary compatibility** | Pre-built binaries in repo; CI builds for Win/Mac/Linux. **Option**: use statically-linked jbig2enc (Alpine Docker build) to avoid dynamic-linking issues across Linux distros |
291: | **lept4j API coverage uncertainty** — `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()` may not be exposed | Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA `NativeLibrary` direct binding; or pure-Java binarization. ImageSegmenter interface stays the same |
292: | **JBIG2 PDF stream embedding complexity** — PDFBox has no high-level API to embed raw jbig2 data with JBIG2Decode filter | Primary: construct PDF stream manually. **Option**: fall back to CCITT G4 fax encoding (PDFBox native) or deflate compression. PDFAssembler interface stays the same |
293: | **Annotation coordinate mapping** | Test with varied PDFs; use PDFBox transforms |
294: ## 10. Key Findings from Dependency Verification
295: 1. **jbig2-imageio is read-only (decode only)** — confirmed from official Apache PDFBox README. Cannot encode JBIG2 in pure Java.
296: 2. **Tess4J only bundles Windows DLLs** — from Tess4J's own documentation. macOS/Linux users must provide native Tesseract + Leptonica libraries. Bootstrap handles this.
297: 3. **lept4j can replace JavaCV entirely** — Tess4J already depends on lept4j (Leptonica JNA wrapper), which exports `pixBackgroundNorm()`, `pixOtsuAdaptiveThreshold()`, `pixThresholdToBinary()`, etc. — exactly what Step 2 needs. Eliminates ~200MB JavaCV dependency.
298: 4. **All licenses verified** — every dependency (direct and transitive) is Apache 2.0, MIT, or BSD. No AGPL/GPL concerns.
299: ## 11. Legal Considerations for Automated Dependency Downloads
300: The install scripts (`install-deps.sh`, `bootstrap.sh`, Gradle Wrapper) download dependencies from Maven Central, Adoptium API, GitHub Releases, and system package managers without requiring users to visit each project's website.
301: ### Assessment
302: All dependencies use self-executing permissive licenses (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD, GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception) — these grant rights automatically upon use or distribution. No "I Accept" click or website visit is legally required.
303: | Dependency | Source | License | Concern? |
304: |---|---|---|---|
305: | Tess4J, picocli (via Gradle) | Maven Central | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Java practice |
306: | OpenJDK 21 LTS | Adoptium API (`api.adoptium.net`) | GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception | None — API designed for automation |
307: | jbig2enc | GitHub Releases | Apache 2.0 | None — standard binary distribution |
308: | Tesseract/Leptonica | `apt` / `brew` | Apache 2.0 / BSD | None — package managers do this |
309: | tessdata | GitHub (Tesseract-OCR) | Apache 2.0 | None |
310: | Gradle (via Wrapper) | Gradle Services (`services.gradle.org`) | Apache 2.0 | None — standard Gradle practice |
311: ### "Stealing GitHub Stars" Consideration
312: A concern was raised: project owners rely on GitHub stars for visibility, and automated downloads skip visiting their repos.
313: **Why this is not a concern:**
314: 1. **Automated dependency resolution does not rob stars.** Stars are a human social signal — no project expects or receives stars from CI pipelines, package managers, or install scripts. Manually downloading a release zip and starring are unrelated actions.
315: 2. **Most dependencies never touch GitHub at all:**
316:    - Tess4J, picocli → Maven Central (not GitHub)
317:    - Gradle → `services.gradle.org` (not GitHub)
318:    - OpenJDK → Adoptium API (not GitHub)
319:    - Tesseract/Leptonica → `apt` / `brew` (not GitHub)
320: 3. **Only jbig2enc hits GitHub Releases** — the same pattern used by Homebrew, Docker, and every package manager that fetches release artifacts. Nobody considers those projects to be "stealing stars."
321: 4. **Universal industry standard.** Every Maven project, npm package, Homebrew formula, and Docker image works this way. If automated downloads were "stealing," the entire open-source ecosystem would be guilty.
322: **Optional nicety**: A credit line in the CLI output or README can drive awareness:```Powered by Tesseract OCR (github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract)```
323: **Verdict**: Not stealing. Stars come from human engagement, not `curl` scripts.
324: ### Mitigation
325: `NOTICE.md` will list every dependency with its exact version, license, and copyright notice to satisfy Apache 2.0 §4 and BSD attribution requirements.
326: ### Verdict
327: No legal concern for publishing on GitHub. Automated downloads from Maven Central, GitHub Releases, and package managers are standard industry practice used by every CI/CD pipeline and container build.
328: 
329: 

(End of file - total 329 lines)
</content>
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I understand Phase 4 better from the plan. ImageSegmenter (Step 2) should:
- Input: BufferedImage
- Process:
  - Convert to grayscale
  - Background normalization (clean to white)
  - Adaptive thresholding (Otsu or Sauvola) to binarize
  - Separate into: foreground mask (binary) + cleaned background
- Output: SegmentedImage (foregroundMask, cleanedBackground)

But lept4j won't work with our system's Leptonica. The plan's mitigation in section 9 says:
> **lept4j API coverage uncertainty**: Primary: use lept4j exposed API. **Option**: fall back to JNA NativeLibrary direct binding; or pure-Java binarization.

Since we already determined that lept4j's Leptonica1 class has a `pixAddMultipleBlackWhiteBorders` symbol that our system Leptonica doesn't have, even direct JNA binding would fail for that symbol.

So I need pure-Java implementations:
1. Grayscale conversion (simple luminance)
2. Background normalization (estimate background per tile, subtract)
3. Otsu adaptive thresholding
4. Output foreground mask + cleaned background

Let me implement ImageSegmenter with pure Java based on standard image processing algorithms, and then write tests for it.

Let me also look at the test-files to see what images I can use for testing.

Actually, for Phase 4 ImageSegmenter tests, I should test:
1. Non-null output for valid input
2. Foreground mask has same dimensions
3. Cleaned background has same dimensions
4. Foreground is binary (black/white)
5. Background is visually clean (less noise)

Let me implement this.

</details>

Phase 4: ImageSegmenter — pure Java implementation since lept4j's Leptonica version doesn't match the system's.

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now let me implement ImageSegmenter with pure Java algorithms:

1. **Grayscale conversion**: Standard luminance `0.299*R + 0.587*G + 0.114*B`
2. **Background normalization**: Use a tile-based approach similar to Leptonica's `pixBackgroundNorm()`:
   - Divide image into tiles
   - Find min/max in each tile
   - Interpolate to get background estimate
   - Normalize
3. **Otsu's thresholding**: Standard algorithm
4. **Output**: Foreground mask (binary with white text on black bg) + cleaned background

Let me write the implementation.

Actually, let me think about what practical algorithms to use:

**Background normalization**:
- Tile the image into e.g. 64x64 blocks
- For each tile, estimate the background level (e.g., use the mean of the top 10% brightest pixels in the tile, since text is dark on light background)
- Bilinear interpolate between tile centers to get a smooth background surface
- Divide each pixel by the background estimate to normalize

**Binarization**:
- Otsu's method on the normalized image

**Foreground mask**:
- Binary image from thresholding (black text on white background)

**Cleaned background**:
- Original image with text regions smoothed over (inpaint)
- Or simpler: original image with the normalized background as the new background

Actually, let me simplify this. For a practical approach:
1. Convert to grayscale
2. Otsu binarization → foreground mask
3. For cleaned background: dilate the foreground mask to get text region, then apply a median filter in those regions to remove text

For the SegmentedImage output:
- `foregroundMask`: binary image (white text on black bg) 
- `cleanedBackground`: original image with text regions filled/inpainted

Let me keep it practical and implement this step by step.

For the test, I can:
- Test that the segmentation runs without errors
- Test that dimensions are preserved
- Test that the foreground mask is binary (only 0 or 255 values)
- Test that the output is not null
- Test on blank image (should have no foreground)
- Test on simple text image (should have some foreground)

</details>

### Tool: write

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;\n\npublic class ImageSegmenter {\n\n    public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Step 1: Convert to grayscale\n        int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);\n\n        // Step 2: Background normalization\n        int tileSize = 64;\n        double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);\n\n        // Step 3: Otsu binarization\n        int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);\n        BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image\n                // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground\n                if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // black\n                } else {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // white\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color\n        BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, 3);\n\n        return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);\n    }\n\n    private int[] toGrayscale(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n        int[] gray = new int[width * height];\n\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int rgb = image.getRGB(x, y);\n                int r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                int g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                int b = rgb & 0xFF;\n                gray[y * width + x] = (int) (0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b);\n            }\n        }\n        return gray;\n    }\n\n    private double[] backgroundNormalize(int[] gray, int width, int height, int tileSize) {\n        int tilesX = (int) Math.ceil((double) width / tileSize);\n        int tilesY = (int) Math.ceil((double) height / tileSize);\n\n        // Sample background level per tile (95th percentile = bright, since text is dark)\n        double[][] bgLevels = new double[tilesY][tilesX];\n        for (int ty = 0; ty < tilesY; ty++) {\n            for (int tx = 0; tx < tilesX; tx++) {\n                int startX = tx * tileSize;\n                int startY = ty * tileSize;\n                int endX = Math.min(startX + tileSize, width);\n                int endY = Math.min(startY + tileSize, height);\n                int count = (endX - startX) * (endY - startY);\n                int[] sorted = new int[count];\n                int idx = 0;\n                for (int y = startY; y < endY; y++) {\n                    for (int x = startX; x < endX; x++) {\n                        sorted[idx++] = gray[y * width + x];\n                    }\n                }\n                java.util.Arrays.sort(sorted);\n                // 95th percentile as background estimate\n                bgLevels[ty][tx] = sorted[(int) (count * 0.95)];\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Bilinear interpolation of background at each pixel\n        double[] result = new double[gray.length];\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                float fx = (float) x / tileSize;\n                float fy = (float) y / tileSize;\n                int tx0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fx), tilesX - 1);\n                int tx1 = Math.min(tx0 + 1, tilesX - 1);\n                int ty0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fy), tilesY - 1);\n                int ty1 = Math.min(ty0 + 1, tilesY - 1);\n\n                double bg = bilinearInterp(\n                        bgLevels[ty0][tx0], bgLevels[ty0][tx1],\n                        bgLevels[ty1][tx0], bgLevels[ty1][tx1],\n                        fx - tx0, fy - ty0);\n\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                if (bg > 0) {\n                    // Normalize: stretch so that bg -> 255\n                    result[idx] = Math.min(255.0, gray[idx] * 255.0 / bg);\n                } else {\n                    result[idx] = gray[idx];\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    private double bilinearInterp(double v00, double v01, double v10, double v11, double fx, double fy) {\n        double top = v00 + (v01 - v00) * fx;\n        double bot = v10 + (v11 - v10) * fx;\n        return top + (bot - top) * fy;\n    }\n\n    private int otsuThreshold(double[] pixels) {\n        int[] histogram = new int[256];\n        for (double p : pixels) {\n            int val = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, (int) Math.round(p)));\n            histogram[val]++;\n        }\n\n        int total = pixels.length;\n        double sum = 0;\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            sum += i * histogram[i];\n        }\n\n        double sumB = 0;\n        int wB = 0;\n        double maxVariance = 0;\n        int threshold = 0;\n\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            wB += histogram[i];\n            if (wB == 0) continue;\n            int wF = total - wB;\n            if (wF == 0) break;\n\n            sumB += i * histogram[i];\n            double meanB = sumB / wB;\n            double meanF = (sum - sumB) / wF;\n            double variance = (double) wB * wF * (meanB - meanF) * (meanB - meanF);\n\n            if (variance > maxVariance) {\n                maxVariance = variance;\n                threshold = i;\n            }\n        }\n        return threshold;\n    }\n\n    private BufferedImage inpaintBackground(BufferedImage original, BufferedImage mask, int radius) {\n        int width = original.getWidth();\n        int height = original.getHeight();\n        BufferedImage result = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);\n        int[] src = new int[width * height];\n        original.getRGB(0, 0, width, height, src, 0, width);\n\n        // For each pixel in the mask that is foreground (black), fill with nearby background color\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int maskRgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);\n                boolean isForeground = (maskRgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0; // black = foreground\n\n                if (!isForeground) {\n                    result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                } else {\n                    // Find nearest background pixel within radius\n                    int bestR = 0, bestG = 0, bestB = 0;\n                    int found = 0;\n                    for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius && found == 0; dy++) {\n                        for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius && found == 0; dx++) {\n                            int nx = x + dx;\n                            int ny = y + dy;\n                            if (nx < 0 || nx >= width || ny < 0 || ny >= height) continue;\n                            int nmask = mask.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                            if ((nmask & 0xFFFFFF) != 0) { // background pixel\n                                int nrgb = original.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                                bestR = (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                                bestG = (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                                bestB = nrgb & 0xFF;\n                                found++;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if (found > 0) {\n                        int rgb = (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB;\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, rgb);\n                    } else {\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }\n}\n"
}

java.io.IOException;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;\n\nclass ImageSegmenterTest {\n\n    static PageExtractor extractor;\n    static ImageSegmenter segmenter;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_blankPage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\"));\n        SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertNotNull(result.getForegroundMask());\n        assertNotNull(result.getCleanedBackground());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);\n        SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);\n        assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getForegroundMask().getWidth());\n        assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getForegroundMask().getHeight());\n        assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getCleanedBackground().getWidth());\n        assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getCleanedBackground().getHeight());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_foregroundMaskIsBinary() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));\n        BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();\n        assertEquals(BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, mask.getType());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_blankPage_hasLittleForeground() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\"));\n        SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));\n        BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();\n        int width = mask.getWidth();\n        int height = mask.getHeight();\n        int blackPixels = 0;\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {\n                    blackPixels++;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        int total = width * height;\n        assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total < 0.05,\n                \"Blank page should have <5% foreground pixels, got \" +\n                (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + \"%\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_simpleText_hasForegroundPixels() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        SegmentedImage result =        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {\n                    blackPixels++;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        int total = width * height;\n        assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total > 0.001,\n                \"Text page should have >0.1% foreground pixels, got \" +\n                (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + \"%\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void segment_cleanedBackground_reducesTextNoise() throws IOException {\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\"));\n        BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);\n        SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);\n        BufferedImage cleaned = result.getCleanedBackground();\n\n        // Compare cleaned vs original: text areas should be filled in\n        BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();\n        int width = page.getWidth();\n        int height = page.getHeight();\n        int textPixels = 0;\n        int differentPixels = 0;\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {\n                    textPixels++;\n                    int origRgb = page.getRGB(x, y);\n                    int cleanRgb = cleaned.getRGB(x, y);\n                    if (origRgb != cleanRgb) {\n                        differentPixels++;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        // Most foreground pixels should differ (text filled in)\n        assertTrue(textPixels > 0);\n        assertTrue((double) differentPixels / textPixels > 0.5,\n                \"Most foreground pixels should differ between original and cleaned\");\n    }\n}\n",

- Background layer: JPEG-compressed cleaned background image
- Text layer: invisible (rendering mode 3) OCR text

1. Create a new PDDocument

   c. Add invisible text (rendering mode 3) using the OCR TextBlocks at their bounding box positions

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I need to implement PDFAssembler. This creates a new PDF with:
1. Background: JPEG image (the cleaned background)
2. Text: invisible text at OCR bounding boxes

Key PDFBox concepts I need:
- `PDPageContentStream` for drawing
- `PDImageXObject` for embedding images
- `PDDocument` for creating new documents
- Font handling for text rendering
- Transforming pixel coordinates to PDF user space coordinates

The image rendered at 300 DPI on US Letter (8.5 x 11 inches) gives 2550 x 3300 pixels. In PDF points (72 DPI), US Letter is 612 x 792 points.

So pixel to point conversion: point = pixel * (72 / 300) = pixel * 0.24

Wait, I should double check: at 300 DPI with US Letter (8.5 x 11 in):
- Width in pixels: 8.5 * 300 = 2550
- Height in pixels: 11 * 300 = 3300

PDF user space uses 72 DPI:
- Width in points: 8.5 * 72 = 612
- Height in points: 11 * 72 = 792

So pixel to point conversion factor: 72/300 = 0.24

But wait, actually the source PDF might have different dimensions. I should probably measure the source PDF page dimensions and use those, rather than deriving them from pixel dimensions.

Actually, for the simple case, I should:
1. Get source PDF page size (in points) using PDPage.getMediaBox()
2. Create output page with same dimensions
3. Scale image to fill the page

Let me implement this. The PDFAssembler should:
- Take a source PDF (to copy page dimensions)
- Take list of BufferedImages (cleaned backgrounds)  
- Take list of PageResults (OCR results)
- Output a new PDDocument

Actually, thinking more carefully, the pipeline flow is:
1. Open source PDF, extract pages as images
2. Segment each image (foreground mask + cleaned background)
3. OCR each image
4. Assemble new PDF from: source PDF dimensions, cleaned backgrounds, OCR results

For PDFAssembler, I need to:
1. Create new PDDocument
2. For each page:
   a. Get page dimensions from source PDF
   b. Create new PDPage with same dimensions
   c. Draw JPEG background image
   d. Draw invisible OCR text at correct positions
3. Return PDDocument

Let me also think about font handling. PDFBox needs a font to render text. I should use a standard Type1 font (like Helvetica) since these don't need embedding. The text is invisible (rendering mode 3) so the font choice is cosmetic - it doesn't affect appearance.

Let me write the PDFAssembler:

</details>

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java",
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport javax.imageio.ImageIO;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;\n\npublic class PDFAssembler {\n\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        PDDocument output = new PDDocument();\n\n        try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {\n            int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n                PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);\n                PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();\n\n                BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);\n                PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);\n\n                PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);\n                output.addPage(outPage);\n\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {\n                    // Draw background image scaled to page dimensions\n                    PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);\n                    float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();\n                    float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();\n                    cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n\n                    // Draw invisible OCR text\n                    float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();\n                    float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();\n                    cs.beginText();\n                    cs.setFont(PDType1Font.HELVETICA, 1);\n                    // Rendering mode 3 = invisible (neither fill nor stroke)\n                    cs.setRenderingMode(org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.state.SetRenderingMode.RENDERING_MODE_NEITHER);\n\n                    for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {\n                        // Convert pixel coords to PDF user space\n                        float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;\n                        // PDF y=0 is bottom, image y=0 is top\n                        float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;\n                        float fontSize = tb.getBbox().height * scaleY;\n                        if (fontSize < 1) fontSize = 1;\n                        cs.setFont(PDType1Font.HELVETICA, fontSize);\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);\n                        cs.showText(tb.getWord());\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);\n                    }\n                    cs.endText();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return output;\n    }\n\n    public void save(PDDocument doc, File outputFile) throws IOException {\n        doc.save(outputFile);\n    }\n}\n"
}

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Hmm, I realize the rendering mode constant - I'm not sure about `SetRenderingMode.RENDERING_MODE_NEITHER`. Let me check PDFBox's API for rendering modes.

Actually, in PDFBox, the rendering mode is set via a PDTextState or directly in the content stream. The operator is `Tr`. The modes are:
- 0 = Fill text
- 1 = Stroke text  
- 2 = Fill then stroke
- 3 = Neither (invisible)

In PDFBox, I can set the rendering mode using `PDPageContentStream.setRenderingMode(int mode)`. Let me look at the correct API.

Actually, the rendering mode is set with a method on PDPageContentStream. Let me check:
- `setRenderingMode(int renderingMode)` - takes 0, 1, 2, or 3

But the import `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.state.SetRenderingMode` is wrong - that's the operator class, not the constant.

Let me fix PDFAssembler to use the correct API. I'll just use integer 3 for rendering mode 3 (invisible).

Also, I need to think about the text placement more carefully. When we call `newLineAtOffset(x, y)` it moves the text position relative to the current position. So after showing text, I need to move back. But actually, it's better to use `setTextMatrix` or individual `showText` calls.

Let me rethink the approach:
- `beginText()` / `endText()` wraps text operations
- Inside, we set font once at the beginning
- For each word, we use `newLineAtOffset(x, y)` to position
- Then `showText(word)` to draw
- To go to the next word, we need to reset position, which is tricky with newLineAtOffset since it's relative

Better approach: Use `setTextMatrix` for absolute positioning, or simpler: just set the text matrix for each word individually.

Actually, `PDPageContentStream` has methods:
- `beginText()` / `endText()`
- `setFont(font, fontSize)`
- `newLineAtOffset(x, y)` - moves text line position
- `showText(text)` - draws text at current text position

The text position is a cursor that moves with each operation. After `showText`, the cursor is at the end of the text. To position the next word independently, I could use `setTextMatrix` to set absolute position.

Wait, looking at PDFBox API more carefully:
- There's no `setTextMatrix` in `PDPageContentStream`. Instead there's `addTextMatrix(a, b, c, d, e, f)`.

Actually, I think the cleanest approach is:
```java
cs.beginText();
cs.setFont(PDType1Font.HELVETICA, 1);
cs.setRenderingMode(3);
for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
    float x = ...;
    float y = ...;
    float fontSize = ...;
    cs.setFont(PDType1Font.HELVETICA, fontSize);
    // Move to position (absolute in text space)
    cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
    cs.showText(tb.getWord());
    // Move back to origin for next word
    cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
}
cs.endText();
```

This should work because `newLineAtOffset` is relative, so after showing text at (x, y), we move back to (0, 0) before the next word.

Wait, but `showText` doesn't change the text line position. The text line position is only changed by `newLineAtOffset`. So the sequence:
1. Start at (0, 0)
2. `newLineAtOffset(x, y)` → position becomes (x, y) + (0, 0) = (x, y)
3. `showText("word")` → draws at (x, y), cursor moves to end of "word" but in PDF text rendering, the text matrix changes
4. `newLineAtOffset(-x, -y)` → This adds (-x, -y) to the current text line position

Hmm, actually I think `showText` also affects the text matrix. Let me think about this differently.

In PDF, the text line matrix is separate from the text matrix. `Td` (which `newLineAtOffset` writes) sets the text line matrix and text matrix to new values. `TJ`/`Tj` (which `showText` writes) only affects the text matrix.

So after:
1. `Td x y` → Text matrix = line matrix = (x, y)
2. `Tj (word)` → Text matrix advances by width of "word", line matrix unchanged
3. `Td -x -y` → Text matrix = line matrix = (previous line matrix) + (-x, -y) = (x, y) + (-x, -y) = (0, 0)

Wait that's wrong. `Td` sets the text line matrix to (current line matrix + offset), and the text matrix to the same new value. So:
1. Start: line matrix = text matrix = (0, 0)
2. `Td x y`: line matrix = (0,0) + (x,y) = (x,y), text matrix = (x,y)
3. `Tj "word"`: text matrix = (x + wordWidth, y), line matrix = (x,y)
4. `Td -x -y`: line matrix = (x,y) + (-x,-y) = (0,0), text matrix = (0,0)

So this works! Each word we position and draw independently. Let me keep this approach.

But actually, I should also set the font size to match the text height. The font size in PDF is in points, and it corresponds to the ascent of the font from baseline to top. For a font size of N, the glyphs will be approximately N units tall.

If I have the text block height in pixels, and convert to PDF points (scaleY factor), then `fontSize = tb.getBbox().height * scaleY`. This should roughly match the text height.

However, there's a consideration: if the word is very small, we don't want the font to be microscopic. Setting minimum font size of 1 is reasonable.

Also, I need to use `showText` properly - it requires properly escaped text. For simple text this should be fine.

Let me also fix the import issue.

{
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;\n\nclass PDFAssemblerTest {\n\n    static PageExtractor extractor;\n    static ImageSegmenter segmenter;\n    static OCREngine engine;\n    static PDFAssembler assembler;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();\n        engine = new OCREngine();\n        assembler = new PDFAssembler();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());\n\n        // Segment and OCR each page\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            assertNotNull(output);\n            assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());\n\n            // Verify text is extractable (searchable)\n            PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();\n            String text = stripper.getText(output);\n            assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains(\"quick\"),\n                    \"Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: \" + text);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/multi-page.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        assertEquals(3, pages.size());\n\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void assemble_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/multi-page.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();\n            for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {\n                stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);\n                stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);\n                String text = stripper.getText(output);\n                assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),\n                        \"Page \" + (i + 1) + \" should have extractable text\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void assemble_blankPage_createsPdfWithCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());\n            var mediaBox = output.getPage(0).getMediaBox();\n            assertEquals(612.0f, mediaBox.getWidth(), 0.01);\n            assertEquals(792.0f, mediaBox.getHeight(), 0.01);\n        }\n    }\n}\n",

27:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
28:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
29:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
30:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();

32:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
33:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
34:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
35:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
36:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
37: 
38:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
39:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
40: 
41:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
42:                 output.addPage(outPage);
43: 
44:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
45:                     // Draw background image scaled to page dimensions
46:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
47:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
48:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
49:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
50: 
51:                     // Draw invisible OCR text
52:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
53:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
54:                     cs.beginText();
55:                     cs.setFont(new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA), 1);
56:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
57: 
58:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
59:                         // Convert pixel coords to PDF user space
60:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
61:                         // PDF y=0 is bottom, image y=0 is top
62:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
63:                         float fontSize = tb.getBbox().height * scaleY;
64:                         if (fontSize < 1) fontSize = 1;
65:                         cs.setFont(new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA), fontSize);
66:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
67:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
68:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
69:                     }
70:                     cs.endText();
71:                 }
72:             }
73:         }
74: 
75:         return output;
76:     }
77:

80:     }

Since jbig2enc isn't available, the fallback should be used. The fallback (CCITT G4) can be implemented using PDFBox's CCITTFaxDecode filter.

Actually, let me think about what approach makes sense. CCITT G4 encoding in pure Java is complex. A simpler approach: use PDFBox's built-in image compression. PDFBox can compress images with deflate (PNG-style) or JPEG. For binary images, the best available option in pure Java is:
1. CCITT G4 - needs manual PDF stream construction
2. Deflate - available via `PDImageXObject` or the `CompressedObject` approach

Let me implement both components.

For SubprocessRunner, it's a simple utility class:
- `run(File executable, String... args)` - runs a command
- Returns stdout as byte[]
- Captures stderr
- Timeout support

For JBIG2Compressor:
- `compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask)` - produces compressed JBIG2 data or CCITT G4 data
- For the pure Java fallback, I can use a simple approach: just return the raw image data compressed with deflate. This won't be as good as JBIG2 but is functional.
- Actually, CCITT G4 encoding is complex. Let me use a simpler approach: just pass through the binary image as a deflate-compressed CCITT G4 or use PDFBox's built-in support.

Actually, the simplest fallback approach that's still useful: compress the binary mask using java.util.zip.Deflater and wrap it in a format that PDFBox can embed. PDFBox's `PDImageXObject` can create CCITT-encoded images from a BufferedImage if the image type is binary and we handle the encoding.

`CCITTFactory.createFromImage(PDDocument, BufferedImage)` exists in PDFBox 3.x! This can create a CCITT Group 4 encoded `PDImageXObject` from a BufferedImage. That's perfect for the fallback.

Now let me implement SubprocessRunner and JBIG2Compressor.

For JBIG2Compressor:
- Input: BufferedImage (foreground mask - binary)
- Try: run `native/{os}/jbig2enc -p -s` via subprocess
- Fallback: use `CCITTFactory.createFromImage()` to create CCITT G4 encoded image
- Output: something that PDFAssembler can use

But wait, the JBIG2Compressor output needs to be used by PDFAssembler. Currently PDFAssembler takes `List<BufferedImage>` backgrounds and `List<PageResult>` OCR results. Phase 7 would add JBIG2 mask integration.

For Phase 6, I just need to implement the compressor itself, not integrate it yet. The integration happens in Phase 7.

Let me implement SubprocessRunner and JBIG2Compressor with tests.

</details>

{
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.util;\n\nimport java.io.BufferedReader;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStreamReader;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\n\npublic class SubprocessRunner {\n\n    public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {\n        return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);\n    }\n\n    public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {\n        List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();\n        command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());\n        command.addAll(args);\n\n        ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);\n        pb.redirectErrorStream(false);\n\n        Process process = pb.start();\n\n        if (timeoutMs > 0) {\n            try {\n                boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n                if (!finished) {\n                    process.destroyForcibly();\n                    throw new IOException(\"Process timed out after \" + timeoutMs + \"ms: \" + executable.getName());\n                }\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        } else {\n            try {\n                process.waitFor();\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();\n        byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();\n        int exitCode = process.exitValue();\n\n        return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);\n    }\n\n    public static class Result {\n        private final int exitCode;\n        private final byte[] stdout;\n        private final byte[] stderr;\n\n        public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {\n            this.exitCode = exitCode;\n            this.stdout = stdout;\n            this.stderr = stderr;\n        }\n\n        public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }\n        public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }\n        public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }\n        public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }\n        public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }\n    }\n}\n",

{
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.IOException;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;\n\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils.Os;\nimport com.trulyfreeocr.util.SubprocessRunner;\n\npublic class JBIG2Compressor {\n\n    public static class CompressionResult {\n        private final boolean isJbig2;\n        private final byte[] data;\n        private final int width;\n        private final int height;\n\n        public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {\n            this.data = data;\n            this.width = width;\n            this.height = height;\n            this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;\n        }\n\n        public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }\n        public byte[] getData() { return data; }\n        public int getWidth() { return width; }\n        public int getHeight() { return height; }\n    }\n\n    public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {\n        // Try jbig2enc first\n        File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();\n        if (jbig2enc != null) {\n            try {\n                return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // Fall through to CCITT fallback\n            }\n        }\n        // Fallback\n        return compressWithCcitt(foregroundMask);\n    }\n\n    private File findJbig2enc() {\n        String nativeDir = System.getProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\", \"native\");\n        Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();\n        String dirName;\n        switch (os) {\n            case WINDOWS: dirName = \"win\"; break;\n            case MAC: dirName = \"mac\"; break;\n            default: dirName = \"linux\";\n        }\n        File exe = new File(nativeDir + \"/\" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? \"jbig2enc.exe\" : \"jbig2enc\");\n        if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Write mask as PNG temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-jbig2-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            File outputDir = tempPng.getParentFile();\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n\n            SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,\n                    \"-p\", \"-s\", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());\n            if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"jbig2enc failed: \" + result.getStderrString());\n            }\n\n            // jbig2enc produces output.sym and output.jbig2 files\n            File symFile = new File(prefix + \".sym\");\n            File jbig2File = new File(prefix + \".jbig2\");\n\n            byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n            byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n\n            // Combine: global (sym) + page data (jbig2)\n            byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];\n            System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);\n            System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);\n\n            return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".sym\", \".jbig2\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private CompressionResult compressWithCcitt(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Use PDFBox CCITTFactory to encode as Group 4 fax\n        // Create a temporary PDDocument just for encoding\n        try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {\n            PDImageXObject ccittImage = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);\n            byte[] data = ccittImage.getCOSObject().toByteArray();\n            return new CompressionResult(data, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/JBIG2Compressor.java"
}

{
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\nimport java.io.IOException;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\nclass JBIG2CompressorTest {\n\n    static JBIG2Compressor compressor;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        compressor = new JBIG2Compressor();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void compress_blankImage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertNotNull(result.getData());\n        assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void compress_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(200, 300, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);\n        assertEquals(200, result.getWidth());\n        assertEquals(300, result.getHeight());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void compress_allWhite_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        // Fill with white (background)\n        for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {\n                img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);\n            }\n        }\n        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void compress_withForegroundPixels_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        // Fill with white\n        for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {\n                img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);\n            }\n        }\n        // Add some black pixels (foreground)\n        for (int x = 10; x < 90; x++) {\n            img.setRGB(x, 50, 0xFF000000);\n        }\n        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void compress_fallbackToCcitt_whenJbig2encUnavailable() throws IOException {\n        BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(50, 50, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        // Remove jbig2enc from path by not setting tfocr.native.dir\n        System.clearProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\");\n        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);\n        assertNotNull(result);\n        assertFalse(result.isJbig2(), \"Should fallback to CCITT when jbig2enc unavailable\");\n    }\n}\n",

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

OK, `getCOSObject()` returns COSDictionary (parent of COSStream). But I can't call `toByteArray()` on it. Let me use a different approach: for the fallback, I'll encode the image as a compressed PNG and return those bytes, or use the `javax.imageio.ImageIO` to write to a ByteArrayOutputStream. That's the simplest approach. The Phase 7 PDFAssembler integration will handle proper encoding.

Let me also check if there's an `ImageIO` method for CCITT. No, that's not how it works.

Simplest fix: for the CCITT fallback, write the binary mask as a PNG to a byte array and return that. It's simple, standard, and works. The actual PDF compression optimization happens in Phase 7.

</details>

Completed Phases 4–6. Here's the summary of this session:

**Phase 3 fix** (carry-over from last time): OCREngine switched from Tess4J JNA to Tesseract CLI subprocess because lept4j's Leptonica symbol `pixAddMultipleBlackWhiteBorders` doesn't exist in system Leptonica 1.82. All 6 tests pass now.

**Phase 4 — ImageSegmenter**: Pure-Java implementation (no Leptonica dependency needed): grayscale conversion → tile-based background normalization → Otsu binarization → foreground/background separation with inpainting. 6 tests pass.

**Phase 5 — PDFAssembler**: Creates searchable PDFs from cleaned background images + OCR results. Uses PDFBox `LosslessFactory` for background image, invisible text (rendering mode 3) with accurate positioning. 4 tests pass including multi-page preservation and text extractability.

**Phase 6 — JBIG2Compressor + SubprocessRunner**: 
- `SubprocessRunner` — generic subprocess utility with stdout/stderr capture, timeout support
- `JBIG2Compressor` — tries `jbig2enc` subprocess (not available on this system), falls back to PNG byte array output (Phase 7 will integrate proper CCITT G4)
- `PlatformUtils` — already existed, 3 smoke tests passing
- 8 tests pass across both new classes

**Full suite**: 35 tests, all passing, 0 failures.

Remaining phases per plan: Phase 7 (JBIG2+PDFAssembler integration), Phase 8 (MetadataPreserver), Phase 9 (bootstrap scripts), Phase 10 (integration tests). Continue?

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

The magic numbers on line 53 are the standard ITU-R BT.601 luma coefficients for converting RGB to grayscale. The formula is:

`Y = 0.299 * R + 0.587 * G + 0.114 * B`

This is the standard luminance formula used in:
- JPEG image compression
- NTSC/PAL television standards
- Color image processing everywhere

The coefficients represent the human eye's sensitivity to different colors:
- 0.299 for Red (least sensitive)
- 0.587 for Green (most sensitive - human eyes have more cone cells sensitive to green)
- 0.114 for Blue (least sensitive - eyes have few blue-sensitive cones)

The weights sum to 1.0, so for a neutral gray (R=G=B), the result equals that value.

</details>

Line 53 uses the **ITU-R BT.601 luma coefficients** (`0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`), the standard formula to convert RGB to grayscale. They reflect human perceptual sensitivity: humans are most sensitive to green (0.587), moderately to red (0.299), and least to blue (0.114). This is the same formula used in JPEG, NTSC/PAL, and virtually all image processing libraries.

42:     private int[] toGrayscale(BufferedImage image) {
43:         int width = image.getWidth();
44:         int height = image.getHeight();
45:         int[] gray = new int[width * height];
46: 
47:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
48:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
49:                 int rgb = image.getRGB(x, y);
50:                 int r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;
51:                 int g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;
52:                 int b = rgb & 0xFF;
53:                 gray[y * width + x] = (int) (0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b);
54:             }
55:         }
56:         return gray;
57:     }
58: 
59:     private double[] backgroundNormalize(int[] gray, int width, int height, int tileSize) {
60:         int tilesX = (int) Math.ceil((double) width / tileSize);
61:         int tilesY = (int) Math.ceil((double) height / tileSize);
62: 
63:         // Sample background level per tile (95th percentile = bright, since text is dark)
64:         double[][] bgLevels = new double[tilesY][tilesX];
65:         for (int ty = 0; ty < tilesY; ty++) {
66:             for (int tx = 0; tx < tilesX; tx++) {
67:                 int startX = tx * tileSize;
68:                 int startY = ty * tileSize;
69:                 int endX = Math.min(startX + tileSize, width);
70:                 int endY = Math.min(startY + tileSize, height);
71:                 int count = (endX - startX) * (endY - startY);
72:                 int[] sorted = new int[count];
73:                 int idx = 0;
74:                 for (int y = startY; y < endY; y++) {
75:                     for (int x = startX; x < endX; x++) {
76:                         sorted[idx++] = gray[y * width + x];
77:                     }
78:                 }
79:                 java.util.Arrays.sort(sorted);
80:                 // 95th percentile as background estimate
81:                 bgLevels[ty][tx] = sorted[(int) (count * 0.95)];
82:             }
83:         }
84: 
85:         // Bilinear interpolation of background at each pixel
86:         double[] result = new double[gray.length];
87:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
88:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
89:                 float fx = (float) x / tileSize;
90:                 float fy = (float) y / tileSize;
91:                 int tx0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fx), tilesX - 1);
92:                 int tx1 = Math.min(tx0 + 1, tilesX - 1);
93:                 int ty0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fy), tilesY - 1);
94:                 int ty1 = Math.min(ty0 + 1, tilesY - 1);
95: 
96:                 double bg = bilinearInterp(
97:                         bgLevels[ty0][tx0], bgLevels[ty0][tx1],
98:                         bgLevels[ty1][tx0], bgLevels[ty1][tx1],
99:                         fx - tx0, fy - ty0);
100: 
101:                 int idx = y * width + x;
102:                 if (bg > 0) {
103:                     // Normalize: stretch so that bg -> 255
104:                     result[idx] = Math.min(255.0, gray[idx] * 255.0 / bg);
105:                 } else {
106:                     result[idx] = gray[idx];
107:                 }
108:             }
109:         }
110:         return result;
111:     }
112: 
113:     private double bilinearInterp(double v00, double v01, double v10, double v11, double fx, double fy) {
114:         double top = v00 + (v01 - v00) * fx;
115:         double bot = v10 + (v11 - v10) * fx;
116:         return top + (bot - top) * fy;
117:     }
118: 
119:     private int otsuThreshold(double[] pixels) {
120:         int[] histogram = new int[256];
121:         for (double p : pixels) {
122:             int val = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, (int) Math.round(p)));
123:             histogram[val]++;
124:         }
125: 
126:         int total = pixels.length;
127:         double sum = 0;
128:         for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
129:             sum += i * histogram[i];
130:         }
131: 
132:         double sumB = 0;
133:         int wB = 0;
134:         double maxVariance = 0;
135:         int threshold = 0;
136: 
137:         for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
138:             wB += histogram[i];
139:             if (wB == 0) continue;
140:             int wF = total - wB;
141:             if (wF == 0) break;
142: 
143:             sumB += i * histogram[i];
144:             double meanB = sumB / wB;
145:             double meanF = (sum - sumB) / wF;
146:             double variance = (double) wB * wF * (meanB - meanF) * (meanB - meanF);
147: 
148:             if (variance > maxVariance) {
149:                 maxVariance = variance;
150:                 threshold = i;
151:             }
152:         }
153:         return threshold;
154:     }
155: 
156:     private BufferedImage inpaintBackground(BufferedImage original, BufferedImage mask, int radius) {
157:         int width = original.getWidth();
158:         int height = original.getHeight();
159:         BufferedImage result = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);
160:         int[] src = new int[width * height];
161:         original.getRGB(0, 0, width, height, src, 0, width);
162: 
163:         // For each pixel in the mask that is foreground (black), fill with nearby background color
164:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
165:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
166:                 int maskRgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
167:                 boolean isForeground = (maskRgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0; // black = foreground
168: 
169:                 if (!isForeground) {
170:                     result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));
171:                 } else {
172:                     // Find nearest background pixel within radius
173:                     int bestR = 0, bestG = 0, bestB = 0;
174:                     int found = 0;
175:                     for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius && found == 0; dy++) {
176:                         for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius && found == 0; dx++) {
177:                             int nx = x + dx;
178:                             int ny = y + dy;
179:                             if (nx < 0 || nx >= width || ny < 0 || ny >= height) continue;
180:                             int nmask = mask.getRGB(nx, ny);
181:                             if ((nmask & 0xFFFFFF) != 0) { // background pixel
182:                                 int nrgb = original.getRGB(nx, ny);
183:                                 bestR = (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF;
184:                                 bestG = (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF;
185:                                 bestB = nrgb & 0xFF;
186:                                 found++;
187:                             }
188:                         }
189:                     }
190:                     if (found > 0) {
191:                         int rgb = (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB;
192:                         result.setRGB(x, y, rgb);
193:                     } else {
194:                         result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));
195:                     }
196:                 }
197:             }
198:         }
199:         return result;
200:     }
201: }
