```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "oldString": "    public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Step 1: Convert to grayscale\n        int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);\n\n        // Step 2: Background normalization\n        int tileSize = 64;\n        double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);\n\n        // Step 3: Otsu binarization\n        int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);\n        BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image\n                // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground\n                if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // black\n                } else {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // white\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color\n        BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, 3);\n\n        return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Orchestrates the full page-segmentation pipeline:\n     *   grayscale -> background normalization -> Otsu binarization -> inpaint background.\n     *\n     * @param image  Input RGB page image (e.g. 300 DPI PDFBox render).\n     * @return       SegmentedImage holding a binary foreground mask and a cleaned background.\n     */\n    public SegmentedImage segment(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Step 1: Convert to grayscale\n        int[] gray = toGrayscale(image);\n\n        // Step 2: Background normalization\n        // tileSize=64: 64x64 pixel tiles; 2^6 chosen for fast bit ops during interpolation.\n        int tileSize = 64;\n        double[] bgNormalized = backgroundNormalize(gray, width, height, tileSize);\n\n        // Step 3: Otsu binarization\n        int threshold = otsuThreshold(bgNormalized);\n        BufferedImage foregroundMask = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                // Foreground = 0 (black), Background = 1 (white) in binary image\n                // Text is dark on light bg, so pixel <= threshold = foreground\n                if (gray[idx] <= threshold) {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFF000000); // ARGB: black, full opacity\n                } else {\n                    foregroundMask.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF); // ARGB: white, full opacity\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Cleaned background — fill text regions with surrounding color\n        // radius=3: search up to 3 px away for a background pixel to sample from.\n        BufferedImage cleanedBackground = inpaintBackground(image, foregroundMask, 3);\n\n        return new SegmentedImage(foregroundMask, cleanedBackground);\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "oldString": "    private int[] toGrayscale(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n        int[] gray = new int[width * height];\n\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int rgb = image.getRGB(x, y);\n                int r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                int g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                int b = rgb & 0xFF;\n                gray[y * width + x] = (int) (0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b);\n            }\n        }\n        return gray;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Converts an RGB BufferedImage to a flat int array of grayscale values\n     * (0 = black, 255 = white) using the ITU-R BT.601 luma formula.\n     *\n     * Magic numbers in luma formula:\n     *   0.299, 0.587, 0.114 — perception-weighted coefficients for R/G/B.\n     *   Human vision is most sensitive to green, least to blue.\n     *   These sum to 1.0, so neutral gray (R=G=B) maps to itself.\n     *   This is the same formula used in JPEG, NTSC/PAL, and most image libraries.\n     *\n     * Bit shifts in RGB extraction:\n     *   (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF  — extract the red  byte (bits 16-23 of 0xAARRGGBB)\n     *   (rgb >>  8) & 0xFF  — extract the green byte (bits  8-15)\n     *   rgb        & 0xFF  — extract the blue byte (bits  0-7)\n     */\n    private int[] toGrayscale(BufferedImage image) {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n        int[] gray = new int[width * height];\n\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int rgb = image.getRGB(x, y);\n                int r = (rgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                int g = (rgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                int b = rgb & 0xFF;\n                gray[y * width + x] = (int) (0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b);\n            }\n        }\n        return gray;\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "oldString": "    private double[] backgroundNormalize(int[] gray, int width, int height, int tileSize) {\n        int tilesX = (int) Math.ceil((double) width / tileSize);\n        int tilesY = (int) Math.ceil((double) height / tileSize);\n\n        // Sample background level per tile (95th percentile = bright, since text is dark)\n        double[][] bgLevels = new double[tilesY][tilesX];\n        for (int ty = 0; ty < tilesY; ty++) {\n            for (int tx = 0; tx < tilesX; tx++) {\n                int startX = tx * tileSize;\n                int startY = ty * tileSize;\n                int endX = Math.min(startX + tileSize, width);\n                int endY = Math.min(startY + tileSize, height);\n                int count = (endX - startX) * (endY - startY);\n                int[] sorted = new int[count];\n                int idx = 0;\n                for (int y = startY; y < endY; y++) {\n                    for (int x = startX; x < endX; x++) {\n                        sorted[idx++] = gray[y * width + x];\n                    }\n                }\n                java.util.Arrays.sort(sorted);\n                // 95th percentile as background estimate\n                bgLevels[ty][tx] = sorted[(int) (count * 0.95)];\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Bilinear interpolation of background at each pixel\n        double[] result = new double[gray.length];\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                float fx = (float) x / tileSize;\n                float fy = (float) y / tileSize;\n                int tx0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fx), tilesX - 1);\n                int tx1 = Math.min(tx0 + 1, tilesX - 1);\n                int ty0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fy), tilesY - 1);\n                int ty1 = Math.min(ty0 + 1, tilesY - 1);\n\n                double bg = bilinearInterp(\n                        bgLevels[ty0][tx0], bgLevels[ty0][tx1],\n                        bgLevels[ty1][tx0], bgLevels[ty1][tx1],\n                        fx - tx0, fy - ty0);\n\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                if (bg > 0) {\n                    // Normalize: stretch so that bg -> 255\n                    result[idx] = Math.min(255.0, gray[idx] * 255.0 / bg);\n                } else {\n                    result[idx] = gray[idx];\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Tile-based background normalization that corrects non-uniform illumination\n     * (e.g. shadows, gradients, scanner vignetting).\n     *\n     * How it works:\n     *   1. Divide the image into a grid of tiles (default 64x64 px).\n     *   2. In each tile, estimate the \"background level\" as the 95th percentile\n     *      of pixel intensities. Since text is dark on a light background, the\n     *      bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface (background).\n     *      The 95th percentile is used (instead of max) to be robust to noise.\n     *   3. Build a smooth background surface across the page using bilinear\n     *      interpolation between tile centers.\n     *   4. Each pixel is then stretched: new_value = old_value * 255 / bg_estimate.\n     *      Pixels at or near the background level map to 255 (white); dark text\n     *      pixels stay dark because their value is far below the bg estimate.\n     *\n     * Magic numbers:\n     *   tileSize = 64 — balances accuracy vs performance. 2^6 allows fast\n     *     integer arithmetic for interpolation (tile centres are at multiples\n     *     of 64).  Smaller = more detail but slower; larger = faster but coarser.\n     *   0.95 — 95th percentile.  Picked empirically: the top 5% of brightness\n     *     values in a tile are assumed to be background, not text or noise.\n     *   255.0 — maximum 8-bit grayscale value (white).\n     */\n    private double[] backgroundNormalize(int[] gray, int width, int height, int tileSize) {\n        int tilesX = (int) Math.ceil((double) width / tileSize);\n        int tilesY = (int) Math.ceil((double) height / tileSize);\n\n        // Sample background level per tile (95th percentile = bright, since text is dark)\n        double[][] bgLevels = new double[tilesY][tilesX];\n        for (int ty = 0; ty < tilesY; ty++) {\n            for (int tx = 0; tx < tilesX; tx++) {\n                int startX = tx * tileSize;\n                int startY = ty * tileSize;\n                int endX = Math.min(startX + tileSize, width);\n                int endY = Math.min(startY + tileSize, height);\n                int count = (endX - startX) * (endY - startY);\n                int[] sorted = new int[count];\n                int idx = 0;\n                for (int y = startY; y < endY; y++) {\n                    for (int x = startX; x < endX; x++) {\n                        sorted[idx++] = gray[y * width + x];\n                    }\n                }\n                java.util.Arrays.sort(sorted);\n                // 95th percentile as background estimate\n                bgLevels[ty][tx] = sorted[(int) (count * 0.95)];\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Bilinear interpolation of background at each pixel\n        double[] result = new double[gray.length];\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                float fx = (float) x / tileSize;\n                float fy = (float) y / tileSize;\n                int tx0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fx), tilesX - 1);\n                int tx1 = Math.min(tx0 + 1, tilesX - 1);\n                int ty0 = Math.min((int) Math.floor(fy), tilesY - 1);\n                int ty1 = Math.min(ty0 + 1, tilesY - 1);\n\n                double bg = bilinearInterp(\n                        bgLevels[ty0][tx0], bgLevels[ty0][tx1],\n                        bgLevels[ty1][tx0], bgLevels[ty1][tx1],\n                        fx - tx0, fy - ty0);\n\n                int idx = y * width + x;\n                if (bg > 0) {\n                    // Normalize: stretch so that bg -> 255\n                    result[idx] = Math.min(255.0, gray[idx] * 255.0 / bg);\n                } else {\n                    result[idx] = gray[idx];\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "oldString": "    private double bilinearInterp(double v00, double v01, double v10, double v11, double fx, double fy) {\n        double top = v00 + (v01 - v00) * fx;\n        double bot = v10 + (v11 - v10) * fx;\n        return top + (bot - top) * fy;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Standard bilinear interpolation over a unit square [0,1]x[0,1].\n     *\n     *   v00 .. v11  — values at the four corners (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right).\n     *   fx, fy      — fractional offsets within the square (0..1).\n     *\n     * Interpolation is performed in two passes:\n     *   1. Linearly interpolate horizontally between the top corners, and between the bottom corners.\n     *   2. Linearly interpolate vertically between the two horizontal results.\n     * This is equivalent to: v = (1-fy)*((1-fx)*v00 + fx*v01) + fy*((1-fx)*v10 + fx*v11)\n     *\n     * No magic numbers — pure linear algebra.\n     */\n    private double bilinearInterp(double v00, double v01, double v10, double v11, double fx, double fy) {\n        double top = v00 + (v01 - v00) * fx;\n        double bot = v10 + (v11 - v10) * fx;\n        return top + (bot - top) * fy;\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/ImageSegmenter.java",
  "oldString": "    private int otsuThreshold(double[] pixels) {\n        int[] histogram = new int[256];\n        for (double p : pixels) {\n            int val = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, (int) Math.round(p)));\n            histogram[val]++;\n        }\n\n        int total = pixels.length;\n        double sum = 0;\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            sum += i * histogram[i];\n        }\n\n        double sumB = 0;\n        int wB = 0;\n        double maxVariance = 0;\n        int threshold = 0;\n\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            wB += histogram[i];\n            if (wB == 0) continue;\n            int wF = total - wB;\n            if (wF == 0) break;\n\n            sumB += i * histogram[i];\n            double meanB = sumB / wB;\n            double meanF = (sum - sumB) / wF;\n            double variance = (double) wB * wF * (meanB - meanF) * (meanB - meanF);\n\n            if (variance > maxVariance) {\n                maxVariance = variance;\n                threshold = i;\n            }\n        }\n        return threshold;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Otsu's method for automatic image thresholding.\n     *\n     * Theory: exhaustively search all 256 possible threshold values and pick the\n     * one that maximises the inter-class variance between foreground and background\n     * pixel distributions.  This gives optimal separation without any user tuning.\n     *\n     * Algorithm steps:\n     *   1. Build a 256-bin histogram of pixel intensities.\n     *   2. For each threshold t (0..255), split pixels into two classes:\n     *      class B (background, intensities > t) and class F (foreground, ≤ t).\n     *   3. Compute the between-class variance:\n     *          σ² = wB * wF * (μB - μF)²\n     *      where w = weight (proportion of pixels), μ = mean intensity.\n     *   4. Return the t that maximises σ².\n     *\n     * Magic numbers:\n     *   256 — number of bins in an 8-bit grayscale histogram (2^8 possible values).\n     *   wB, wF  — weights (class pixel counts).  These are integers but the formula\n     *     uses them as the product to give heavier weight to balanced splits.\n     */\n    private int otsuThreshold(double[] pixels) {\n        int[] histogram = new int[256];\n        for (double p : pixels) {\n            int val = Math.min(255, Math.max(0, (int) Math.round(p)));\n            histogram[val]++;\n        }\n\n        int total = pixels.length;\n        double sum = 0;\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            sum += i * histogram[i];\n        }\n\n        double sumB = 0;\n        int wB = 0;\n        double maxVariance = 0;\n        int threshold = 0;\n\n        for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {\n            wB += histogram[i];\n            if (wB == 0) continue;\n            int wF = total - wB;\n            if (wF == 0) break;\n\n            sumB += i * histogram[i];\n            double meanB = sumB / wB;\n            double meanF = (sum - sumB) / wF;\n            double variance = (double) wB * wF * (meanB - meanF) * (meanB - meanF);\n\n            if (variance > maxVariance) {\n                maxVariance = variance;\n                threshold = i;\n            }\n        }\n        return threshold;\n    }"
}
```

```json
  "oldString": "    private BufferedImage inpaintBackground(BufferedImage original, BufferedImage mask, int radius) {\n        int width = original.getWidth();\n        int height = original.getHeight();\n        BufferedImage result = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);\n        int[] src = new int[width * height];\n        original.getRGB(0, 0, width, height, src, 0, width);\n\n        // For each pixel in the mask that is foreground (black), fill with nearby background color\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int maskRgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);\n                boolean isForeground = (maskRgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0; // black = foreground\n\n                if (!isForeground) {\n                    result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                } else {\n                    // Find nearest background pixel within radius\n                    int bestR = 0, bestG = 0, bestB = 0;\n                    int found = 0;\n                    for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius && found == 0; dy++) {\n                        for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius && found == 0; dx++) {\n                            int nx = x + dx;\n                            int ny = y + dy;\n                            if (nx < 0 || nx >= width || ny < 0 || ny >= height) continue;\n                            int nmask = mask.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                            if ((nmask & 0xFFFFFF) != 0) { // background pixel\n                                int nrgb = original.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                                bestR = (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                                bestG = (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                                bestB = nrgb & 0xFF;\n                                found++;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if (found > 0) {\n                        int rgb = (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB;\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, rgb);\n                    } else {\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Simple inpainting: replace foreground (text) pixels with the nearest\n     * background pixel colour found within a search radius.\n     *\n     * This produces a \"cleaned\" page where text regions are filled in with\n     * the surrounding page colour — useful as a background layer for PDF\n     * re-assembly (the text will be overlaid as an invisible OCR layer).\n     *\n     * Algorithm:\n     *   For each pixel that the mask identifies as foreground (black):\n     *     1. Search outward in a square (top-left to bottom-right) within radius.\n     *     2. Pick the first background (white in mask) pixel found.\n     *     3. Copy that pixel's colour to the result.\n     *   Background pixels are copied unchanged.\n     *\n     * This is a crude \"nearest neighbour\" inpaint — a real implementation would\n     * use a proper diffusion or patch-match algorithm, but this is sufficient\n     * for the page-background use case.\n     *\n     * Magic numbers:\n     *   radius — search window size (passed in as parameter; typically 3).\n     *     3 is used because typical text stroke widths at 300 DPI are 2-4 px.\n     *     Larger values handle thicker strokes but are slower.\n     *   0xFFFFFF — bitmask to extract only the RGB channels, discarding alpha.\n     *     setRGB expects full ARGB, so foreground is 0xFF000000 (black w/ alpha=FF)\n     *     and background is 0xFFFFFFFF (white w/ alpha=FF).\n     *   Bit shifts for colour reconstruction:\n     *     (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB — packs R, G, B into 0x00RRGGBB.\n     *     Alpha is also needed: | 0xFF000000 for full opacity.\n     *     (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF, (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF, nrgb & 0xFF — extract\n     *     individual 8-bit colour channels from a 32-bit ARGB int.\n     */\n    private BufferedImage inpaintBackground(BufferedImage original, BufferedImage mask, int radius) {\n        int width = original.getWidth();\n        int height = original.getHeight();\n        BufferedImage result = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_INT_RGB);\n\n        for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {\n                int maskRgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);\n                boolean isForeground = (maskRgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0;\n\n                if (!isForeground) {\n                    result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                } else {\n                    // Find nearest background pixel within radius\n                    int bestR = 0, bestG = 0, bestB = 0;\n                    int found = 0;\n                    for (int dy = -radius; dy <= radius && found == 0; dy++) {\n                        for (int dx = -radius; dx <= radius && found == 0; dx++) {\n                            int nx = x + dx;\n                            int ny = y + dy;\n                            if (nx < 0 || nx >= width || ny < 0 || ny >= height) continue;\n                            int nmask = mask.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                            if ((nmask & 0xFFFFFF) != 0) {\n                                int nrgb = original.getRGB(nx, ny);\n                                bestR = (nrgb >> 16) & 0xFF;\n                                bestG = (nrgb >> 8) & 0xFF;\n                                bestB = nrgb & 0xFF;\n                                found++;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if (found > 0) {\n                        int rgb = 0xFF000000 | (bestR << 16) | (bestG << 8) | bestB;\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, rgb);\n                    } else {\n                        result.setRGB(x, y, original.getRGB(x, y));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return result;\n    }"
}
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.Rectangle;
4: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
5: import java.io.BufferedReader;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
9: import java.util.ArrayList;
10: import java.util.List;
11: 
12: import javax.imageio.ImageIO;
13: 
14: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
15: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
16: 
17: public class OCREngine {
18: 
19:     private final File tessdataDir;
20: 
21:     public OCREngine() {
22:         String tessdata = System.getenv("TESSDATA_PREFIX");
23:         if (tessdata != null && !tessdata.isEmpty()) {
24:             this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdata);
25:         } else {
26:             this.tessdataDir = new File("/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata");
27:         }
28:     }
29: 
30:     public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {
31:         int width = image.getWidth();
32:         int height = image.getHeight();
33: 
34:         // Write image to temp file
35:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-", ".png");
36:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
37:         ImageIO.write(image, "png", tempPng);
38: 
39:         try {
40:             // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes
41:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
42:             ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(
43:                     "tesseract",
44:                     tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),
45:                     prefix,
46:                     "-l", "eng",
47:                     "--psm", "1",
48:                     "tsv"
49:             );
50:             pb.environment().put("TESSDATA_PREFIX", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());
51:             pb.redirectErrorStream(true);
52: 
53:             Process process = pb.start();
54:             try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
55:                     new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {
56:                 // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)
57:                 while (reader.readLine() != null) { }
58:             }
59:             int exitCode = process.waitFor();
60:             if (exitCode != 0) {
61:                 throw new IOException("Tesseract exited with code " + exitCode);
62:             }
63: 
64:             // Parse TSV output
65:             File tsvFile = new File(prefix + ".tsv");
66:             if (!tsvFile.exists()) {
67:                 return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());
68:             }
69: 
70:             List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);
71:             return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);
72: 
73:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {
74:             Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
75:             throw new IOException("Tesseract was interrupted", e);
76:         } finally {
77:             // Cleanup temp files
78:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
79:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".tsv"}) {
80:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
81:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
82:             }
83:         }
84:     }
85: 
86:     private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {
87:         List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();
88:         try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {
89:             String header = reader.readLine(); // skip header
90:             if (header == null) return blocks;
91: 
92:             String line;
93:             while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
94:                 if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;
95:                 String[] parts = line.split("\t");
96:                 // TSV columns: level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,
97:                 //              left, top, width, height, conf, text
98:                 if (parts.length < 12) continue;
99: 
100:                 String level = parts[0];
101:                 String text = parts[11].trim();
102:                 if (!"5".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue; // word level only
103: 
104:                 try {
105:                     int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);
106:                     int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);
107:                     int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);
108:                     int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);
109:                     double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);
110: 
111:                     blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));
112:                 } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
113:                     // skip malformed lines
114:                 }
115:             }
116:         }
117:         return blocks;
118:     }
119: }
```

```
20:         private final int height;
21: 
22:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
23:             this.data = data;
24:             this.width = width;
25:             this.height = height;
26:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
27:         }
28: 
29:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
30:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
31:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
32:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
33:     }
34: 
35:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
36:         // Try jbig2enc first
37:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
38:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
39:             try {
40:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
41:             } catch (IOException e) {
42:                 // Fall through to CCITT fallback
43:             }
44:         }
45:         // Fallback: PNG (Phase 7 will add CCITT G4 encoding)
46:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
47:     }
48: 
49:     private File findJbig2enc() {
50:         String nativeDir = System.getProperty("tfocr.native.dir", "native");
51:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
52:         String dirName;
53:         switch (os) {
54:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
55:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
56:             default: dirName = "linux";
57:         }
58:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
59:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
60:         return null;
61:     }
62: 
63:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
64:         // Write mask as PNG temp file
65:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
66:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
67:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
68: 
69:         try {
70:             File outputDir = tempPng.getParentFile();
71:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
72: 
73:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,
74:                     "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
75:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
76:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
77:             }
78: 
79:             // jbig2enc produces output.sym and output.jbig2 files
80:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
81:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
82: 
83:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
84:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
85: 
86:             // Combine: global (sym) + page data (jbig2)
87:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
88:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
89:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
90: 
91:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
92:         } finally {
93:             // Cleanup
94:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
95:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
96:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
97:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
98:             }
99:         }
100:     }
101: 
102:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
103:         // Fallback: return raw PNG bytes (Phase 7 will integrate CCITT G4 encoding)
104:         ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
105:         ImageIO.write(mask, "png", baos);
106:         return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
107:     }
108: }

(End of file - total 108 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.List;
7: 
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
18: 
19: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
20: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
21: 
22: public class PDFAssembler {
23: 
24:     private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);
25: 
26:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
27:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
28:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
29:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
30: 
31:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
32:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
33:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
34:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
35:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
36: 
37:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
38:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
39: 
40:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
41:                 output.addPage(outPage);
42: 
43:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
44:                     // Draw background image scaled to page dimensions
45:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
46:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
47:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
48:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
49: 
50:                     // Draw invisible OCR text
51:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
52:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
53:                     cs.beginText();
54:                     cs.setFont(FONT, 1);
55:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
56: 
57:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
58:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
59:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
60:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);
61:                         cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);
62:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
63:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
64:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
65:                     }
66:                     cs.endText();
67:                 }
68:             }
69:         }
70: 
71:         return output;
72:     }
73: 
74: }
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.BufferedReader;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
10: 
11: public class SubprocessRunner {
12: 
13:     public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {
14:         return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);
15:     }
16: 
17:     public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {
18:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
19:         command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());
20:         command.addAll(args);
21: 
22:         ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
23:         pb.redirectErrorStream(false);
24: 
25:         Process process = pb.start();
26: 
27:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {
28:             try {
29:                 boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
30:                 if (!finished) {
31:                     process.destroyForcibly();
32:                     throw new IOException("Process timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms: " + executable.getName());
33:                 }
34:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
35:                 process.destroyForcibly();
36:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
37:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
38:             }
39:         } else {
40:             try {
41:                 process.waitFor();
42:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
43:                 process.destroyForcibly();
44:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
45:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
46:             }
47:         }
48: 
49:         byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();
50:         byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();
51:         int exitCode = process.exitValue();
52: 
53:         return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);
54:     }
55: 
56:     public static class Result {
57:         private final int exitCode;
58:         private final byte[] stdout;
59:         private final byte[] stderr;
60: 
61:         public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {
62:             this.exitCode = exitCode;
63:             this.stdout = stdout;
64:             this.stderr = stderr;
65:         }
66: 
67:         public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }
68:         public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }
69:         public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }
70:         public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }
71:         public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }
72:     }
73: }
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Wait, I just changed PageResult from a class with explicit getters to a Java `record`. Records have been available since Java 16. But the original code was a class, and other code uses `result.getPageNumber()`, `result.getTextBlocks()`, `result.getWidth()`, `result.getHeight()`. Java records automatically provide accessor methods named after the components — so for a `record PageResult(int pageNumber, ...)`, the accessor is `pageNumber()`, NOT `getPageNumber()`.

This would break all the code that uses `result.getPageNumber()`, `result.getWidth()`, `result.getHeight()`, `result.getTextBlocks()`. Let me change it back to a regular class with comments instead.

</details>

That record change breaks existing getter conventions — reverting to a class with Javadoc:

```json
  "newString": "package com.trulyfreeocr.model;\n\nimport java.awt.image.BufferedImage;\n\n/**\n * Output of the ImageSegmenter pipeline.\n *\n * foregroundMask    Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY) where black pixels\n *                   represent text/foreground and white is background.\n * cleanedBackground The original page image with text regions inpainted\n *                   (filled in with surrounding colour), suitable as a\n *                   JPEG background layer in the output PDF.\n */\npublic class SegmentedImage {\n\n    private final BufferedImage foregroundMask;\n    private final BufferedImage cleanedBackground;\n\n    public SegmentedImage(BufferedImage foregroundMask, BufferedImage cleanedBackground) {\n        this.foregroundMask = foregroundMask;\n        this.cleanedBackground = cleanedBackground;\n    }\n\n    public BufferedImage getForegroundMask() { return foregroundMask; }\n    public BufferedImage getCleanedBackground() { return cleanedBackground; }\n}"
}
```

```json
  "oldString": "public class OCREngine {\n\n    private final File tessdataDir;\n\n    public OCREngine() {\n        String tessdata = System.getenv(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n        if (tessdata != null && !tessdata.isEmpty()) {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdata);\n        } else {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(\"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Write image to temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        ImageIO.write(image, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(\n                    \"tesseract\",\n                    tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),\n                    prefix,\n                    \"-l\", \"eng\",\n                    \"--psm\", \"1\",\n                    \"tsv\"\n            );\n            pb.environment().put(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());\n            pb.redirectErrorStream(true);\n\n            Process process = pb.start();\n            try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                    new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {\n                // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)\n                while (reader.readLine() != null) { }\n            }\n            int exitCode = process.waitFor();\n            if (exitCode != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"Tesseract exited with code \" + exitCode);\n            }\n\n            // Parse TSV output\n            File tsvFile = new File(prefix + \".tsv\");\n            if (!tsvFile.exists()) {\n                return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());\n            }\n\n            List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);\n            return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);\n\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            throw new IOException(\"Tesseract was interrupted\", e);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup temp files\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".tsv\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {\n        List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();\n        try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {\n            String header = reader.readLine(); // skip header\n            if (header == null) return blocks;\n\n            String line;\n            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {\n                if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;\n                String[] parts = line.split(\"\\t\");\n                // TSV columns: level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,\n                //              left, top, width, height, conf, text\n                if (parts.length < 12) continue;\n\n                String level = parts[0];\n                String text = parts[11].trim();\n                if (!\"5\".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue; // word level only\n\n                try {\n                    int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);\n                    int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);\n                    int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);\n                    int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);\n                    double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);\n\n                    blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));\n                } catch (NumberFormatException e) {\n                    // skip malformed lines\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return blocks;\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * OCR engine that delegates to the Tesseract CLI via subprocess.\n *\n * Tess4J's JNA binding was originally planned, but lept4j's\n * Leptonica version requires symbols not present in the system's\n * liblept.so.5.  Running tesseract as a subprocess (with TSV output)\n * avoids this native-library version mismatch entirely and is more\n * portable across Linux distributions.\n *\n * Tesseract output format: TSV (tab-separated values) with columns:\n *   level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,\n *   left, top, width, height, conf, text\n *\n * CLI flags used:\n *   -l eng    — English language model\n *   --psm 1   — Automatic page segmentation with OSD\n *   tsv       — Output format: TSV (word-level bounding boxes)\n *\n * The output PageResult always reports pageNumber=1 because each\n * call processes exactly one BufferedImage.\n */\npublic class OCREngine {\n\n    private final File tessdataDir;\n\n    public OCREngine() {\n        String tessdata = System.getenv(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\");\n        if (tessdata != null && !tessdata.isEmpty()) {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdata);\n        } else {\n            this.tessdataDir = new File(\"/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    public PageResult ocr(BufferedImage image) throws IOException {\n        int width = image.getWidth();\n        int height = image.getHeight();\n\n        // Write image to temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        ImageIO.write(image, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            // Run tesseract with TSV output for word-level bounding boxes\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(\n                    \"tesseract\",\n                    tempPng.getAbsolutePath(),\n                    prefix,\n                    \"-l\", \"eng\",\n                    \"--psm\", \"1\",\n                    \"tsv\"\n            );\n            pb.environment().put(\"TESSDATA_PREFIX\", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());\n            pb.redirectErrorStream(true);\n\n            Process process = pb.start();\n            try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(\n                    new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {\n                // Read and discard stdout (status messages go here)\n                while (reader.readLine() != null) { }\n            }\n            int exitCode = process.waitFor();\n            if (exitCode != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"Tesseract exited with code \" + exitCode);\n            }\n\n            // Parse TSV output\n            File tsvFile = new File(prefix + \".tsv\");\n            if (!tsvFile.exists()) {\n                return new PageResult(1, width, height, List.of());\n            }\n\n            List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);\n            return new PageResult(1, width, height, blocks);\n\n        } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n            throw new IOException(\"Tesseract was interrupted\", e);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup temp files\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".tsv\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {\n        List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();\n        try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {\n            String header = reader.readLine(); // skip header\n            if (header == null) return blocks;\n\n            String line;\n            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {\n                if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;\n                String[] parts = line.split(\"\\t\");\n                // TSV columns: level, page_num, block_num, par_num, line_num, word_num,\n                //              left, top, width, height, conf, text\n                if (parts.length < 12) continue;\n\n                String level = parts[0];\n                String text = parts[11].trim();\n                if (!\"5\".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue; // word level only\n\n                try {\n                    int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);\n                    int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);\n                    int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);\n                    int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);\n                    double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);\n\n                    blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));\n                } catch (NumberFormatException e) {\n                    // skip malformed lines\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return blocks;\n    }\n}"
}
```

```json
  "oldString": "/**\n * Compresses a binary foreground mask using jbig2enc (via subprocess)\n * with a fallback to PNG byte-array output.\n *\n * jbig2enc flags used:\n *   -p  — Produce a symbol table (shared across pages for better compression)\n *   -s  — Refine smearing (improves symbol matching)\n *\n * jbig2enc generates two output files per input:\n *   .sym  — Global symbol dictionary (shared across pages)\n *   .jbig2 — Page-specific symbol instances\n * These are concatenated (symbol dictionary first) and returned\n * as a single byte array.  A PDF JBIG2Decode stream expects this\n * exact layout: global segment(s) followed by page data.\n *\n * When jbig2enc is not available (not installed or missing from\n * native/{os}/), the fallback wraps the mask as PNG bytes.\n * Phase 7 will replace this fallback with proper CCITT Group 4\n * fax encoding using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.\n */\npublic class JBIG2Compressor {\n\n    /**\n     * Holds the compressed foreground mask data.\n     *\n     * isJbig2  True if data was produced by jbig2enc (JBIG2 format).\n     *          False if fallback was used (PNG format).\n     * data     Compressed byte array (JBIG2 or PNG).\n     * width    Width of the original mask in pixels.\n     * height   Height of the original mask in pixels.\n     */\n    public static class CompressionResult {\n        private final boolean isJbig2;\n        private final byte[] data;\n        private final int width;\n        private final int height;\n\n        public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {\n            this.data = data;\n            this.width = width;\n            this.height = height;\n            this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;\n        }\n\n        public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }\n        public byte[] getData() { return data; }\n        public int getWidth() { return width; }\n        public int getHeight() { return height; }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Attempts JBIG2 compression; falls back to PNG on failure.\n     *\n     * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY).\n     * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or PNG data.\n     */\n    public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {\n        // Try jbig2enc first\n        File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();\n        if (jbig2enc != null) {\n            try {\n                return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // Fall through to fallback\n            }\n        }\n        // Fallback: PNG (Phase 7 will add CCITT G4 encoding)\n        return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current platform.\n     *\n     * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).\n     * The native directory can be overridden via the system property\n     * \"tfocr.native.dir\" (defaults to \"native\").\n     *\n     * @return The executable File if found and executable, or null.\n     */\n    private File findJbig2enc() {\n        String nativeDir = System.getProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\", \"native\");\n        Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();\n        String dirName;\n        switch (os) {\n            case WINDOWS: dirName = \"win\"; break;\n            case MAC: dirName = \"mac\"; break;\n            default: dirName = \"linux\";\n        }\n        File exe = new File(nativeDir + \"/\" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? \"jbig2enc.exe\" : \"jbig2enc\");\n        if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Runs jbig2enc as a subprocess and combines the output into a single byte array.\n     *\n     * jbig2enc writes two files next to the input PNG:\n     *   {prefix}.sym   — Global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).\n     *   {prefix}.jbig2 — Page-specific symbol-instance data.\n     *\n     * These are concatenated (sym first, then jbig2) because a PDF\n     * JBIG2Decode stream expects global segments before page data.\n     *\n     * @param jbig2enc  The jbig2enc executable.\n     * @param mask      Binary foreground mask image.\n     * @return CompressionResult with JBIG2 data.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Write mask as PNG temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-jbig2-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            File outputDir = tempPng.getParentFile();\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n\n            SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,\n                    \"-p\", \"-s\", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());\n            if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"jbig2enc failed: \" + result.getStderrString());\n            }\n\n            // jbig2enc produces output.sym and output.jbig2 files\n            File symFile = new File(prefix + \".sym\");\n            File jbig2File = new File(prefix + \".jbig2\");\n\n            byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n            byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n\n            // Combine: global (sym) + page data (jbig2)\n            byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];\n            System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);\n            System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);\n\n            return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".sym\", \".jbig2\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Fallback: encodes the mask as a PNG and returns the bytes.\n     *\n     * Phase 7 will replace this with proper CCITT Group 4 fax encoding\n     * using PDFBox's CCITTFactory for better compression.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", baos);\n        return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Compresses a binary foreground mask using jbig2enc (via subprocess)\n * with a fallback to PNG byte-array output.\n *\n * jbig2enc flags used:\n *   -p  — Produce a symbol table (shared across pages for better compression)\n *   -s  — Refine smearing (improves symbol matching)\n *\n * jbig2enc generates two output files per input:\n *   .sym  — Global symbol dictionary (shared across pages)\n *   .jbig2 — Page-specific symbol instances\n * These are concatenated (symbol dictionary first) and returned\n * as a single byte array.  A PDF JBIG2Decode stream expects this\n * exact layout: global segment(s) followed by page data.\n *\n * When jbig2enc is not available (not installed or missing from\n * native/{os}/), the fallback wraps the mask as PNG bytes.\n * Phase 7 will replace this fallback with proper CCITT Group 4\n * fax encoding using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.\n */\npublic class JBIG2Compressor {\n\n    /**\n     * Holds the compressed foreground mask data.\n     *\n     * isJbig2  True if data was produced by jbig2enc (JBIG2 format).\n     *          False if fallback was used (PNG format).\n     * data     Compressed byte array (JBIG2 or PNG).\n     * width    Width of the original mask in pixels.\n     * height   Height of the original mask in pixels.\n     */\n    public static class CompressionResult {\n        private final boolean isJbig2;\n        private final byte[] data;\n        private final int width;\n        private final int height;\n\n        public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {\n            this.data = data;\n            this.width = width;\n            this.height = height;\n            this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;\n        }\n\n        public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }\n        public byte[] getData() { return data; }\n        public int getWidth() { return width; }\n        public int getHeight() { return height; }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Attempts JBIG2 compression; falls back to PNG on failure.\n     *\n     * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY).\n     * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or PNG data.\n     */\n    public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {\n        File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();\n        if (jbig2enc != null) {\n            try {\n                return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // Fall through to fallback\n            }\n        }\n        return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current platform.\n     *\n     * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).\n     * The native directory can be overridden via the system property\n     * \"tfocr.native.dir\" (defaults to \"native\").\n     *\n     * @return The executable File if found and executable, or null.\n     */\n    private File findJbig2enc() {\n        String nativeDir = System.getProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\", \"native\");\n        Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();\n        String dirName;\n        switch (os) {\n            case WINDOWS: dirName = \"win\"; break;\n            case MAC: dirName = \"mac\"; break;\n            default: dirName = \"linux\";\n        }\n        File exe = new File(nativeDir + \"/\" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? \"jbig2enc.exe\" : \"jbig2enc\");\n        if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;\n        return null;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Runs jbig2enc as a subprocess and combines the output into a single byte array.\n     *\n     * jbig2enc writes two files next to the input PNG:\n     *   {prefix}.sym   — Global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).\n     *   {prefix}.jbig2 — Page-specific symbol-instance data.\n     *\n     * These are concatenated (sym first, then jbig2) because a PDF\n     * JBIG2Decode stream expects global segments before page data.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-jbig2-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            File outputDir = tempPng.getParentFile();\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n\n            SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,\n                    \"-p\", \"-s\", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());\n            if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"jbig2enc failed: \" + result.getStderrString());\n            }\n\n            File symFile = new File(prefix + \".sym\");\n            File jbig2File = new File(prefix + \".jbig2\");\n\n            byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n            byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n\n            byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];\n            System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);\n            System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);\n\n            return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);\n        } finally {\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".sym\", \".jbig2\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Fallback: encodes the mask as a PNG and returns the bytes.\n     * Phase 7 will replace this with proper CCITT Group 4 fax encoding.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", baos);\n        return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n    }\n}"
}
```

```
74:                     "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
75:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
76:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
77:             }
78: 
79:             // jbig2enc produces output.sym and output.jbig2 files
80:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
81:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
82: 
83:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
84:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
85: 
86:             // Combine: global (sym) + page data (jbig2)
87:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
88:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
89:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
90: 
91:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
92:         } finally {
93:             // Cleanup
94:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
95:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
96:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
97:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
98:             }
99:         }
100:     }
101:

103:         // Fallback: return raw PNG bytes (Phase 7 will integrate CCITT G4 encoding)

105:         ImageIO.write(mask, "png", baos);
```

```json
  "oldString": "    public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {\n        // Try jbig2enc first\n        File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();\n        if (jbig2enc != null) {\n            try {\n                return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // Fall through to CCITT fallback\n            }\n        }\n        // Fallback: PNG (Phase 7 will add CCITT G4 encoding)\n        return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to PNG on failure.\n     *\n     * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).\n     * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or PNG data.\n     */\n    public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {\n        File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();\n        if (jbig2enc != null) {\n            try {\n                return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback\n            }\n        }\n        return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/JBIG2Compressor.java",
  "oldString": "    private File findJbig2enc() {\n        String nativeDir = System.getProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\", \"native\");\n        Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();\n        String dirName;\n        switch (os) {\n            case WINDOWS: dirName = \"win\"; break;\n            case MAC: dirName = \"mac\"; break;\n            default: dirName = \"linux\";\n        }\n        File exe = new File(nativeDir + \"/\" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? \"jbig2enc.exe\" : \"jbig2enc\");\n        if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;\n        return null;\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.\n     *\n     * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).\n     * The base path can be overridden via system property \"tfocr.native.dir\"\n     * (defaults to \"native\").\n     *\n     * @return The executable File, or null if not found.\n     */\n    private File findJbig2enc() {\n        String nativeDir = System.getProperty(\"tfocr.native.dir\", \"native\");\n        Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();\n        String dirName;\n        switch (os) {\n            case WINDOWS: dirName = \"win\"; break;\n            case MAC: dirName = \"mac\"; break;\n            default: dirName = \"linux\";\n        }\n        File exe = new File(nativeDir + \"/\" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? \"jbig2enc.exe\" : \"jbig2enc\");\n        if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;\n        return null;\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/JBIG2Compressor.java",
  "oldString": "    private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Write mask as PNG temp file\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-jbig2-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            File outputDir = tempPng.getParentFile();\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n\n            SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,\n                    \"-p\", \"-s\", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());\n            if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"jbig2enc failed: \" + result.getStderrString());\n            }\n\n            // jbig2enc produces output.sym and output.jbig2 files\n            File symFile = new File(prefix + \".sym\");\n            File jbig2File = new File(prefix + \".jbig2\");\n\n            byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n            byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n\n            // Combine: global (sym) + page data (jbig2)\n            byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];\n            System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);\n            System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);\n\n            return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);\n        } finally {\n            // Cleanup\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".sym\", \".jbig2\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Fallback: return raw PNG bytes (Phase 7 will integrate CCITT G4 encoding)\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", baos);\n        return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.\n     *\n     * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)\n     * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that\n     * jbig2enc produces.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        File tempPng = File.createTempFile(\"tfocr-jbig2-\", \".png\");\n        tempPng.deleteOnExit();\n        javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", tempPng);\n\n        try {\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n\n            SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,\n                    \"-p\", \"-s\", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());\n            if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {\n                throw new IOException(\"jbig2enc failed: \" + result.getStderrString());\n            }\n\n            File symFile = new File(prefix + \".sym\");\n            File jbig2File = new File(prefix + \".jbig2\");\n\n            byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n            byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];\n\n            byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];\n            System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);\n            System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);\n\n            return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);\n        } finally {\n            String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll(\"\\\\.png$\", \"\");\n            for (String ext : new String[]{\".png\", \".sym\", \".jbig2\"}) {\n                File f = new File(prefix + ext);\n                if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Fallback: encodes the mask as PNG bytes.  Phase 7 will replace this\n     * with proper CCITT Group 4 using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", baos);\n        return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n    }"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java",
  "oldString": "public class PDFAssembler {\n\n    private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);\n\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        PDDocument output = new PDDocument();\n\n        try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {\n            int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n                PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);\n                PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();\n\n                BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);\n                PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);\n\n                PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);\n                output.addPage(outPage);\n\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {\n                    // Draw background image scaled to page dimensions\n                    PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);\n                    float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();\n                    float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();\n                    cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n\n                    // Draw invisible OCR text\n                    float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();\n                    float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();\n                    cs.beginText();\n                    cs.setFont(FONT, 1);\n                    cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);\n\n                    for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {\n                        float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;\n                        float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;\n                        float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);\n                        cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);\n                        cs.showText(tb.getWord());\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);\n                    }\n                    cs.endText();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return output;\n    }\n\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from three per-page inputs:\n *   - Source PDF (to copy page dimensions / media boxes)\n *   - Cleaned background images (JPEG-compressed later)\n *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)\n *\n * Rendering mode RenderingMode.NEITHER (= PDF Tr 3) makes text\n * invisible on screen and in print, but still selectable and\n * searchable — the key requirement for a hidden OCR layer.\n *\n * Coordinate transformation:\n *   Page images are rendered at 300 DPI; PDF user space is 72 DPI.\n *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px\n *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px\n *   Y is flipped because image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.\n *\n * Font: Helvetica (Standard 14 Font, no embedding needed).\n *       The font choice doesn't affect appearance since rendering mode\n *       is NEITHER; it only affects text-extraction metadata.\n */\npublic class PDFAssembler {\n\n    private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);\n\n    /**\n     * Builds a new searchable PDF.\n     *\n     * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (used only for per-page media boxes).\n     * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images (from ImageSegmenter).\n     * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results (from OCREngine).\n     * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.\n     */\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        PDDocument output = new PDDocument();\n\n        try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {\n            int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n                PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);\n                PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();\n\n                BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);\n                PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);\n\n                PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);\n                output.addPage(outPage);\n\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {\n                    PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);\n                    float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();\n                    float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();\n                    cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n\n                    float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();\n                    float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();\n                    cs.beginText();\n                    cs.setFont(FONT, 1);\n                    cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);\n\n                    for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {\n                        float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;\n                        float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;\n                        float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);\n                        cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);\n                        cs.showText(tb.getWord());\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);\n                    }\n                    cs.endText();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return output;\n    }\n\n}"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PageExtractor.java",
  "oldString": "public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private PDDocument document;\n\n    public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {\n        document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);\n        PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);\n        int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        return pages;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws IOException {\n        if (document != null) {\n            document.close();\n        }\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Renders each page of a PDF to a BufferedImage at 300 DPI\n * using PDFBox's PDFRenderer.\n *\n * 300 DPI is the standard scanning resolution for OCR:\n *   - US Letter (8.5\"x11\")  → 2550 × 3300 px\n *   - A4 (210×297 mm)        → 2480 × 3508 px\n *\n * The renderer uses PDFBox's built-in SFF (subpixel font finalizer)\n * for high-quality text rendering.\n *\n * Implements AutoCloseable so the underlying PDDocument is released.\n * Callers should use try-with-resources.\n */\npublic class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private PDDocument document;\n\n    /**\n     * Loads a PDF and renders every page.\n     *\n     * @param pdf  Input PDF file.\n     * @return List of BufferedImages, one per page, at 300 DPI.\n     */\n    public List<BufferedImage> extractPages(File pdf) throws IOException {\n        document = Loader.loadPDF(pdf);\n        PDFRenderer renderer = new PDFRenderer(document);\n        int pageCount = document.getNumberOfPages();\n        List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, 300);\n            pages.add(img);\n        }\n        return pages;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws IOException {\n        if (document != null) {\n            document.close();\n        }\n    }\n}"
}
```
