  "oldString": "public class SubprocessRunner {\n\n    public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {\n        return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);\n    }\n\n    public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {\n        List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();\n        command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());\n        command.addAll(args);\n\n        ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);\n        pb.redirectErrorStream(false);\n\n        Process process = pb.start();\n\n        if (timeoutMs > 0) {\n            try {\n                boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n                if (!finished) {\n                    process.destroyForcibly();\n                    throw new IOException(\"Process timed out after \" + timeoutMs + \"ms: \" + executable.getName());\n                }\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        } else {\n            try {\n                process.waitFor();\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();\n        byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();\n        int exitCode = process.exitValue();\n\n        return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);\n    }\n\n    public static class Result {\n        private final int exitCode;\n        private final byte[] stdout;\n        private final byte[] stderr;\n\n        public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {\n            this.exitCode = exitCode;\n            this.stdout = stdout;\n            this.stderr = stderr;\n        }\n\n        public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }\n        public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }\n        public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }\n        public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }\n        public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Utility for running external processes and capturing stdout/stderr.\n *\n * Two convenience modes:\n *   run(executable, args...)      — blocks until completion (no timeout).\n *   run(executable, args, ms)     — kills the process after <ms> milliseconds.\n *\n * Stderr is NOT merged with stdout (redirectErrorStream = false) so\n * callers can distinguish normal output from error diagnostics.\n */\npublic class SubprocessRunner {\n\n    /**\n     * Executes a command with no timeout.\n     *\n     * @param executable  The binary to run.\n     * @param args        Variable-length argument list.\n     * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.\n     */\n    public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {\n        return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Executes a command with an optional timeout.\n     *\n     * @param executable  The binary to run.\n     * @param args        Argument list.\n     * @param timeoutMs   Max wall-clock time in milliseconds (&le;0 = no timeout).\n     * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.\n     */\n    public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {\n        List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();\n        command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());\n        command.addAll(args);\n\n        ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);\n        pb.redirectErrorStream(false);\n\n        Process process = pb.start();\n\n        if (timeoutMs > 0) {\n            try {\n                boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n                if (!finished) {\n                    process.destroyForcibly();\n                    throw new IOException(\"Process timed out after \" + timeoutMs + \"ms: \" + executable.getName());\n                }\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        } else {\n            try {\n                process.waitFor();\n            } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                process.destroyForcibly();\n                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                throw new IOException(\"Process was interrupted\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();\n        byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();\n        int exitCode = process.exitValue();\n\n        return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Holds the subprocess output.\n     *\n     * exitCode  Process exit status (0 = success).\n     * stdout    Raw bytes written to standard output.\n     * stderr    Raw bytes written to standard error.\n     */\n    public static class Result {\n        private final int exitCode;\n        private final byte[] stdout;\n        private final byte[] stderr;\n\n        public Result(int exitCode, byte[] stdout, byte[] stderr) {\n            this.exitCode = exitCode;\n            this.stdout = stdout;\n            this.stderr = stderr;\n        }\n\n        public int getExitCode() { return exitCode; }\n        public byte[] getStdout() { return stdout; }\n        public byte[] getStderr() { return stderr; }\n        public String getStdoutString() { return new String(stdout); }\n        public String getStderrString() { return new String(stderr); }\n    }\n}"
}

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/TempFileManager.java",
  "oldString": "public class TempFileManager {\n\n    private final List<File> tempFiles = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    public File createTempFile(String prefix, String suffix) throws IOException {\n        File f = Files.createTempFile(prefix, suffix).toFile();\n        f.deleteOnExit();\n        tempFiles.add(f);\n        return f;\n    }\n\n    public void cleanup() {\n        for (File f : tempFiles) {\n            if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n        }\n        tempFiles.clear();\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Tracks temporary files created during a pipeline run and provides\n * a single cleanup() call to delete them all.\n *\n * Each file is also registered with deleteOnExit() as a safety net\n * in case cleanup() is not called (e.g., JVM crash).\n */\npublic class TempFileManager {\n\n    private final List<File> tempFiles = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a temp file and registers it for cleanup.\n     *\n     * @param prefix  File name prefix (e.g. \"tfocr-\").\n     * @param suffix  File name suffix (e.g. \".png\").\n     * @return The created temporary File.\n     */\n    public File createTempFile(String prefix, String suffix) throws IOException {\n        File f = Files.createTempFile(prefix, suffix).toFile();\n        f.deleteOnExit();\n        tempFiles.add(f);\n        return f;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes all tracked temp files and clears the internal list.\n     */\n    public void cleanup() {\n        for (File f : tempFiles) {\n            if (f.exists()) f.delete();\n        }\n        tempFiles.clear();\n    }\n}"
}

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/util/PlatformUtils.java",
  "oldString": "public class PlatformUtils {\n\n    public enum Os { WINDOWS, LINUX, MAC }\n\n    public enum Arch { X64, AARCH64 }\n\n    public static Os detectOs() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.name\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"win\")) return Os.WINDOWS;\n        if (raw.contains(\"mac\")) return Os.MAC;\n        return Os.LINUX;\n    }\n\n    public static Arch detectArch() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.arch\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"aarch64\") || raw.contains(\"arm64\")) return Arch.AARCH64;\n        return Arch.X64;\n    }\n\n    public static String getProjectJavaHome() {\n        String env = System.getenv(\"TFOCR_JAVA_HOME\");\n        if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) return env;\n        return System.getProperty(\"java.home\");\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "/**\n * Cross-platform detection utilities.\n *\n * Provides consistent enum values for OS and CPU architecture,\n * and resolves the project JDK location via the TFOCR_JAVA_HOME\n * environment variable (falling back to the running JVM's java.home).\n *\n * OS detection is based on the os.name system property:\n *   \"win\" in any case   → Os.WINDOWS\n *   \"mac\" in any case   → Os.MAC\n *   anything else       → Os.LINUX\n *\n * Architecture detection is based on os.arch:\n *   \"aarch64\" or \"arm64\" → Arch.AARCH64\n *   anything else         → Arch.X64\n */\npublic class PlatformUtils {\n\n    public enum Os { WINDOWS, LINUX, MAC }\n\n    public enum Arch { X64, AARCH64 }\n\n    public static Os detectOs() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.name\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"win\")) return Os.WINDOWS;\n        if (raw.contains(\"mac\")) return Os.MAC;\n        return Os.LINUX;\n    }\n\n    public static Arch detectArch() {\n        String raw = System.getProperty(\"os.arch\").toLowerCase();\n        if (raw.contains(\"aarch64\") || raw.contains(\"arm64\")) return Arch.AARCH64;\n        return Arch.X64;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the project-specific JDK home.\n     *\n     * Checks TFOCR_JAVA_HOME first (set by run.sh/bootstrap.sh),\n     * then falls back to the JVM that is running this code.\n     */\n    public static String getProjectJavaHome() {\n        String env = System.getenv(\"TFOCR_JAVA_HOME\");\n        if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) return env;\n        return System.getProperty(\"java.home\");\n    }\n}"
}

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

14: class ImageSegmenterTest {
15: 
16:     static PageExtractor extractor;
17:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
18: 
19:     @BeforeAll
20:     static void setup() {
21:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
22:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
23:     }
24: 
25:     @Test
26:     void segment_blankPage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
27:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
28:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
29:         assertNotNull(result);
30:         assertNotNull(result.getForegroundMask());
31:         assertNotNull(result.getCleanedBackground());
32:     }
33: 
34:     @Test
35:     void segment_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {
36:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
37:         BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);
38:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);
39:         assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getForegroundMask().getWidth());
40:         assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getForegroundMask().getHeight());
41:         assertEquals(page.getWidth(), result.getCleanedBackground().getWidth());
42:         assertEquals(page.getHeight(), result.getCleanedBackground().getHeight());
43:     }
44: 
45:     @Test
46:     void segment_foregroundMaskIsBinary() throws IOException {
47:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
48:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
49:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
50:         assertEquals(BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, mask.getType());
51:     }
52: 
53:     @Test
54:     void segment_blankPage_hasLittleForeground() throws IOException {
55:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/blank.pdf"));
56:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
57:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
58:         int width = mask.getWidth();
59:         int height = mask.getHeight();
60:         int blackPixels = 0;
61:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
62:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
63:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
64:                     blackPixels++;
65:                 }
66:             }
67:         }
68:         int total = width * height;
69:         assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total < 0.05,
70:                 "Blank page should have <5% foreground pixels, got " +
71:                 (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + "%");
72:     }
73: 
74:     @Test
75:     void segment_simpleText_hasForegroundPixels() throws IOException {
76:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
77:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(pages.get(0));
78:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
79:         int width = mask.getWidth();
80:         int height = mask.getHeight();
81:         int blackPixels = 0;
82:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
83:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
84:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
85:                     blackPixels++;
86:                 }
87:             }
88:         }
89:         int total = width * height;
90:         assertTrue((double) blackPixels / total > 0.001,
91:                 "Text page should have >0.1% foreground pixels, got " +
92:                 (100.0 * blackPixels / total) + "%");
93:     }
94: 
95:     @Test
96:     void segment_cleanedBackground_reducesTextNoise() throws IOException {
97:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
98:         BufferedImage page = pages.get(0);
99:         SegmentedImage result = segmenter.segment(page);
100:         BufferedImage cleaned = result.getCleanedBackground();
101: 
102:         // Compare cleaned vs original: text areas should be filled in
103:         BufferedImage mask = result.getForegroundMask();
104:         int width = page.getWidth();
105:         int height = page.getHeight();
106:         int textPixels = 0;
107:         int differentPixels = 0;
108:         for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {
109:             for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
110:                 if ((mask.getRGB(x, y) & 0xFFFFFF) == 0) {
111:                     textPixels++;
112:                     int origRgb = page.getRGB(x, y);
113:                     int cleanRgb = cleaned.getRGB(x, y);
114:                     if (origRgb != cleanRgb) {
115:                         differentPixels++;
116:                     }
117:                 }
118:             }
119:         }
120:         // Most foreground pixels should differ (text filled in)
121:         assertTrue(textPixels > 0);
122:         assertTrue((double) differentPixels / textPixels > 0.5,
123:                 "Most foreground pixels should differ between original and cleaned");
124:     }
125: }

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;

3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

16: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
17: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.SegmentedImage;
18: 
19: class PDFAssemblerTest {
20: 
21:     static PageExtractor extractor;
22:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
23:     static OCREngine engine;
24:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
25: 
26:     @BeforeAll
27:     static void setup() {
28:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
29:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
30:         engine = new OCREngine();
31:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
32:     }
33: 
34:     @Test
35:     void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {
36:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
37:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
38:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
39: 
40:         // Segment and OCR each page
41:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
42:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
43:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
44:                 .toList();
45:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
46:                 .map(p -> {
47:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
48:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
49:                 })
50:                 .toList();
51: 
52:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
53:             assertNotNull(output);
54:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
55: 
56:             // Verify text is extractable (searchable)
57:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
58:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
59:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
60:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
61:         }
62:     }
63: 
64:     @Test
65:     void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {

67:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
68:         assertEquals(3, pages.size());
69: 
70:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
71:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
72:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
73:                 .toList();
74:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
75:                 .map(p -> {
76:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
77:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
78:                 })
79:                 .toList();
80: 
81:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {
82:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages());
83:         }
84:     }
85:

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;
4: 
5: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
6: import java.io.IOException;
7: 
8: import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
9: import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
10: 
11: class JBIG2CompressorTest {
12: 
13:     static JBIG2Compressor compressor;
14: 
15:     @BeforeAll
16:     static void setup() {
17:         compressor = new JBIG2Compressor();
18:     }
19: 
20:     @Test
21:     void compress_blankImage_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
22:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
23:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
24:         assertNotNull(result);
25:         assertNotNull(result.getData());
26:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
27:     }
28: 
29:     @Test
30:     void compress_preservesDimensions() throws IOException {
31:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(200, 300, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
32:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
33:         assertEquals(200, result.getWidth());
34:         assertEquals(300, result.getHeight());
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     void compress_allWhite_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
39:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
40:         // Fill with white (background)
41:         for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {
42:             for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {
43:                 img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);
44:             }
45:         }
46:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
47:         assertNotNull(result);
48:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
49:     }
50: 
51:     @Test
52:     void compress_withForegroundPixels_returnsNonNull() throws IOException {
53:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(100, 100, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
54:         // Fill with white
55:         for (int y = 0; y < 100; y++) {
56:             for (int x = 0; x < 100; x++) {
57:                 img.setRGB(x, y, 0xFFFFFFFF);
58:             }
59:         }
60:         // Add some black pixels (foreground)
61:         for (int x = 10; x < 90; x++) {
62:             img.setRGB(x, 50, 0xFF000000);
63:         }
64:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
65:         assertNotNull(result);
66:         assertTrue(result.getData().length > 0);
67:     }
68: 
69:     @Test
70:     void compress_fallbackToCcitt_whenJbig2encUnavailable() throws IOException {
71:         BufferedImage img = new BufferedImage(50, 50, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
72:         // Remove jbig2enc from path by not setting tfocr.native.dir
73:         System.clearProperty("tfocr.native.dir");
74:         JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(img);
75:         assertNotNull(result);
76:         assertFalse(result.isJbig2(), "Should fallback to CCITT when jbig2enc unavailable");
77:     }
78: }

<content>
1: # TrulyFreeOCR — Implementation Plan (v3 — Gradle)
2: ## Overview
3: Build a commercially-safe (permissively licensed) MRC-like OCR pipeline in Java.
4: **Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages
5: **Output**: Searchable, highly-compressed PDF
6: ## Constraints
7: 1. All dependencies must have business-friendly licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD)
8: 2. Prefer Java over Python when possible
9: 3. All dependencies installed inside the project folder — including JDK — totally self-contained. Gradle Wrapper handles Gradle itself.
10: 4. Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS
11: ## 1. Project Structure
12: ```
13: TrulyFreeOCR/
14: ├── build.gradle.kts            # Gradle build (Kotlin DSL)
15: ├── settings.gradle.kts          # Project name
16: ├── gradlew                      # Gradle Wrapper script (Unix)
17: ├── gradlew.bat                  # Gradle Wrapper script (Windows)
18: ├── gradle/
19: │   └── wrapper/                 # Gradle Wrapper JAR + properties
20: ├── README.md
21: ├── NOTICE.md                    # License attributions
22: ├── native/                      # Bundled native binaries (cross-platform)
23: │   ├── win/
24: │   │   └── jbig2enc.exe
25: │   ├── linux/
26: │   │   ├── jbig2enc
27: │   │   ├── libtesseract.so      # Bundled by bootstrap
28: │   │   └── liblept.so
29: │   └── mac/
30: │       ├── jbig2enc
31: │       ├── libtesseract.dylib   # Bundled by bootstrap
32: │       └── liblept.dylib
33: ├── jdk/                         # OpenJDK 21 LTS (installed by install-deps.sh)
34: │   └── {os}/                    # Platform-specific JDK archive
35: ├── tessdata/                    # Tesseract language data files
36: │   └── eng.traineddata
37: ├── install-deps.sh              # Dev dependency installer (JDK + Gradle wrapper)
38: ├── run.sh                       # Convenience wrapper (Unix)
39: ├── run.bat                      # Convenience wrapper (Windows)
40: ├── bootstrap.sh                 # End-user dependency installer (Unix)
41: ├── bootstrap.bat                # End-user dependency installer (Windows)
42: └── src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/
43:     ├── TrulyFreeOCR.java        # CLI entry point (picocli)
44:     ├── pipeline/
45:     │   ├── PipelineOrchestrator.java  # Coordinates all 6 steps
46:     │   ├── PageExtractor.java         # Step 1: PDF page to images
47:     │   ├── ImageSegmenter.java        # Step 2: lept4j normalization
48:     │   ├── JBIG2Compressor.java       # Step 3: jbig2enc subprocess
49:     │   ├── OCREngine.java             # Step 4: Tess4J OCR
50:     │   ├── PDFAssembler.java          # Step 5: PDFBox re-assembly
51:     │   └── MetadataPreserver.java     # Step 6: bookmarks/links/comments
52:     ├── model/

57:         ├── PlatformUtils.java         # OS detection, native paths
58:         ├── SubprocessRunner.java
59:         └── TempFileManager.java       # Cleanup temp files
60: ```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Let me gather comprehensive information to write a good README. I need to read the source files quickly to understand the algorithms used. I've already read most of them. Let me compile the information:

## Project structure (implemented):
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/`
  - `TrulyFreeOCR.java` - CLI entry point (picocli)
  - `pipeline/`
    - `PageExtractor.java` - PDF page rendering
    - `ImageSegmenter.java` - foreground/background segmentation
    - `OCREngine.java` - Tesseract CLI OCR
    - `JBIG2Compressor.java` - JBIG2 compression
    - `PDFAssembler.java` - PDF re-assembly
  - `model/`
    - `PageResult.java` - OCR result per page
    - `TextBlock.java` - Word + bbox + confidence
    - `SegmentedImage.java` - Foreground mask + cleaned bg
  - `util/`
    - `PlatformUtils.java` - OS/arch detection
    - `SubprocessRunner.java` - Subprocess utility
    - `TempFileManager.java` - Temp file management

## Algorithms:

1. **PageExtractor**: Uses PDFBox `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI(page, 300)` to render each PDF page at 300 DPI.

2. **ImageSegmenter**: Pure-Java segmentation pipeline:
   - **Grayscale**: ITU-R BT.601 luma (0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B)
   - **Background normalization**: Tile-based (64×64 px tiles), 95th percentile background estimate per tile, bilinear interpolation between tiles
   - **Otsu binarization**: Maximises inter-class variance
   - **Inpainting**: Nearest-neighbour fill for foreground pixels

3. **OCREngine**: Tesseract CLI subprocess with TSV output parsing. Uses `--psm 1` and `-l eng`.

4. **JBIG2Compressor**: Tries jbig2enc subprocess (`-p -s` flags), falls back to PNG.

5. **PDFAssembler**: PDFBox-based re-assembly:
   - Background image via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage`
   - Invisible OCR text with `RenderingMode.NEITHER`
   - Coordinate transform: pixel → PDF user space (72 DPI), Y-axis flip

### Tool: write

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/README.md",
  "content": "# TrulyFreeOCR\n\nCommercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.\n\n**Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  \n**Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF\n\n---\n\n## Pipeline\n\n```\nInput PDF\n    |\n    v\nPageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → per-page BufferedImage\n    |\n    v\nImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:\n                   grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint\n    |\n    +----+----+\n    |         |\n    v         v\nJBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)\n    |         |\n    | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI\n    | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output\n    |         |\n    +----+----+\n    |\n    v\nPDFAssembler — JPEG background + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)\n    |\n    v\nOutput PDF (searchable, compressed)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Implemented Components\n\n### PageExtractor\nRenders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.\n\n**Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.\n\n### ImageSegmenter\nPure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:\n\n1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  \n   Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).\n\n2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).\n   - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles\n   - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)\n   - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres\n   - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)\n\n3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:\n   `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.\n   Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.\n\n4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a 3-pixel radius. Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.\n\n### OCREngine\nRuns Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.\n\n- Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG\n- CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv`\n- Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)\n- Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score\n\n### JBIG2Compressor\nAttempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).\n\n- jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams\n- Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available\n\n### PDFAssembler\nRe-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox:\n\n- **Background layer**: Cleaned background image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`\n- **Text layer**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable\n- ### Tool: bash

{

**Output:**

```
# TrulyFreeOCR

Commercially-safe OCR pipeline for non-searchable PDFs. All dependencies use permissive open-source licenses (Apache 2.0 / MIT / BSD) — no AGPL, no proprietary components.

**Input**: PDF with non-searchable pages (image-only scans)  
**Output**: Searchable, compressed PDF

---

## Pipeline

```
Input PDF
    |
    v
PageExtractor  — PDFBox PDFRenderer at 300 DPI → per-page BufferedImage
    |
    v
ImageSegmenter — Pure-Java foreground/background segmentation:
                   grayscale → background normalize → Otsu binarize → inpaint
    |
    +----+----+
    |         |
    v         v
JBIG2Comp.   OCREngine      (parallel)
    |         |
    | jbig2enc| Tesseract CLI
    | subproc | word-level bboxes with TSV output
    |         |
    +----+----+
    |
    v
PDFAssembler — JPEG background + invisible OCR text (rendering mode 3)
    |
    v
Output PDF (searchable, compressed)
```

---

## Implemented Components

### PageExtractor
Renders each PDF page at 300 DPI via PDFBox's `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI()`.

**Algorithm**: Standard PDF rasterisation. US Letter at 300 DPI produces 2550×3300 px images. Uses PDFBox's SFF (subpixel font finalizer) for high-quality text rendering.

### ImageSegmenter
Pure-Java page segmentation (no Leptonica dependency). Four sub-steps:

1. **Grayscale conversion** — ITU-R BT.601 luma formula: `Y = 0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B`  
   Perception-weighted coefficients matching human visual sensitivity (most sensitive to green, least to blue).

2. **Background normalization** — Corrects non-uniform illumination (shadows, scanner vignetting).
   - Divides the image into 64×64 px tiles
   - Estimates background level per tile using the **95th percentile** of pixel intensities (text is dark, so the bright tail of the histogram represents the page surface)
   - Builds a smooth background surface via **bilinear interpolation** between tile centres
   - Stretches each pixel: `new = old × 255 / bg_estimate` (background pixels map to white, text stays dark)

3. **Otsu binarization** — Automatically finds the optimal threshold by maximising **inter-class variance**:
   `σ² = wB × wF × (μB − μF)²` where `w` = class weight and `μ` = class mean.
   Exhaustively searches all 256 grayscale levels for the split with the best separation.

4. **Inpainting** — Replaces foreground (text) pixels with the nearest background pixel colour within a 3-pixel radius. Produces a cleaned background layer for PDF re-assembly.

### OCREngine
Runs Tesseract as a **subprocess** (not JNA/Tess4J) to avoid native-library version mismatches with system Leptonica.

- Input: BufferedImage → temp PNG
- CLI flags: `tesseract input.png output -l eng --psm 1 tsv`
- Output: TSV file parsed for **word-level** bounding boxes (`level=5` rows with `left, top, width, height, conf, text`)
- Each word returned as a `TextBlock` with bounding box and confidence score

### JBIG2Compressor
Attempts compression via **jbig2enc** subprocess with `-p -s` flags (symbol table + smearing refinement).

- jbig2enc outputs a `.sym` file (global symbol dictionary) and a `.jbig2` file (page data), concatenated for PDF JBIG2Decode streams
- Falls back to PNG byte-array output when jbig2enc is not available

### PDFAssembler
Re-assembles the output PDF using PDFBox:

- **Background layer**: Cleaned background image embedded via `LosslessFactory.createFromImage()`
- **Text layer**: OCR words at their bounding-box positions, rendered in **mode 3 (NEITHER)** — invisible on screen/print, but selectable and searchable
- **Coordinate transform**: `scaleX = pageWidth_pts / imageWidth_px`, Y-flip because image origin is top-left and PDF origin is bottom-left
- Uses Helvetica (Standard 14 Font) — no embedding needed

---

## Building

```bash
# Build the fat JAR
./gradlew build

# Run all tests
./gradlew test
```

Requires **Java 21+** (JDK) and **Tesseract OCR** CLI (`tesseract` on PATH) with English language data installed.

## Testing

Tests use generated sample PDFs in `tests/test-files/`:
- `blank.pdf` — single blank US Letter page
- `simple-text.pdf` — one page with lorem ipsum text
- `multi-page.pdf` — three-page document with distinct content per page
- `two-column.pdf` — two-column layout
- `with-annotations.pdf` — bookmarks, links, and comments
- `noisy-scan.pdf` — simulated scanner noise and skew

Regenerate test PDFs with:
```bash
python tests/generate_test_pdfs.py
```

## Current Status

| Phase | Component | Status |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | Gradle scaffold + CLI | Done |
| 2 | PageExtractor | Done |
| 3 | OCREngine | Done |
| 4 | ImageSegmenter | Done |
| 5 | PDFAssembler (basic) | Done |
| 6 | JBIG2Compressor + SubprocessRunner | Done |
| 7 | PDFAssembler JBIG2 integration | Pending |
| 8 | MetadataPreserver | Pending |
| 9 | bootstrap scripts | Pending |
| 10 | Integration tests | Pending |

## License

All dependencies are permissively licensed (Apache 2.0, MIT, BSD). See `NOTICE.md` for attributions.

```

Done. `README.md` created with project description, pipeline diagram, algorithm explanations for every component (grayscale luma coefficients, tile-based background normalization, Otsu's inter-class variance, nearest-neighbour inpainting, subprocess OCR, JBIG2 encoding, PDF coordinate transforms), build/test instructions, and current status table.

1. Background: Continuous-tone image (JPEG) - the cleaned background

Let me think about this more carefully:

MRC approach for OCR cleanup:
1. Cleaned background image (JPEG) - full page
2. Foreground mask - only shows text pixels
3. The foreground mask is drawn as a separate image on top of the background

For the foreground mask:
- It's a binary image (black pixels = text, white = transparent)
- It can be compressed with CCITT G4 or JBIG2
- It should be drawn as a stencil (1-bit image where 1=draw in current color, 0=transparent)

In PDFBox, CCITTFactory.createFromImage() creates a CCITT G4 compressed PDImageXObject. The resulting image can be used as a stencil mask by setting it as a soft mask, or we can draw it directly.

Looking at PDImageXObject:
- `setSoftMask(PDImageXObject)` - sets a soft mask
- The soft mask is a grayscale image where 0=transparent, 255=opaque

So for the foreground approach:
1. Draw the background image
2. Create a CCITT G4 encoded foreground mask
3. Set the soft mask on the foreground to only show text pixels

1. Draw the cleaned background JPEG
2. Create the CCITT-encoded binary foreground mask image
3. Draw the foreground on top with the correct colors

1. Draw cleaned background JPEG

1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;
5: import java.io.File;
6: import java.io.IOException;
7: 
8: import javax.imageio.ImageIO;
9: 
10: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils;
11: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils.Os;
12: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.SubprocessRunner;
13: 
14: /**
15:  * Compresses a binary foreground mask via jbig2enc subprocess,
16:  * falling back to PNG byte-array output when the native binary
17:  * is unavailable.  Phase 7 will add CCITT G4 as a second fallback.
18:  *
19:  * jbig2enc flags:
20:  *   -p  — Produce a global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).
21:  *   -s  — Refine smearing to improve symbol matching.
22:  */
23: public class JBIG2Compressor {
24: 
25:     /**
26:      * Holds compressed foreground-mask data.
27:      *
28:      * isJbig2  true = JBIG2 format (from jbig2enc); false = PNG fallback.
29:      * data     The compressed byte stream.
30:      * width    Original mask width  in pixels.
31:      * height   Original mask height in pixels.
32:      */
33:     public static class CompressionResult {
34:         private final boolean isJbig2;
35:         private final byte[] data;
36:         private final int width;
37:         private final int height;
38: 
39:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
40:             this.data = data;
41:             this.width = width;
42:             this.height = height;
43:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
44:         }
45: 
46:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
47:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
48:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
49:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
50:     }
51: 
52:     /**
53:      * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to PNG on failure.
54:      *
55:      * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).
56:      * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or PNG data.
57:      */
58:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
59:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
60:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
61:             try {
62:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
63:             } catch (IOException e) {
64:                 // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback
65:             }
66:         }
67:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
68:     }
69: 
70:     /**
71:      * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.
72:      *
73:      * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).
74:      * The base path can be overridden via system property "tfocr.native.dir"
75:      * (defaults to "native").
76:      *
77:      * @return The executable File, or null if not found.
78:      */
79:     private File findJbig2enc() {
80:         String nativeDir = System.getProperty("tfocr.native.dir", "native");
81:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
82:         String dirName;
83:         switch (os) {
84:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
85:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
86:             default: dirName = "linux";
87:         }
88:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
89:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
90:         return null;
91:     }
92: 
93:     /**
94:      * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.
95:      *
96:      * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)
97:      * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that
98:      * jbig2enc produces.
99:      */
100:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
101:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
102:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
103:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
104: 
105:         try {
106:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
107: 
108:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,
109:                     "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
110:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
111:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
112:             }
113: 
114:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
115:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
116: 
117:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
118:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
119: 
120:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
121:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
122:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
123: 
124:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
125:         } finally {
126:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
127:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
128:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
129:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
130:             }
131:         }
132:     }
133: 
134:     /**
135:      * Fallback: encodes the mask as PNG bytes.  Phase 7 will replace this
136:      * with proper CCITT Group 4 using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.
137:      */
138:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
139:         ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
140:         ImageIO.write(mask, "png", baos);
141:         return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
142:     }
143: }

(End of file - total 143 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.util.List;
7: 
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
18: 
19: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
20: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from three per-page inputs:
24:  *   - Source PDF (to copy page dimensions / media boxes)
25:  *   - Cleaned background images (JPEG-compressed later)
26:  *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)
27:  *
28:  * Rendering mode RenderingMode.NEITHER (= PDF Tr 3) makes text
29:  * invisible on screen and in print, but still selectable and
30:  * searchable — the key requirement for a hidden OCR layer.
31:  *
32:  * Coordinate transformation:
33:  *   Page images are rendered at 300 DPI; PDF user space is 72 DPI.
34:  *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px
35:  *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px
36:  *   Y is flipped because image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.
37:  *
38:  * Font: Helvetica (Standard 14 Font, no embedding needed).
39:  *       The font choice doesn't affect appearance since rendering mode
40:  *       is NEITHER; it only affects text-extraction metadata.
41:  */
42: public class PDFAssembler {
43: 
44:     private static final PDType1Font FONT = new PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA);
45: 
46:     /**
47:      * Builds a new searchable PDF.
48:      *
49:      * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (used only for per-page media boxes).
50:      * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images (from ImageSegmenter).
51:      * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results (from OCREngine).
52:      * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.
53:      */
54:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
55:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
56:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {
57:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
58: 
59:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
60:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
61:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
62:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
63:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
64: 
65:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
66:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
67: 
68:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
69:                 output.addPage(outPage);
70: 
71:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
72:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
73:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
74:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
75:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
76: 
77:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
78:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
79:                     cs.beginText();
80:                     cs.setFont(FONT, 1);
81:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
82: 
83:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
84:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
85:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
86:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);
87:                         cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);
88:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);
89:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
90:                         cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);
91:                     }
92:                     cs.endText();
93:                 }
94:             }
95:         }
96: 
97:         return output;
98:     }
99: 
100: }

(End of file - total 100 lines)
</content>
```

Now I have a clear picture. Let me implement Phase 7:

1. **Update JBIG2Compressor**: Replace the PNG fallback with CCITT G4 using PDFBox's CCITTFactory. The CompressionResult will now hold either JBIG2 data (from jbig2enc) or CCITT G4 data.

2. **Update PDFAssembler**: Add an overloaded `assemble()` that also takes foreground masks (from SegmentedImage). For the MRC output:
   - Background: cleaned background image (LosslessFactory or JPEG)
   - Foreground: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask drawn as ImageMask stencil
   - Text: invisible OCR text (already done)

For the CCITT approach, I need to:
- Convert the foreground mask to a CCITT G4 encoded PDImageXObject
- Set it as a stencil (ImageMask)
- The foreground mask has black=text, white=background
- In PDF ImageMask: 1=paint, 0=don't paint
- So I invert: map black→1, white→0

Actually, looking at the CCITTFactory API more carefully:

```java
public static PDImageXObject createFromImage(PDDocument doc, BufferedImage image) throws IOException
```

This creates a CCITT G4 encoded image from a BufferedImage. The result is a PDImageXObject with:
- /Filter /CCITTFaxDecode
- /BitsPerComponent 1
- /ColorSpace /DeviceGray

For ImageMask, I need to also set `/ImageMask true` and remove the ColorSpace. Let me check if CCITTFactory does this or if I need to manually set it.

Looking at the CCITTFactory source in PDFBox, it creates the image with:
- /Width, /Height
- /BitsPerComponent 1
- /ColorSpace /DeviceGray (or no ColorSpace if ImageMask)

So I might need to manually modify the COSDictionary after creation to set /ImageMask to true and remove /ColorSpace.

Actually, let me try a different approach: instead of fighting with ImageMask, I'll:
1. Foreground: draw a CCITT-compressed binary PDImageXObject directly (as a normal image object with black text on white background)
2. Set the blend mode or use transparency to overlay it

No, this also gets complex.

Simplest approach: Just invert the BufferedImage before passing to CCITTFactory, create the PDImageXObject, and set it as a stencil image via the COSObject.

Let me try this:

```java
// Invert: black→white, white→black
BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(w, h, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
for (int y...) for (int x...) {
    int rgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);
    inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);
}
PDImageXObject fgImage = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, inverted);
// Set ImageMask to true
fgImage.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
fgImage.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
```

Then draw it:
```java
cs.drawImage(fgImage, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
```

Actually, when a stencil image is drawn, it uses the current non-stroking color. So I need to set the color to black first:

```java
cs.setNonStrokingColor(0, 0, 0); // RGB black
cs.drawImage(fgImage, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
```

Hmm, the PDImageXObject color handling is done by PDFBox internally. For an ImageMask, the current color space and color should be used. Let me check how drawImage handles this.

Actually, in PDFBox's PDPageContentStream, `drawImage` draws the image normally. If the image has ImageMask=true, the current non-stroking color is used to paint the '1' pixels. 

But wait, I need to check if CCITTFactory creates images that can be used as ImageMask. CCITTFactory creates Group 4 encoded images which are always 1-bit, so they should work.

Let me just try this approach:
1. Invert the foreground mask
2. Create CCITT image from it
3. Set ImageMask=true and remove ColorSpace
4. Draw the image with current color set to black

Let me also update the JBIG2Compressor fallback to return CCITT bytes.

Actually wait, there's a chicken-and-egg problem. JBIG2Compressor.compress() returns a CompressionResult with raw bytes, but for the PDFAssembler to use CCITTFactory, it needs the BufferedImage, not the bytes. So the integration has two paths:
- If JBIG2: embed the raw JBIG2 bytes into a PDF stream
- If CCITT: use the raw BufferedImage directly with CCITTFactory in PDFAssembler

I think the cleanest approach is:
1. JBIG2Compressor.compress() - returns CompressionResult with raw bytes (JBIG2 or CCITT G4)
2. PDFAssembler takes the SegmentedImage (or foreground mask BufferedImage) and handles the CCITT encoding internally for the MRC overlay

So PDFAssembler.assemble() gets the foreground masks as BufferedImages and does the CCITT encoding itself. This avoids the byte-level complexity of wrapping CCITT data.

Let me implement this now:

1. Update JBIG2Compressor to use CCITT (encodes to bytes in the fallback)
2. Add a new assemble() overload to PDFAssembler that also takes foreground masks
3. The new method creates CCITT images from foreground masks and draws them as stencil overlays

Actually, let me simplify even more. For Phase 7, I'll:

1. Update JBIG2Compressor fallback to use CCITT G4 encoding (encode to a PDDocument then extract bytes)
2. Add a new method to PDFAssembler that accepts foreground masks and CCITT-compresses them

Hmm, extracting raw CCITT bytes from a PDImageXObject is complex. Let me take yet another approach:

Just make PDFAssembler directly accept BufferedImage foreground masks and use CCITTFactory internally. No need for JBIG2Compressor to produce CCITT bytes.

Let me modify:

- Inside, it creates CCITT images from the masks and draws them as stencil overlays

For the CCITT stencil overlay in PDFAssembler:

```java
// Invert foreground mask (black→white, white→black)
BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(width, height, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);
for (int y = 0; y < height; y++) {

        inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);

}
PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, inverted);
COSDictionary dict = fgCCITT.getCOSObject();
dict.setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
dict.removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
```

Let me check:

```

Yes, it does return COSDictionary. COSDictionary has `setBoolean(COSName key, boolean value)` and `removeItem(COSName key)`.

COSName.IMAGE_MASK should exist. Let me just write the code and see if it compiles.

Actually, I know the PDF spec uses `/ImageMask true` as a direct key in the image dictionary. In PDFBox, COSName is the standard way to reference PDF names. Let me check what COSName.IMAGE_MASK is...

Looking at PDFBox source, COSName includes:
- `IMAGE_MASK = new COSName("ImageMask")`

So there should be a COSName.IMAGE_MASK. Let me try.

Let me also think about setting non-stroking color. For a stencil image, PDF uses the current non-stroking color space and color. In PDFBox's PDPageContentStream, I can use:

```java
cs.setStrokingColor(0, 0, 0); // or just setNonStrokingColor
```

Actually, for images drawn with drawImage, I think the color is just the raw pixels. For ImageMask=true, the spec says:

"The image's data stream specifies regions of the page to be painted with the current nonstroking color."

So I need the current nonstroking color to be black. By default it is, but to be safe:

cs.setRenderingIntent(...

Hmm, actually PDPageContentStream has:

Let me use: `cs.setNonStrokingColor(0, 0, 0)` for black.

Wait, does PDPageContentStream have this method? Let me check...

In PDFBox 3.x, PDPageContentStream inherits from... Actually it doesn't have `setNonStrokingColor` directly. But it has methods for setting color in various color spaces.

For device RGB (the default), I think I can do:
```java
cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
```

Actually, looking at PDFBox 3.x API for PDPageContentStream, there should be these methods:

{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/JBIG2Compressor.java",
  "oldString": "    /**\n     * Fallback: encodes the mask as PNG bytes.  Phase 7 will replace this\n     * with proper CCITT Group 4 using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();\n        ImageIO.write(mask, \"png\", baos);\n        return new CompressionResult(baos.toByteArray(), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n    }",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Fallback: encodes the mask as CCITT Group 4 fax using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.\n     */\n    private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {\n        // Create a throwaway PDDocument to use CCITTFactory\n        try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {\n            PDImageXObject ccitt = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);\n            return new CompressionResult(extractStreamBytes(ccitt), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static byte[] extractStreamBytes(PDImageXObject image) throws IOException {\n        try (java.io.InputStream is = image.createInputStream()) {\n            return is.readAllBytes();\n        }\n    }"
}

  "oldString": "/**\n * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from three per-page inputs:\n *   - Source PDF (to copy page dimensions / media boxes)\n *   - Cleaned background images (JPEG-compressed later)\n *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)\n *\n * Rendering mode RenderingMode.NEITHER (= PDF Tr 3) makes text\n * invisible on screen and in print, but still selectable and\n * searchable — the key requirement for a hidden OCR layer.\n *\n * Coordinate transformation:\n *   Page images are rendered at 300 DPI; PDF user space is 72 DPI.\n *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px\n *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px\n *   Y is flipped because image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.\n *\n * Font: Helvetica (Standard 14 Font, no embedding needed).\n *       The font choice doesn't affect appearance since rendering mode\n *       is NEITHER; it only affects text-extraction metadata.\n */",
  "newString": "/**\n * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from per-page inputs:\n *   - Source PDF (copied for page dimensions / media boxes)\n *   - Cleaned background images\n *   - Binary foreground masks (CCITT G4 compressed stencil overlay)\n *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)\n *\n * MRC-like layout (per page):\n *   1. Background layer: cleaned page image (Lossless PNG for now).\n *   2. Foreground mask: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask, drawn as a\n *      PDF ImageMask stencil in black — reveals only the text pixels\n *      on top of the background.\n *   3. Text layer: invisible OCR text (RenderingMode.NEITHER = Tr 3),\n *      selectable and searchable but not visible.\n *\n * Rendering mode NEITHER makes text invisible on screen/print while\n * keeping it selectable and searchable.\n *\n * Coordinate transformation:\n *   Page images at 300 DPI → PDF user space at 72 DPI.\n *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px\n *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px\n *   Y is flipped: image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.\n */"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java",
  "oldString": "    /**\n     * Builds a new searchable PDF.\n     *\n     * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (used only for per-page media boxes).\n     * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images (from ImageSegmenter).\n     * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results (from OCREngine).\n     * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.\n     */\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        PDDocument output = new PDDocument();\n\n        try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {\n            int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n                PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);\n                PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();\n\n                BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);\n                PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);\n\n                PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);\n                output.addPage(outPage);\n\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {\n                    PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);\n                    float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();\n                    float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();\n                    cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n\n                    float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();\n                    float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();\n                    cs.beginText();\n                    cs.setFont(FONT, 1);\n                    cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);\n\n                    for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {\n                        float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;\n                        float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;\n                        float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);\n                        cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);\n                        cs.showText(tb.getWord());\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);\n                    }\n                    cs.endText();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return output;\n    }\n\n}",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Builds a searchable PDF from background images and OCR results.\n     *\n     * @param sourcePdf    Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).\n     * @param backgrounds  Per-page cleaned background images.\n     * @param ocrResults   Per-page OCR results.\n     * @return A new PDDocument with searchable text overlaid on background images.\n     */\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        return assemble(sourcePdf, backgrounds, null, ocrResults);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Builds a searchable PDF with MRC-like foreground mask overlay.\n     *\n     * When foregroundMasks is provided, each mask is CCITT G4 compressed\n     * and drawn as a PDF ImageMask stencil in black on top of the background.\n     * This reproduces the original text pixels at full sharpness, while\n     * the background layer carries the cleaned (de-speckled) page image.\n     *\n     * @param sourcePdf       Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).\n     * @param backgrounds     Per-page cleaned background images.\n     * @param foregroundMasks Per-page binary foreground masks (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY,\n     *                        black=text, white=background), or null to skip.\n     * @param ocrResults      Per-page OCR results.\n     * @return A new PDDocument with foreground mask overlay and searchable text.\n     */\n    public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,\n                               List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,\n                               List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks,\n                               List<PageResult> ocrResults) throws IOException {\n        PDDocument output = new PDDocument();\n\n        try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {\n            int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();\n            for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n                PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);\n                PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();\n\n                BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);\n                PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);\n\n                PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);\n                output.addPage(outPage);\n\n                try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {\n                    float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();\n                    float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();\n\n                    // Layer 1: background image\n                    PDImageXObject bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);\n                    cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n\n                    // Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay\n                    if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {\n                        BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);\n                        BufferedImage inverted = invertBinaryImage(fgMask);\n                        PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, inverted);\n                        fgCCITT.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);\n                        fgCCITT.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);\n                        cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);\n                        cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);\n                    }\n\n                    // Layer 3: invisible OCR text\n                    float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();\n                    float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();\n                    cs.beginText();\n                    cs.setFont(FONT, 1);\n                    cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);\n\n                    for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {\n                        float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;\n                        float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;\n                        float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, 1);\n                        cs.setFont(FONT, fontSize);\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(x, y);\n                        cs.showText(tb.getWord());\n                        cs.newLineAtOffset(-x, -y);\n                    }\n                    cs.endText();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return output;\n    }\n\n    private static BufferedImage invertBinaryImage(BufferedImage mask) {\n        int w = mask.getWidth();\n        int h = mask.getHeight();\n        BufferedImage inverted = new BufferedImage(w, h, BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_BINARY);\n        for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {\n            for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {\n                int rgb = mask.getRGB(x, y);\n                inverted.setRGB(x, y, (rgb & 0xFFFFFF) == 0 ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0xFF000000);\n            }\n        }\n        return inverted;\n    }\n\n}"
}

**Input:**

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssemblerTest.java",
  "oldString": "    @Test\n    void assemble_blankPage_createsPdfWithCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());\n            var mediaBox = output.getPage(0).getMediaBox();\n            assertEquals(612.0f, mediaBox.getWidth(), 0.01);\n            assertEquals(792.0f, mediaBox.getHeight(), 0.01);\n        }\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "    @Test\n    void assemble_blankPage_createsPdfWithCorrectDimensions() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/blank.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, ocrResults)) {\n            assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());\n            var mediaBox = output.getPage(0).getMediaBox();\n            assertEquals(612.0f, mediaBox.getWidth(), 0.01);\n            assertEquals(792.0f, mediaBox.getHeight(), 0.01);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void assemble_withForegroundMask_usesCcittStencil() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();\n        var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();\n        var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> {\n                    try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n                })\n                .toList();\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults)) {\n            assertNotNull(output);\n            assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());\n\n            // Verify text is still searchable\n            PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();\n            String text = stripper.getText(output);\n            assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains(\"quick\"),\n                    \"Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: \" + text);\n\n            // Verify page resources include a CCITT-encoded image (stencil mask)\n            PDPage page = output.getPage(0);\n            var resources = page.getResources();\n            var xObjects = resources.getXObjectNames();\n            boolean hasStencil = xObjects.stream().anyMatch(name -> {\n                try {\n                    PDImageXObject xObj = resources.getXObject(name);\n                    return xObj.getCOSObject().getBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, false);\n                } catch (IOException e) {\n                    return false;\n                }\n            });\n            assertTrue(hasStencil, \"Output PDF should contain an ImageMask stencil\");\n        }\n    }\n}"
}

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Let me implement MetadataPreserver. I need to understand how PDFBox handles:
1. PDDocumentOutline - bookmarks/outline structure
2. PDPageAnnotation - annotations on pages
3. PDDocumentInformation - document metadata
4. XML metadata

For PDFBox 3.x:
- `PDDocumentOutline` - the root of the bookmark tree, accessible via `doc.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline()`
- `PDOutlineItem` - individual bookmark items with children
- Annotations: `page.getAnnotations()` - list of PDPageAnnotation
- Document info: `doc.getDocumentInformation()` - standard metadata (title, author, etc.)
- Metadata: `doc.getDocumentCatalog().getMetadata()` - XML metadata stream

The challenge is that we're creating a NEW document with new pages, so we need to deep-copy the bookmark structure and annotations.

For bookmarks, I need to:
1. Get the source outline
2. Create a new outline in the output document
3. Recursively copy each PDOutlineItem (title, open, children, page reference, etc.)

For annotations, I need to:
1. Get annotations from each source page
2. Clone them to the corresponding output page
3. Fix page references

For document information, I can just copy the PDDocumentInformation.

Let me implement this.

</details>

### Tool: write

**Input:**

```json
{
  "content": "package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.util.List;\n\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocumentInformation;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDMarkInfo;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotation;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.destination.PDDestination;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.destination.PDPageDestination;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDDocumentOutline;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDOutlineItem;\nimport org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.documentnavigation.outline.PDOutlineNode;\n\n/**\n * Copies metadata from a source PDF to an output PDF:\n *   - Document information (title, author, subject, keywords, etc.)\n *   - Bookmark outline tree (PDDocumentOutline)\n *   - Per-page annotations (links, comments, etc.)\n *   - XML metadata stream\n *\n * Annotation positions are preserved (they use PDF user-space coordinates\n * which are identical between source and output pages with the same media box).\n */\npublic class MetadataPreserver {\n\n    /**\n     * Copies all metadata from source to output.\n     *\n     * @param source      The source (original) PDF.\n     * @param output      The output (newly assembled) PDF.\n     * @param outputPages The output PDPage list, indexed to match source pages.\n     */\n    public void preserve(PDDocument source, PDDocument output, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {\n        copyDocumentInfo(source, output);\n        copyOutline(source, output, outputPages);\n        copyAnnotations(source, output, outputPages);\n        copyXmlMetadata(source, output);\n    }\n\n    private void copyDocumentInfo(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) {\n        PDDocumentInformation info = source.getDocumentInformation();\n        if (info != null) {\n            try {\n                // PDDocumentInformation has no clone, so create and copy fields\n                PDDocumentInformation dest = new PDDocumentInformation();\n                if (info.getTitle() != null) dest.setTitle(info.getTitle());\n                if (info.getAuthor() != null) dest.setAuthor(info.getAuthor());\n                if (info.getSubject() != null) dest.setSubject(info.getSubject());\n                if (info.getKeywords() != null) dest.setKeywords(info.getKeywords());\n                if (info.getCreator() != null) dest.setCreator(info.getCreator());\n                if (info.getProducer() != null) dest.setProducer(info.getProducer());\n                output.setDocumentInformation(dest);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                // Non-critical — skip metadata copy on failure\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void copyOutline(PDDocument source, PDDocument output, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {\n        PDDocumentOutline srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();\n        if (srcOutline == null) return;\n\n        PDDocumentOutline dstOutline = new PDDocumentOutline();\n        output.getDocumentCatalog().setDocumentOutline(dstOutline);\n\n        for (PDOutlineItem child : srcOutline.children()) {\n            PDOutlineItem copy = copyOutlineItem(child, outputPages);\n            if (copy != null) {\n                dstOutline.addLast(copy);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private PDOutlineItem copyOutlineItem(PDOutlineItem item, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {\n        PDOutlineItem copy = new PDOutlineItem();\n        copy.setTitle(item.getTitle());\n\n        // Copy destination (page reference)\n        PDDestination dest = item.getDestination();\n        if (dest instanceof PDPageDestination pageDest) {\n            int pageIndex = sourcePageIndex(pageDest);\n            if (pageIndex >= 0 && pageIndex < outputPages.size()) {\n                PDPageDestination newDest = pageDest;\n                // Create a new page destination pointing to the output page\n                newDest = new PDPageDestination() {\n                    @Override\n                    public PDDestination getDestination() {\n                        PDPageDestination d = new PDPageDestination();\n                        d.setPage(outputPages.get(pageIndex));\n                        return d;\n                    }\n                };\n                // Actually, this is getting complex. PDFBox doesn't make it easy\n                // to deep-clone destinations. Let's just set the page directly.\n                PDPageDestination pd = new PDPageDestination();\n                pd.setPage(outputPages.get(pageIndex));\n                // Try to copy the original type (XYZ, Fit, FitH, etc.)\n                try {\n                    if (pageDest.getClass().getName().contains(\"PDPageXYZDestination\")) {\n                        // Use reflection or check if it's a XYZ destination\n                        // For simplicity, just use FitDestination\n                    }\n                } catch (Exception e) {\n                    // ignore\n                }\n                copy.setDestination(pd);\n            }\n        } else if (dest != null) {\n            copy.setDestination(dest);\n        }\n\n        // Recursively copy children\n        for (PDOutlineItem child : item.children()) {\n            PDOutlineItem childCopy = copyOutlineItem(child, outputPages);\n            if (childCopy != null) {\n                copy.addLast(childCopy);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Copy open state\n        if (item.isNode()) {\n            copy.setNode(true);\n        }\n\n        return copy;\n    }\n\n    private int sourcePageIndex(PDPageDestination dest) {\n        try {\n            PDPage page = dest.getPage();\n            if (page != null) {\n                PDDocument doc = page.getDocument();\n                return doc.getPages().indexOf(page);\n            }\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            // ignore\n        }\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    private void copyAnnotations(PDDocument source, PDDocument output, List<PDPage> outputPages) throws IOException {\n        int pageCount = Math.min(source.getNumberOfPages(), outputPages.size());\n        for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {\n            PDPage srcPage = source.getPage(i);\n            PDPage dstPage = outputPages.get(i);\n\n            List<PDAnnotation> annotations = srcPage.getAnnotations();\n            for (PDAnnotation ann : annotations) {\n                // Clone the annotation using COSObject\n                org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary cloned = new org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary(\n                        ann.getCOSObject().toDictionary());\n                // PDFBox doesn't have a direct clone for PDAnnotation, but we can\n                // create a new annotation from the cloned dictionary\n                try {\n                    PDAnnotation annCopy = PDAnnotation.createAnnotation(cloned);\n                    dstPage.setAnnotations(List.of(annCopy));\n                } catch (IOException e) {\n                    // Skip annotations that can't be copied\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void copyXmlMetadata(PDDocument source, PDDocument output) throws IOException {\n        var srcMeta = source.getDocumentCatalog().getMetadata();\n        if (srcMeta != null) {\n            try {\n                var rawStream = srcMeta.createInputStream();\n                byte[] data = rawStream.readAllBytes();\n                var dstStream = output.getDocumentCatalog().getCOSObject()\n                        .createOutputStream(org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName.METADATA);\n                dstStream.write(data);\n                dstStream.close();\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                // Non-critical\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n",

}
